В» ЛҙО– ЛёП„ ЛҙОҙ ЛҙО‘ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ Пҹ ЛҙОҫ Л¶О‘ ОҺ ЛҙП— ОҺЛҙ Пҹ НүПў ЛөП¬ ЛёП  Пҹ ОҚ Л¬Лө ПӘНҠ П Лө Пӣ ЛөОӘ ЛёПӨ ЛҙОӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҡ ЛҙПҹ Нү ПўЛө П¬Лё П ПҹОҚ ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ Л¬Лҙ О–Лё П Лҙ П Лё ОҝЛҙ Оғ ЛёПҰ ЛҙПӨ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙПұ Л¶О© ОҺ ЛҙП« ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ Л¬Лҙ О–Лё ПҖЛҙ О’Лҙ П— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ Пҹ ЛҙПӮ Л¶Оі ОҺ ЛҙО‘ ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ЛҙО– ЛёПҢ ЛҙПЁ ЛҙПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ Пҹ ЛҙПІ Л¶П„ ЛёПҢ ЛөПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ Л¬Лҙ О–Лё ПіЛҙ ОӘЛҙ П« ЛёПҰ ЛҙПӨ Л¶Пҹ НүПһ Л¶ПҖ ЛөПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ Л¬Лҙ О•Лё ОӘЛҙ ПӢОҺЛҙ О‘ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ Пҹ ЛҙОҸ НӢО® ЛҙПҳ ЛөПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ Л¬Лҙ О–Лё ПҙЛҙ П„Лё ПӢЛҙ Оғ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ Пҹ ЛҙПҠ Л¶П§ ОҺ ЛҙПЈ ЛҙО– ЛёО‘ НүО® ЛҙП— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ Пҹ ЛҙОӘ Л¶ПӢ ОҺ ЛҙО’ ЛөПЈ .ЛҙПӣ ЛҙО® ЛҙО‘ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛёПӮ ЛөОҙ ЛёО‘ ОҚ Нү ПўЛө П¬Лё П ПҹОҚ ЛҙПҡ Л¶О— ОҺ ЛҙПҡ ЛөПҹ ЛҙО„ ЛёОі ЛҙОғ ПІНӢ П§Л¶ ОҮ Нү ПўЛө П¬Лё П ПҹОҚ Л¬Лҙ ПҡЛ¶ П—Лё ОҜЛ¶ ОӯЛҙ Пӯ ЛҙПҡ Л¶П  ЛёПҖ ЛҙП“ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙПҡ Л¶Оҳ ЛҙПӨ ЛёОЈ ЛҙОӯ ЛҙПӯ ЛөПқ Пӯ ЛөО° ЛҙПі ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ЛөПқ П® ЛөОӨ ЛҙПі ОҺЛҙ Пҹ ПұЛ¶ О¬Нү ПҹОҚ ЛҙПў Пҙ Л¶Пҳ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Лҙ ПўПҙЛ¶ ПҢНү ПЁПҹОҚ . ПІНӢ П§Л¶ ОҮ Нү ПўЛө П¬Лё П ПҹОҚ Л¶П‘ ЛёП® ЛҙОЁ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі ЛҙПҰ ЛёПЈ ЛҙО„ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Л¶О” ЛҙП  ЛёПҙ ЛҙПҢ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі Лҙ ПўПҙЛ¶ ПҢНү ПЁПҹОҚ ЛҙПҡ ЛөПҹ ЛҙО„ ЛёОі ЛҙОғ .

НӢО® ЛҙО· ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛҙПҡ Л¶О‘ ЛІО¬ Л¶ОӢ ОҺ ЛҙПӢ ПІНӢ П§Л¶ ОҮ Нү ПўЛө П¬Лё П ПҹОҚ НӢО® ЛҙО· ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛҙПӯ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛҙ ОҳЛё ПҙЛҙ П„Лё ПӢЛҙ Оғ ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛҙ ОҳЛё ПҢЛҙ ПЁЛҙ ПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПЈ . ПІЛ¶ П“ ЛөПӘ ЛёПЁ НӢПі ЛҙОҜ ЛҙПӯ Лҙ ПҘОҺЛҙ ПӨПіОҲЛё ПҹОҚ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ ЛёОҗ НӢО’ ЛҙОЈ Нү ПўЛө П¬Лё П ПҹОҚ Лҙ ПҘОҺЛҙ ПҙЛё ОјЛ¶ ПҢЛё ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ ЛҙП• П® ЛөОҙ ЛөП” ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙО® ЛёП” ЛөПң ЛёПҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ ЛёП© НӢО® ЛҙПӣ ЛҙПӯ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛ¶ О‘П®Лө П Лө П— ЛҙПҰ Пі Л¶ОӘ Л¶О· ОҚ НүО® Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҰ Л¶ПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё П Лҙ ПҢЛё ОҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ . Лҙ ПҰПҙЛ¶ ПӨЛ¶ П Лё ОҙЛө ПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПЁНү П“Лҙ П®Лҙ О— Нү ПўЛө П¬Лё П ПҹОҚ Л¶П  ЛёОҙ ЛөПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛ¶ ПҙЛё ОЈЛҙ ОғЛҙ Пӯ ОҺ ЛҙПі ОҚ ЛҙО° ЛҙО§ ЛҙО® ЛёПҙ ЛҙПҸ ЛҙПҰ Пҙ Л¶ОӨ Л¶Пҹ ОҺ НүОј Пҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҳЛ¶ ОӨЛё ПҹЛҙ ОғЛҙ Пӯ ЛҙПҰ Пҙ Л¶ПӨ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө Пі ЛҙПҰ Пі Л¶О¬ НүПҹ ОҚ ЛҙО“ ЛҙО® ЛҙП” ЛҙПң ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶Пһ Л¶О— ОҺ ЛҙП— Нү ПўЛө П¬Лё П ПҹОҚ Л¬Лҙ ПҰПҙЛ¶ П§П®Лө ОҳЛё П”Лҙ ПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ЛёПў Л¶П¬ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ ЛёПһ ЛҙПҢ ЛёОҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙПҡ Л¶П  Пҙ Л¶О’ ЛҙОі ЛёПҰ ЛҙПӢ Лҙ ПҘПӯНҠ ОӘЛө ОјЛҙ ПіЛҙ Пӯ ЛҙПҡ ЛҙП  ЛөОі ЛөОӯ ОҚП®Лө О—ПӯЛө Оғ ЛҙПҰ Пі Л¶О¬ НүПҹ ОҚ ЛҙО“ ЛҙО® ЛҙП” ЛҙПң ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶Пһ Л¶О— ОҺ ЛҙП— Нү ПўЛө П¬Лё П ПҹОҚ Л¬ ЛҙПҡ ЛҙО‘ ОҚ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӢ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙПҷ ЛҙО° ЛёОҹ Л¶Оӯ ЛёПҹ ОҚП–Лҙ ОӨЛё ПҹОҚ Лҙ ПӘПҹЛ¶ ОҮ ЛҙОҸ ОҺ ЛҙОҳ Л¶ПңВ« (O Allah! Your is the praise. O Allah! There is none who can withhold what You send, or send what You withhold, or guide whom You send astr ay, or misguide whomever You guide, or give what You deprive, or deprive whom You give, or draw closer whom You cast or cast whom You draw closer. O Allah! Bestow on us from Your bl essings, mercy, grace and provisions. O Allah! I ask You for the eternal delight th at never ends or fades away. O Allah! Iask You for provisions on the Day of deprivation and safety on the Day of fear. O Allah! Iseek refuge with You from the evil repercussions of what You have given us and from the evil of what You have deprived us of. O Allah! Make faith dear to us and beatify i t in our hearts and make disbelief, Fusuq and ' Isyan hateful to us, and make us among the rightl y guided. O Allah! Allow us to die as Muslims,

live as Muslims and j oin us with the ranks of the righteous ones, without tasting humiliation or turmoil. O Allah! Fight the disbelievers who deny Your Messengers and hinder others from Your path; send on them Your torment and punishme nt. O Allah! Fight the disbelievers who were given the Scriptures, the True God.)'' An-Nasa' i collected this Hadith in ' Amal Al-Yawm walLaylah. Allah said,

НјЛұ О”Лҙ ПӨЛё ПҢЛ¶ П§Лҙ Пӯ Л¶ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ ЛҙПҰ НӢПЈ ЛұПј ЛёПҖ ЛҙП“ НҪ ((This is) a grace from Allah and His favor.) meani ng, this favor that He has bestowed on you is a favor and a bounty from Him to you,

Нј ЛІПў Пҙ Л¶Пң ЛҙОЈ ЛІПў Пҙ Л¶П  ЛҙПӢ Лө ПӘНү П ПҹОҚЛҙ ПӯНҪ (And Allah is All-Knowing, All- Wise.) All-Knower in those who deserve guidance and those who deserve misguidance, All-Wise in His statemen ts, actions, legislation and the destiny He appoints.

ЛёОҚ П® ЛөОӨ Л¶П  ЛёО» ЛҙО„ ЛҙП“ Лё ОҚП®Лө П Лҙ ОҳЛҙ ОҳЛё П—ОҚ Лҙ ПҰПҙЛ¶ ПЁЛ¶ ПЈЛё ОҶЛө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ ЛҙПҰ Л¶ПЈ Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ОҳЛҙ П”Л¶ ОӢОӮЛҙ Пғ ПҘЛ¶ ОҮЛҙ ПӯНҪ ЛҙО® ЛёО§ ЛөП· ОҚ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ОҺ ЛҙПӨ ЛөП« ОҚ ЛҙОӘ ЛёОЈ Л¶ОҮ ЛёО– ЛҙПҗ ЛҙО‘ ПҘЛ¶ ОҲЛҙ П“ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө П¬Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ О‘ Лё ОҚП®Лө П Л¶ ОҳЛ°Лҙ ПҳЛҙ П“ ПҜ ЛёО• ЛҙЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙП“ ПҘЛ¶ ОҲЛҙ П“ Л¶ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ Л¶О® ЛёПЈ ЛҙОғ П°Лҙ ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ ЛҙЛҜ П° Л¶П” ЛҙО— П°Нү ОҳЛҙ ОЈ П°Л¶ ПҗЛё О’Лҙ О— П°Л¶ ОҳНү ПҹОҚ НҠОҗ Л¶ОӨ ЛөПі ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөП„ Л¶Оҙ ЛёП— ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ Л¶Пқ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҢ ЛёПҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө П¬Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ О‘ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөОӨ Л¶П  ЛёО» ЛҙО„ ЛҙП“ ЛҙПҰ Пҙ Л¶П„ Л¶Оҙ ЛёПҳ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ- ЛҙПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөОӨ Л¶П  ЛёО» ЛҙО„ ЛҙП“ ЛІО“ ЛҙП® ЛёО§ Л¶ОҮ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө ПЁЛ¶ ПЈЛё ОҶЛө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ ОҺЛҙ ПӨНү П§Л¶ ОҮ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ Лё ОҚП®Лө ПҳНү О—ОҚЛҙ Пӯ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛёПі ЛҙП® ЛҙО§ ЛҙОғНј Лҙ ПҘП®Лө ПӨЛҙ ОЈЛё О®Лө О— ЛёПў ЛөПң НүП  ЛҙПҢ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӘ (9. And if two parties among the believers fall to fighting, then make peace between them both. But if one of them outrages against the ot her, then fight you (all) against the one that which outrages till it complies with the command of Allah. Then if it complies, then make reconciliation between them j ustly, and be eq uitable. Verily, Allah loves those who are the equitable.) (10. The believers are but a brotherhoo d. S o make reconciliation between your brothers, and have Taqwa of Alla h that you may receive mercy.)

Making Peace between Disputing Muslims Allah commands making peace between Muslims that fi ght each other,

ЛёОҚ П® ЛөОӨ Л¶П  ЛёО» ЛҙО„ ЛҙП“ Лё ОҚП®Лө П Лҙ ОҳЛҙ ОҳЛё П—ОҚ Лҙ ПҰПҙЛ¶ ПЁЛ¶ ПЈЛё ОҶЛө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ ЛҙПҰ Л¶ПЈ Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ОҳЛҙ П”Л¶ ОӢОӮЛҙ Пғ ПҘЛ¶ ОҮЛҙ ПӯНҪ Нј ОҺ ЛҙПӨ ЛөП¬ ЛҙПЁ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘ (And if two parties among the be lievers fall to fighting, then ma ke peace between them both.) Therefore, Allah calls both o pposing groups among Muslims, believers, although t hey are fighting each other. Al-Bukhari and other schola r s r e l i e d o n t h i s H a d i t h a s e v i d e n c e t h a t committing a sin does not nullify faith, no matter how maj or the sin is. This creed contradicts the creed of the Khawarij sect and those who accepted their idea , such as the Mu'tazilah sect.

Al-Bukhari narrated that Al-Hasan said that Abu Bakrah said that the Messenger of Allah gave a speech on the Minbar while Al-Hasan bin ' Ali wa s with him. He was repeatedly looking at AlHasan and then at the people; then said,

В» Л¶ПӘ Л¶О‘ ЛҙОў Л¶П  ЛёОј ЛөПі ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ П° ЛҙПҹ ОҺ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙО— ЛҙНҝ ОҚ НүПһ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ ЛІОӘ НӢПҙ ЛҙОі ОҚЛҙ О¬П« ПІЛ¶ ПЁЛё О‘ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ПҰ Пҙ Л¶ПӨ Л¶П  ЛёОҙ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҰ Л¶ПЈ Л¶ПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙОҳ ЛҙПӨ Пҙ Л¶ПҲ ЛҙПӢ Л¶ПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙОҳ ЛҙОҢ Л¶П“ ЛҙПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘В« (Verily, this son of mine is a Sayyid (chief or mas ter), and may Allah make peace between two great groups of Muslims through him.) What the Prop het said, occurred. Al-Hasan brought peace between the people of Ash-Sham and ' Iraq, afte r they fought tremendous wars and frightening battles. Allah's statement,

П°Л¶ ОҳНү ПҹОҚ Лё ОҚП®Лө П Л¶ ОҳЛ°Лҙ ПҳЛҙ П“ ПҜЛҙ О®Лё О§Лө П·ОҚ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ОҺ ЛҙПӨ ЛөП« ОҚ ЛҙОӘ ЛёОЈ Л¶ОҮ ЛёО– ЛҙПҗ ЛҙО‘ ПҘЛ¶ ОҲЛҙ П“НҪ НүОҳ ЛҙОЈ П°Л¶ ПҗЛё О’Лҙ О—НјЛ¶ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ Л¶О® ЛёПЈ ЛҙОғ П°Лҙ ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ ЛҙЛҜ П° Л¶П” ЛҙО— П° (But if one of them outrages against the other, then fight you (all) against the one that which outrages till it complies with the command of Allah.) means, until the rebellious group refers to the commands of Allah and His Messenger for j udg ement and they listen to and obey the truth. There is a Hadith in the Sahih in which Anas states that the Messenger of Allah said,

В»ОҺЛұ ПЈП®Лө П Лё ПҲЛҙ ПЈ ЛёПӯ ЛҙОғ ОҺЛұ ПӨЛ¶ ПҹОҺЛҙ ПҮ ЛҙПҷ ОҺ ЛҙО§ ЛҙОғ ЛёО® ЛөОј ЛёП§ ОҚВ« (Help your brother, whether he is an oppresso r or he is oppressed.) "Iasked, ' O Allah's Messenger! It is right that Ihelp him if he is o ppressed, but how should Ihelp him if he is an oppressor' He said,

В»П©ОҺНү ПіЛ¶ ОҮ ЛҙПҷ ЛөО® ЛёОј ЛҙП§ ЛҙПҷ ОҚ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙП“ Л¶Пў ЛёП  НҠПҲ Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛөПӘ ЛөПҢ ЛҙПЁ ЛёПӨ ЛҙО—В« (By preventing him from oppressi ng others; this is how you help him in this case.)'' S a'id bin Jubayr said that tribes of Aws and Khazraj once got in a scuffle using date tree branches and slippers. Allah revealed this honorable Ayah commanding them to make peace between them.

As-Suddi said, "Aman from Al-Ansar, whose name was ' Imran, had a wife called Umm Zayd.

She wanted to visit her family, but her husband prevented her from visiting them by locking her in an upper room. S o, none of her family could vi sit or see her. She sent someone to her family.

They came, took her down from the room an d wanted to take her away. Her husband was absent at the time, so his family called on their p eople. Their cousins came to help prevent the wife from going with her family. Apush and shove s ituation occurred that led to them fighting using slippers. This Ayah was then revealed in their case, and the Messenger of Allah sent someone to bring peace between them, and they both agreed to resort to the decision of Allah the Exalted.'' The statement of Allah the Exalted, next,

НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөП„ Л¶Оҙ ЛёП— ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ Л¶Пқ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҢ ЛёПҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө П¬Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ О‘ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөОӨ Л¶П  ЛёО» ЛҙО„ ЛҙП“ ЛёО• ЛҙЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙП“ ПҘЛ¶ ОҲЛҙ П“НҪ Нј ЛҙПҰ Пҙ Л¶П„ Л¶Оҙ ЛёПҳ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ НҠОҗ Л¶ОӨ ЛөПі ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ (Then if it complies, then make reconciliation between them j ustly, and be equitable. Verily,

Allah loves those who are the equitable.) mean s, be fair in your j udgement regarding the dispute that occurred between them,

Нј ЛҙПҰ Пҙ Л¶П„ Л¶Оҙ ЛёПҳ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ НҠОҗ Л¶ОӨ ЛөПі Лҙ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ НҪ (Verily, Allah loves those who are the equitabl e.) Ibn Abi Hatim recorded, that ' Abdullah bin ' Amr said that the Messenger of Allah said,

В» НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛіОҶ ЛөПҹ ЛёОҶ ЛөПҹ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛҙО® Л¶О‘ ОҺ ЛҙПЁ ЛҙПЈ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ОҺЛҙ ПҙЛё П§НҠ ОӘПҹОҚ ПІЛ¶ П“ ЛҙПҰ Пҙ Л¶П„ Л¶Оҙ ЛёПҳ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ПІЛ¶ П“ ОҚ П® ЛөП„ ЛҙОҙ ЛёП— ЛҙОғ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ О‘ НүПһ ЛҙОҹ ЛҙПӯ НүО° ЛҙПӢ Л¶ ПҰПӨЛё ОЈНү О®ПҹОҚ Л¶Пұ ЛҙОӘ ЛҙПі ЛҙПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘ ОҺЛҙ ПҙЛё П§НҠ ОӘПҹОҚВ« (Verily, those who are equitable in this life, will be on podiums made of pearls before ArRahman, the Exalted and Most Honored, on acco unt of their fairness in this life.) An-Nasa'i

collected this Hadith. Allah's statement,

НјЛІ О“Лҙ П®Лё О§Л¶ ОҮ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө ПЁЛ¶ ПЈЛё ОҶЛө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ ОҺЛҙ ПӨНү П§Л¶ ОҮНҪ (The believers are but a brotherhood.) means, all of them are brothers in Islam. The Messenger of Allah said,

В»ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚПӘЛө ПӨЛ¶ П Лё ОҙЛө Пі ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ЛөПӘ ЛөПӨ Л¶П  ЛёПҲ ЛҙПі ОҺЛҙ Пҹ Л¶Пў Л¶П  ЛёОҙ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ П®Лө О§Лҙ Оғ ЛөПў Л¶П  ЛёОҙВ« (The Muslim is the brother of the Muslim, he is not unj ust with him nor does he forsake him.) In the Sahih,

В» Л¶ПҘ ЛёП® ЛҙПӢ ПІЛ¶ П“ ЛөОӘ ЛёО’ ЛҙПҢ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПӣ ОҺЛҙ ПЈ Л¶ОӘ ЛёО’ ЛҙПҢ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶ПҘ ЛёП® ЛҙПӢ ПІЛ¶ П“ ЛөНҝ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ ПӘ Пҙ Л¶О§ ЛҙОғВ« (Allah helps the servant as long as the servant helps his brother.) Also in the Sahih:

В»ЛөПҡ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПқ ОҺ ЛҙП— Л¶Оҗ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПҗ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶О® ЛёП¬ ЛҙПҲ Л¶О‘ Л¶ПӘ Пҙ Л¶О§ ЛҙО„ Л¶Пҹ ЛөПў Л¶П  ЛёОҙ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ ПӢЛҙ О© ОҚЛҙ О«Л¶ ОҮ :

ПӘЛ¶ П Лё ОңЛ¶ ПӨЛ¶ О‘ ЛҙПҡ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙПҰ Пҙ Л¶ПЈ ОҒВ« (If the Muslim invokes Allah for the benefit of hi s absent brother, the angel says afterwards,

"Amin, and for you the same.'') There are many Hadiths with this meaning, such as, in the Sahih,

В» ЛёПў Л¶П¬ Л¶ПӨ ЛөОЈ ОҚ ЛҙО® ЛҙО— ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў Л¶П« НӢО© ОҚ ЛҙП® ЛҙО— ПІЛ¶ П“ Лҙ ПҰПҙЛ¶ ПЁЛ¶ ПЈЛё ОҶЛө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ ЛөПһ ЛҙОң ЛҙПЈ ЛөПӘ ЛёПЁ Л¶ПЈ П°Лҙ ПңЛҙ ОҳЛё О·ОҚ ОҚЛҙ О«Л¶ ОҮ Л¬Л¶ ОӘЛ¶ ОЈОҚЛҙ П®Лё ПҹОҚ Л¶ОӘ ЛҙОҙ ЛҙО  ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶Пһ ЛҙОң ЛҙПӨ ЛҙПӣ ЛёПў Л¶П¬ Л¶П  ЛөО» ОҚ ЛҙП® ЛҙО— ЛҙПӯ ЛөО® Л¶ОӢ ОҺ ЛҙОі ЛөПӘ ЛҙПҹ П° ЛҙПӢ ОҚ ЛҙОӘ ЛҙО— ЛІП® ЛёПҖ ЛөПӢО®Лҙ П¬Нү ОҙПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ П°Нү ПӨЛө ОӨЛё ПҹОҺЛ¶ О‘ Л¶ОӘ ЛҙОҙ ЛҙО  ЛёПҹ ОҚ В« (The parable of the believers in relation to th e kindness, mercy and compassion they have for each other, is that of the body: when an organ of it falls ill, the rest of the body responds with fever and sleeplessness.) And also in Sahih.

В»ОҺЛұ ПҖЛё ПҢЛҙ О‘ ЛөПӘ ЛөПҖ ЛёПҢ ЛҙО‘ НҠОӘ ЛөОё ЛҙПі Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ПҙЛё ПЁЛө О’Лё ПҹОҺЛҙ Пӣ Л¶ПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛёОҶ ЛөПӨ ЛёП  Л¶Пҹ ЛөПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛёОҶ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚВ« (Abeliever to another believer is like a buildi ng whose different parts enforce each other.) The Prophet then clasped his hands with the fingers interlaced. Allah's statement,

НјЛё ПўЛө ПңЛё ПіЛҙ П®Лҙ О§Лҙ Оғ ЛҙПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөОӨ Л¶П  ЛёО» ЛҙО„ ЛҙП“ НҪ (S o make reconciliation between your brothers, ) re fers to the two groups that fight each other,

НјЛҙ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ Лё ОҚП®Лө ПҳНү О—ОҚЛҙ ПӯНҪ (and have Taqwa of Allah) in all of your affairs,

Нј ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөПӨ ЛҙОЈ ЛёО® ЛөО— ЛёПў ЛөПң НүП  ЛҙПҢ ЛҙПҹ НҪ (that you may receive mercy.) and this is a promise from Allah that He will grant mercy to those who fear and obey Him.

П°Лҙ ОҙЛҙ ПӢ ЛіПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙП— ПҰНӢ ПЈ ЛІПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙП— ЛёО® ЛҙОЁ ЛёОҙ ЛҙПі ЛҙП» Лё ОҚП®Лө ПЁЛҙ ПЈОҚЛҙ ЛҜ ЛҙПҰ Пі Л¶О¬ НүПҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ П¬НҠ ПіЛҙ О„ПіНҪ П°Лҙ ОҙЛҙ ПӢ ЛіЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙОҙ НӢП§ ПҰНӢ ПЈ ЛІЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙОҙ Л¶П§ ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛёПЁ НӢПЈ ЛұОҚ О® ЛёПҙ ЛҙО§ Лё ОҚП®Лө П§П®Лө ПңЛҙ Пі ПҘЛҙ Оғ НүПҰ ЛөПң ЛҙПі ПҘЛҙ Оғ ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛҙОҙ ЛөП” П§ ЛҙОғ Лё ОҚПӯЛө О°Л¶ ПӨЛё П Лҙ О— ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ НүПҰ ЛөП¬ ЛёПЁ НӢПЈ ЛұОҚ О® ЛёПҙ ЛҙО§ Л¶ПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПӨ Пі П» ОҚ ЛҙОӘ ЛёПҢ ЛҙО‘ ЛөП• П® ЛөОҙ ЛөП” ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөПў ЛёОі П» ОҚ ЛҙОІ ЛёОҢ Л¶О‘ Л¶ ОҗЛ°Лҙ ПҳЛё ПҹНҮ П»ОҺЛ¶ О‘ Лё ОҚПӯЛө О°Лҙ О‘ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛҙ О— Нј Лҙ ПҘП®Лө ПӨЛ¶ П Л°Нү ПҲПҹОҚ ЛөПў ЛөП« ЛҙПҡ Л¶ОҢ Л° ЛҙПҹ ЛёПӯ ЛөО„ ЛҙП“ ЛёОҗ ЛөОҳ ЛҙПі ЛёПў НүПҹ ПҰЛҙ ПЈЛҙ Пӯ (11. Oyou who believe! Let not a group scoff at an other group, it may be that the latter are better than the former. Nor let (some) women scoff at other women, it may be that the latter are better than the former. Nor defame yourselves, nor insult one another by nicknames. Evil is the name of wickedness after faith. And whosoever d oes not repent, then such are indeed wrongdoers.)

The Prohibition of mocking and ridiculing One Another Allah the Exalted forbids scoffing at people, which implies humiliating and belittlin g them. In the Sahih, it is recorded that the Messenger of All ah said,

В»ОұОҺНү ПЁПҹОҚ ЛөОә ЛёПӨ ЛҙПҸ ЛҙПӯ НӢП– ЛҙОӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөО® ЛҙП„ ЛҙО‘ ЛөО® ЛёО’ Л¶Пң ЛёПҹ ОҚВ« (Arrogance is refusing the truth and be littling people.) And in another Version В»ОұОҺНү ПЁПҹОҚ ЛөПӮ ЛёПӨ ЛҙПҸВ« (And despising people) It is forbidden to sco ff at and belittle people, for the inj ured party could be more honored and dearer to Allah the Exalt ed than those who ridicule and belittle them. This is why Allah the Exalted said,

П°Лҙ ОҙЛҙ ПӢ ЛіПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙП— ПҰНӢ ПЈ ЛІПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙП— ЛёО® ЛҙОЁ ЛёОҙ ЛҙПі ЛҙП» Лё ОҚП®Лө ПЁЛҙ ПЈОҚЛҙ ЛҜ ЛҙПҰ Пі Л¶О¬ НүПҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ П¬НҠ ПіЛҙ О„ПіНҪ ЛҙОҙ НӢП§ ПҰНӢ ПЈ ЛІЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙОҙ Л¶П§ ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛёПЁ НӢПЈ ЛұОҚ О® ЛёПҙ ЛҙО§ Лё ОҚП®Лө П§П®Лө ПңЛҙ Пі ПҘЛҙ Оғ П°Лҙ ОҙЛҙ ПӢ ЛіЛҜ ОӮ НјНү ПҰЛө П¬Лё ПЁНӢ ПЈ ЛұОҚ О® ЛёПҙ ЛҙО§ НүПҰ ЛөПң ЛҙПі ПҘЛҙ Оғ (Oyou who believe! Let not a group scoff at anot her group, it may be that the latter are better than the former. Nor let (some) women scoff at othe r women, it may be that the latter are better than the former.) thus, stating this proh ibition for men and then women. The statement of Allah the Exalted,

НјЛё ПўЛө ПңЛҙ ОҙЛө П”П§Лҙ Оғ Лё ОҚПӯЛө О°Л¶ ПӨЛё П Лҙ О— ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ НҪ (Nor defame yourselves,) forbids defaming each othe r. He among men who is a slanderer, and a backbiter, is cursed and condemned as Allah states Нј ЛіО“ ЛҙО° ЛҙПӨ НҠПҹ ЛіО“ ЛҙО° ЛҙПӨ ЛөП« НӢПһ ЛөПң НӢПҹ ЛІПһ ЛёПі ЛҙПӯ НҪ (Woe to every Humazah, Lumazah)(104:1) Hamz is defa mation by action, while Lamz is by words. Allah the Exalted and Most Honored said,

Нј Лі ПўПҙЛ¶ ПӨЛҙ ПЁЛ¶ О‘ Л¶ЛҜ ОӮ НүОё НүПЈ ЛіОҜ ОҺ НүПӨ ЛҙП« НҪ (Hammaz (defaming), going about with slander.) (68:11) meaning, he belittles and de fames people, transgressing and spreading slander among them, which is the Lamz that uses words as its tool. Allah's statement here,

НјЛё ПўЛө ПңЛҙ ОҙЛө П”П§Лҙ Оғ Лё ОҚПӯЛө О°Л¶ ПӨЛё П Лҙ О— ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ НҪ (Nor defame yourselves,) j ust as He said in another Ayah,

НјЛё ПўЛө ПңЛҙ ОҙЛө П”П§Лҙ Оғ Лё ОҚП®Лө П Лө ОҳЛё ПҳЛҙ О— ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ НҪ (Nor kill yourselves) (4:29), me aning, nor kill one another. ' Abdullah bin ' Abbas, Muj ahid,

S a'id bin Jubayr, Qatadah and Muqatil bin Hayyan said that the Ayah,

НјЛё ПўЛө ПңЛҙ ОҙЛө П”П§Лҙ Оғ Лё ОҚПӯЛө О°Л¶ ПӨЛё П Лҙ О— ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ НҪ (Nor defame yourselves,) means, none of you should defame each other,' while,

НјЛ¶ ОҗЛ°Лҙ ПҳЛё ПҹНҮ П»ОҺЛ¶ О‘ Лё ОҚПӯЛө О°Лҙ О‘ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛҙ О— ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ НҪ (nor insult one another by nicknames.) means, ' you should not address people by nick names that people dislike.' Imam Ahmad recorded that Abu Jabirah bin Ad-Dahhak said, "This Ayah was revealed about us, Banu Salamah;

НјЛ¶ ОҗЛ°Лҙ ПҳЛё ПҹНҮ П»ОҺЛ¶ О‘ Лё ОҚПӯЛө О°Лҙ О‘ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛҙ О— ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ НҪ (nor insult one another by nicknames.) When the Messenger of Allah migrated to Al-Madinah,

every man among us had two or three nicknames. When the Prophet called a man by one of these nicknames, people would say, ' O Allah's Me ssenger! He hates that nickname.' Then this Ayah,

НјЛ¶ ОҗЛ°Лҙ ПҳЛё ПҹНҮ П»ОҺЛ¶ О‘ Лё ОҚПӯЛө О°Лҙ О‘ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛҙ О— ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ НҪ (nor insult one another by nicknames.) was revealed .'' Abu Dawud also collected this Hadith.

The statement of Allah the Exalted and Most Honored,

НјЛ¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ПӨПіП»ОҚ ЛҙОӘ ЛёПҢ ЛҙО‘ ЛөП• П® ЛөОҙ ЛөП” ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөПў ЛёОі П» ОҚ ЛҙОІ ЛёОҢ Л¶О‘ НҪ (Evil is the name of wickedness after faith), means, the names and descriptions of wickedness are evil; meaning, ' to use the nicknames that were used by the people of Jahiliyyah, after you embraced Islam and understood it,'

НјЛё ОҗЛө ОҳЛҙ Пі ЛёПў НүПҹ ПҰЛҙ ПЈЛҙ ПӯНҪ (And whosoever does not repent,) means, from this sin,

НјЛҙ ПҘП®Лө ПӨЛ¶ П Л°Нү ПҲПҹОҚ ЛөПў ЛөП« ЛҙПҡ Л¶ОҢ Л° ЛҙПҹ ЛёПӯ ЛөО„ ЛҙП“ НҪ (then such are indeed wrongdoers.) ЛҙОҳ ЛёОҹ ОҚ Лё ОҚП®Лө ПЁЛҙ ПЈОҚЛҙ ЛҜ ЛҙПҰ Пі Л¶О¬ НүПҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ П¬НҠ ПіЛҙ О„ПіНҪ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ НӢПҰ НүПҲ Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҰ НӢПЈ ЛұОҚ О® Пҙ Л¶Оң ЛҙПӣ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөО’ Л¶ПЁ ПўЛө ПңЛө ПҖЛё ПҢНү О‘ ОҗЛҙ ОҳЛё ПҗЛҙ Пі ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ Лё ОҚП®Лө ОҙНү ОҙЛҙ О Лҙ О— ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ ЛІПў ЛёОӣ Л¶ОҮ НӢПҰ НүПҲ Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙОҫ ЛёПҢ ЛҙО‘ Лұ ОҺОҳЛё ПҙЛҙ ПЈ Л¶ПӘ Пҙ Л¶О§ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПў ЛёОӨ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПһ ЛөПӣ ЛёО„ ЛҙПі ПҘЛҙ Оғ ЛёПў ЛөПӣ ЛөОӘ ЛҙОЈ ЛҙОғ НҠОҗ Л¶ОӨ ЛөПі ЛҙОғ Лұ ОҺПҖЛё ПҢЛҙ О‘ Нј ЛІПў Пҙ Л¶ОЈ НүОӯ ЛІОҸ ОҚ НүП® ЛҙО— ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ Лё ОҚП®Лө ПҳНү О—ОҚЛҙ Пӯ Лө П©П®Лө ПӨЛө ОҳЛё П«Л¶ О®Лҙ ПңЛҙ П“ (12. Oyou who believe! Avoid much suspicion; indeed some suspicion is sin. And spy not,

neither backbite one another. Would one of you like to eat the flesh of his dead brother You would hate it. And have Taqwa of Allah. Veri ly, Allah is the One Who forgives and accepts repentance, Most Merciful.)

The Prohibition of Unfounded Suspicion Allah the Exalted forbids His faithful servants from being suspicious, which includes having doubts and suspicions about the conduct of one's family, relatives and othe r people in general.

Therefore, Muslims are to avoid suspicion with out foundation. The Leader of the faithful ' Umar bin Al-Khattab said, "Never think ill of the word t hat comes out of your believing brother's mouth, as long as you can find a good excuse for it .'' Malik recorded that Abu Hurayrah, may Allah be pleased with him, said that Allah's Messenger said,

В» ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ Л¬ Л¶Оҡ Пі Л¶ОӘ ЛҙОӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөОҸ ЛҙО¬ ЛёПӣ ЛҙОғ НүПҰ НүПҲ Пҹ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҲ ЛҙП“ НүПҰ НүПҲ Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў ЛөПӣ ОҺ НүПі Л¶ОҮ Л¬ ОҚ Пӯ ЛөОӘ ЛҙОі ОҺ ЛҙОӨ ЛҙО— ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ОҚП®Лө ОҙЛҙ П“ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛҙ О— ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ Л¬ОҚП®Лө ОҙНү ОҙЛҙ ОӨЛҙ О— ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ОҚ П® ЛөОҙ НүОҙ ЛҙО  ЛҙО— Л¶Нҝ ОҚ ЛҙО© ОҺ ЛҙО’ Л¶ПӢ ОҚП®Лө П§П®Лө ПӣЛҙ Пӯ Л¬ОҚПӯЛө О®Лҙ О‘ОҚЛҙ ОӘЛҙ О— ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ОҚП®Лө ПҖЛҙ ПҸОҺЛҙ О’Лҙ О— ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ОҺ ЛұП§ ОҚ ЛҙП® ЛёО§ Л¶ОҮВ« (Beware of suspicion, for suspicion is the worst of false tales; do not spy on one another; do not look for other's faults; do not be j ealous of one another; do not envy one another; do not hate one another; and do not desert (shun) one anothe r. And O Allah's servants! Be brothers!) The Two Sahihs and Abu Dawud recorded this Hadith. Anas said that the Messenger of Allah said,

В» ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ОҚП®Лө ПҖЛҙ ПҸОҺЛҙ О’Лҙ О— ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ОҚПӯЛө О®Лҙ О‘ОҚЛҙ ОӘЛҙ О— ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ОҚП®Лө ПҢЛҙ ПғОҺЛҙ ПҳЛҙ О— ОҺЛҙ Пҹ НҠПһ Л¶ОӨ ЛҙПі ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ Л¬ОҺЛұ П§ОҚЛҙ П®Лё О§Л¶ ОҮ Л¶Нҝ ОҚ ЛҙО© ОҺ ЛҙО’ Л¶ПӢ ОҚП®Лө П§П®Лө ПӣЛҙ Пӯ Л¬ОҚПӯЛө ОӘЛҙ ОіОҺЛҙ ОӨЛҙ О— ПЎОҺНү ПіЛҙ Оғ Л¶О” ЛҙОӣ ОҺ ЛҙП  ЛҙОӣ ЛҙП• ЛёП® ЛҙП“ ЛөП© ОҺ ЛҙО§ ЛҙОғ ЛҙО® ЛөО  ЛёП¬ ЛҙПі ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ ЛіПў Л¶П  ЛёОҙ ЛөПӨ Л¶ПҹВ« (Do not shun each other; do not ignore one another; do not hate one another, and do not envy one another, and be brothers, Oservants of Alla h. No Muslim is allowe d to shun his brother for more than three days.) Muslim and At-Tirmidhi colle cted this Hadith, who considered it Sahih.

Allah said,

Нј ЛёОҚ П® ЛөОҙ НүОҙ ЛҙО  ЛҙО— ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ НҪ (And spy not), on each other. Taj assus, usually harbors ill intentions, and the spy is called a Jasus. As for Tahassus (inquiring ) it is usually done for a good reason. Allah the Exalted and Most Honored said that Prophet Ya'qub said,

ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ Л¶ПӘ Пҙ Л¶О§ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙП’ ЛөОі П® ЛөПі ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ Лё ОҚП®Лө ОҙНү ОҙЛҙ ОӨЛҙ ОҳЛҙ П“ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөО’ ЛҙП« ЛёО« ОҚ НүП° Л¶ПЁ ЛҙО’ Пі НҪ НјЛ¶ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ Л¶ОЎ ЛёПӯ НүОӯ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ Лё ОҚП®Лө ОҙЛҙ Л°Лё ПіОҺЛҙ О— ("Omy sons! Go you and inquire (Tahassasu) ab out Yusuf and his brother, and never give up hope of Allah's mercy.'') (12:87) Both of these ter ms, ' Taj assus' and ' Tahassus' could have evil connotations. In the Sahih it is recorded that the Messenger of Allah said,

В» ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ОҚП®Лө ПҖЛҙ ПҸОҺЛҙ О’Лҙ О— ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ Л¬ОҚП®Лө ОҙНү ОҙЛҙ ОӨЛҙ О— ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ОҚ П® ЛөОҙ НүОҙ ЛҙО  ЛҙО— ОҺЛҙ Пҹ ОҺ ЛұП§ ОҚ ЛҙП® ЛёО§ Л¶ОҮ Л¶Нҝ ОҚ ЛҙО© ОҺ ЛҙО’ Л¶ПӢ ОҚП®Лө П§П®Лө ПӣЛҙ Пӯ Л¬ОҚПӯЛө О®Лҙ О‘ОҚЛҙ ОӘЛҙ О—В« (Neither commit Taj assus nor Tahassus nor hate each other nor commit Tadabur. And be brothers, Oservants of Allah.) Al-Awza'i said, "Taj assus means, to search for something, while Tahassus means, listening to people when they are t alking without their permission, or eavesdropping at their doors. Tadabur refers to shunning each other. '' Ibn Abi Hatim recorded this statement. Allah the Exalted said about backbi ting;

НјЛұ ОҺПҖЛё ПҢЛҙ О‘ ПўЛө ПңЛө ПҖЛё ПҢНү О‘ ОҗЛҙ ОҳЛё ПҗЛҙ Пі ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ НҪ (neither backbite one another), thus prohibitin g it, which was explained in a Hadith collected by Abu Dawud that Abu Hurayrah said, "It was as ked, ' O Allah's Messenger! What is backbiting'

He said,

В»П©Лҙ О®Лё ПңЛҙ Пі ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ О‘ ЛҙПҷ ОҺ ЛҙО§ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПҷ ЛөО® ЛёПӣ Л¶О«В« (Mentioning about your brother in a manner that h e d i s l i k e s .) H e w a s a s k e d , 'W h a t i f m y brother was as Imentioned' He said,

В» ЛёПҰ ЛөПң ЛҙПі ЛёПў ЛҙПҹ ЛёПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛҙПӯ Л¬Лө ПӘЛҙ ОҳЛё О’Лҙ ОҳЛё ПҸОҚ Л¶ОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙП“ ЛөПқ П® ЛөПҳ ЛҙО— ОҺЛҙ ПЈ Л¶ПӘ Пҙ Л¶П“ ЛҙПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПӣ ЛёПҘ Л¶ОҮ ПӘНү ОҳЛҙ П¬Лҙ О‘ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙП“ ЛөПқ П® ЛөПҳ ЛҙО— ОҺЛҙ ПЈ Л¶ПӘ Пҙ Л¶П“В« (If he was as you mentioned, you will have commit ted backbiting. But if he was not as you say about him, you will have falsely accused him.)' ' At-Tirmidhi collected this Hadith and said "Hasan Sahih.'' Backbiting was sternly warned ag ainst, and this is why Allah the Exalted and Most Blessed compared it to eating the flesh of a d ead human being,

Л¶ПӘ Пҙ Л¶О§ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПў ЛёОӨ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПһ ЛөПӣ ЛёО„ ЛҙПі ПҘЛҙ Оғ ЛёПў ЛөПӣ ЛөОӘ ЛҙОЈ ЛҙОғ НҠОҗ Л¶ОӨ ЛөПі ЛҙОғ НҪ Лұ ОҺОҳЛё ПҙЛҙ ПЈ Нј ЛөП© П® ЛөПӨ ЛөОҳ ЛёП« Л¶О® ЛҙПң ЛҙП“ (Would one of you like to eat th e flesh of his dead brother You would hate it.) Jus t as you hate eating the flesh of a dead person , on account of your nature; so hate backbiting, on account of your religion. The latter carries a punishment that is worse than the former. This Ayah seeks to discourage people from backbiting and warns against it. The Prophet used these words to discourage taking back a gift that one gives to som eone,

В»ПӘЛ¶ ОҢЛё ПҙЛҙ П— ПІЛ¶ П“ ЛөПҠ Л¶Оҹ ЛёО® ЛҙПі НүПў ЛөОӣ ЛөЛҜ ПІ Л¶Пҳ ЛҙПі Л¶Оҗ ЛёП  ЛҙПң ЛёПҹ ОҺ ЛҙПӣВ« (He is j ust like the dog that eats its vomit.) after saying,

В»ЛҜЛё П®Нү ОҙПҹОҚ ЛөПһ ЛҙОң ЛҙПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛҙ Пҹ ЛҙОІ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПҹВ« (Ours is not an evil parable.) Using various chains of narration, the Sahihs and Musnads record that the Prophet said during the Farewell Haj j :

В»Лө ПӣЛҙ ЛҜОҺЛҙ ПЈЛ¶ О© НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛІПЎ ОҚ ЛҙО® ЛҙОЈ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛҙОҝ ОҚ ЛҙО® ЛёПӢ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ Лё ПўЛө ПңЛҙ ПҹОҚЛҙ П®Лё ПЈЛҙ ОғЛҙ Пӯ ЛёПў ЛёПў ЛөПӣ Л¶ОӘ ЛҙП  ЛҙО‘ ПІЛ¶ П“ Л¬ОҚЛҙ О¬П« ЛёПў ЛөПӣ Л¶О® ЛёП¬ ЛҙО· ПІЛ¶ П“ Л¬ОҚЛҙ О¬П« ЛёПў ЛөПң Л¶ПЈ ЛёП® ЛҙПі Л¶О” ЛҙПЈ ЛёО® ЛөОӨ ЛҙПӣ ОҚЛҙ О¬П«В« (Verily, your blood, wealth and honor are as sacred among you as the sanctity of this day of yours, in this month of yours, in this town of yours.) Abu Dawud recorded that Abu Hurayrah said that the Messenger of Allah said,

В» ЛөПӘ ЛөОҝ ЛёО® Л¶ПӢ ЛҙПӯ ЛөПӘ ЛөПҹ ОҺ ЛҙПЈ ЛІПЎ ОҚ ЛҙО® ЛҙОЈ Л¶Пў Л¶П  ЛёОҙ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ Л¶Пў Л¶П  ЛёОҙ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ НҠПһ ЛөПӣ ЛөП© ОҺ ЛҙО§ ЛҙОғ ЛҙО® Л¶Пҳ ЛёОӨ ЛҙПі ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ НӢО® НүОё Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛіЛҜ ПҜ Л¶О® ЛёПЈ ОҚ ЛөОҗ ЛёОҙ ЛҙОЈ Л¬Лө ПӘЛө ПЈЛҙ О©Лҙ Пӯ ПўЛ¶ П Лё ОҙЛө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚВ« (All of the Muslim is sacred to the Muslim, his wea lth, honor and his blood. It is evil enough for someone to belittle his Muslim brother.) At-Tirmi dhi collected this Hadith and said "Hasan Gharib.'' Al-Hafiz Abu Ya'la recorded that a cous in of Abu Hurayrah said, "Ma'iz came to the Messenger of Allah and said, ' O Allah's Mess enger! Ihave committed adultery,' and the Messenger turned away from him until Ma'iz repeat ed his statement four times. The fifth time,

the Prophet asked him,

В»Л®Лҙ О–Лё ПҙЛҙ П§Лҙ ОҜВ« (Have you committed adultery Л®) Ma0 iz said, Yes. The Prophet asked,

В»Л®ОҺЛҙ П§НӢ О°ПҹОҚ ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ПұЛ¶ ОӯЛё ОӘЛҙ О—Лҙ ПӯВ« (Do you know what adultery means) Ma'iz said, ' Yes. Ihave illegally done with her what a husband legally does with hi s wife.' The Prophet said,

В»Л®Л¶ ПқЛё П®Лҙ ПҳЛё ПҹОҚ ОҚЛҙ О¬П« П°Лҙ ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ ЛөОӘ Пі Л¶О® ЛөО— ОҺЛҙ ПЈВ« (What do you seek to accomplish by this statement) Ma'iz said, ' Iintend that you pu rify me.'

The Prophet asked,

В» ЛөПһ Пҙ Л¶ПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Лө ОҗПҙЛ¶ ПҗЛҙ Пі ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛҙ Пӣ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лё ПЁЛ¶ ПЈ ЛҙПҡ Л¶Пҹ О« ПІЛ¶ П“ ЛҙПҡ ЛёПЁ Л¶ПЈ ЛҙПҡ Л¶Пҹ О« ЛҙО– ЛёП  ЛҙО§ ЛёО© ЛҙОғ Л®Л¶ О®Лё ОҢЛ¶ О’Лё ПҹОҚ ПІЛ¶ П“ ОҺЛҙ О·НӢ О®ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ Л¶О” ЛҙП  ЛөОӨ ЛёПң ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ПІЛ¶ П“В« (Have you gone into her j ust as the stick goes into the kohl container and the rope goes into the well) Ma'iz said, ' Yes, O Allah's Messenger!' The Prophet commanded that Ma'iz be stoned to death and his order was carried out. The Prophet heard two men saying to one another,

' Have you not seen the man who had Allah cover his secret, but his heart could not let him rest until he was stoned to death, j ust as the dog is stoned' The Prophet continued on and when he passed by the corpse of a donkey, he asked,

В»ЛҙП  ЛөПң ЛҙП“ ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛ¶ О°Лё П§ОҚ Л® ЛІПҘ ОҺ ЛҙП  ЛөП“ ЛҙПӯ ЛІПҘ ОҺ ЛҙП  ЛөП“ ЛҙПҰ ЛёПі ЛҙОғ ОҚЛҙ О¬П« Л¶О” ЛҙП” Пҙ Л¶Оҹ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ОҺ Оӯ ОҺ ЛҙПӨ Л¶ОӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚВ« (Where are so-and-so Dismount and eat from this donkey.) They said, ' May Allah forgive you, O Allah's Messenger! Would anyone eat this meat' The Prophet said;

В» Л¬Лө ПӘЛё ПЁЛ¶ ПЈ ОҺЛұ П Лё ПӣЛҙ Оғ НҠОӘ ЛҙО· ЛҙОғ ОҺЛұ П”Л¶ П§ОҒ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңПҙЛ¶ О§Лҙ Оғ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ОҳЛё П Л¶ П§ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛҙ П“ ПұЛ¶ О¬Нү ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ ЛөОІ Л¶ПӨ ЛҙПҗ ЛёПЁ ЛҙПі Л¶О” НүПЁ ЛҙО  ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶ ОӯОҺЛҙ П¬Лё П§Лҙ Оғ ПІЛ¶ П”Лҙ Пҹ ЛҙПҘ ОҺ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөПӘ НүП§ Л¶ОҮ Л¶П© Л¶ОӘ ЛҙПҙ Л¶О‘ ПІЛ¶ ОҙЛё П”Лҙ П§ ОҺЛҙ П¬ПҙЛ¶ П“В« (The backbiting you committed against your brother is worse as a meal than this meal. By He in Whose Hand is my soul! He is now swimming in the rivers of Paradise.) '' This Hadith has an authentic chain of narration. Imam Ahmad reco rded that Jabir bin ' Abdullah said, "We were with the Messenger of Allah when a rotten odor was carried by the wind. The Messenger of Allah said,

В»ЛөОў Пі Л¶Оӯ Л¶П© Л¶О¬ ЛҙП« Л®Лө ОўПіНӢ О®ПҹОҚ Л¶П© Л¶О¬ П« ОҺЛҙ ПЈ Лҙ ПҘПӯЛө ОӯЛё ОӘЛҙ О—Лҙ Оғ ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөО‘ ОҺ ЛҙОҳ ЛёПҗ ЛҙПі ЛҙПҰ Пі Л¶О¬ НүПҹ ОҚ ОұОҺНү ПЁПҹОҚВ« (Do you know what this odor is It is the odor of th ose who backbite people.)''

Accepted Repentance from Backbiting and Slander Allah the Exalted and Most Honored said,

НјЛҙ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ Лё ОҚП®Лө ПҳНү О—ОҚЛҙ ПӯНҪ (And have Taqwa of Allah), that is, regardin g what He has commanded you and forbidden for you. Fear Him and beware of Him,

НјЛІ ПўПҙЛ¶ ОЈНү Оӯ ЛІОҸ ОҚ НүП® ЛҙО— Лҙ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ НҪ (Verily, Allah is the One Who forgives and ac cepts repentance, Most Merciful.) He forgives those who repent to Him, is merciful with those who go back to Him and trust in Him. The maj ority of scholars have stated that repentance for committing the sin of backbiting is that one refrains from backbiting intending not to repea t it again. There is a difference of opinion if whether feeling remorse is required in this case , and also if one should apologize to those who he has backbitten. Some scholars stated that it is not necessary for one to ask those whom he has backbitten to forgive him, because if they knew what was said about them, they could be hurt more than if they were not told about it. It is better, they said, that one should praise those whom he has backbitten in audiences in which he has committed the act. It is also better if one defends the inj ured party against any furthe r backbiting, as much as one can, as recompense for his earlier backbiting.

П°Лҙ ОңЛё П§Лө ОғЛҙ Пӯ ЛіО® ЛҙПӣ ЛҙО« ПҰНӢ ПЈ Пў ЛөПң Л° ЛҙПЁ ЛёПҳ ЛҙП  ЛҙО§ ОҺНү П§Л¶ ОҮ ЛөОұ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ П¬НҠ ПіЛҙ О„ПіНҪ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛҙПЈ ЛҙО® ЛёПӣ ЛҙОғ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөП“ ЛҙО® Л° ЛҙПҢ ЛҙОҳ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙПһ Л¶ОӢ ОӮ ЛҙО’ ЛҙП— ЛҙПӯ Лұ ОҺО‘П®Лө ПҢЛө О· Лё ПўЛө ПңЛ°Лҙ ПЁЛё П Лҙ ПҢЛҙ ОҹЛҙ Пӯ Л¶П  ЛҙПӢ ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛёПў ЛөПң Л° ЛҙПҳ ЛёО— ЛҙОғ Л¶ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ ЛҙОӘ ПЁ ЛҙПӢНј ЛІ О®ПҙЛ¶ О’Лҙ О§ ЛІПў Пҙ (13. Omankind! We have created you from a male and a female, and made you into nations and tribes that you may know one another. Verily , the most honorable of you with Allah is that (believer) who has At-Taqwa. Verily, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Aware.)

All of Mankind are the Children of ' Adam and Hawwa'

Allah the Exalted declares to mankind that He has created them all from a single person,

' Adam, and from that person He created his mate, Ha wwa'. From their offspring He made nations, comprised of tribe, which include subtribe s of all sizes. It was also said that ' nations refers to non-Arabs, while ' tribes refers to Arab s. Various statements about this were collected in an individual introduction from the book, Al-Inbah, by Abu ' Amr Ibn ' Abdul-Barr, and from the book, Al-Qasad wal-Amam fi Ma'rifah Ansab Al-Ar ab wal-' Aj am. Therefore, all people are the descendants of ' Adam and Hawwa' and sh are this honor equally. The only difference between them is in the religion that revolves around their obedience to Allah the Exalted and their following of His Messenger . After He fo rbade backbiting and belittling other people,

alerting mankind that they are all eq ual in their humanity. Allah said,

ЛёП§ ЛөОғ ЛҙПӯ ЛіО® ЛҙПӣ ЛҙО« ПҰНӢ ПЈ Пў ЛөПң Л° ЛҙПЁ ЛёПҳ ЛҙП  ЛҙО§ ОҺНү П§Л¶ ОҮ ЛөОұ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ П¬НҠ ПіЛҙ О„ПіНҪ П°Лҙ Оң НјЛё ОҚП®Лө П“Лҙ О®Л°Лҙ ПҢЛҙ ОҳЛ¶ Пҹ ЛҙПһ Л¶ОӢ ОӮ ЛҙО’ ЛҙП— ЛҙПӯ Лұ ОҺО‘П®Лө ПҢЛө О· Лё ПўЛө ПңЛ°Лҙ ПЁЛё П Лҙ ПҢЛҙ ОҹЛҙ Пӯ (Omankind! We have created you from a male and a f emale, and made you into nations and tribes that you may know one another.) so that th ey get to know each other by their nation or tribe. Muj ahid said that Allah's statement,

НјЛё ОҚП®Лө П“Лҙ О®Л°Лҙ ПҢЛҙ ОҳЛ¶ ПҹНҪ (that you may know one another.) refers to one' s saying, "S o-and-so the son of so-and-so, from the tribe of so-and-so.'' Sufyan Ath-Thawri said, " The Himyar (who resided in Yemen) dealt with each other according to their provinces, while the Arabs in the Hij az (Western Arabia) dealt with each other according to their tribes.''

Honor is earned through Taqwa of Allah Allah the Exalted said,

Нј ЛёПў ЛөПң Л° ЛҙПҳ ЛёО— ЛҙОғ Л¶ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ ЛҙОӘ ПЁ ЛҙПӢ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛҙПЈ ЛҙО® ЛёПӣ ЛҙОғ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ НҪ (Verily, the most honorable of you with Allah is th at (believer) who has At-Taqwa.) meaning,

' you earn honor with Allah the Exalted on ac count of Taqwa, not family lineage.' There are many Hadiths from the Prophet that support th is meaning. Al-Bukhari narrated that Abu Hurayrah said, "Some people asked the Prophet , ' Who is the most honorable among people' He replied,

В»Пў ЛөП« ОҺ ЛҙПҳ ЛёО— ЛҙОғ Л¶Нҝ ОҚ ЛҙОӘ ЛёПЁ Л¶ПӢ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛөПЈ ЛҙО® ЛёПӣ ЛҙОғВ« (The most honorable among them with Allah is the one who has the most Taqwa.) The y said,

' We did not ask you about this.' He said,

В» Л¶ПҰ ЛёО‘ ОҚ Л¬Л¶ НҝОҚ НӢПІ Л¶О’ ЛҙП§ ЛөПҰ ЛёО‘ ОҚ Л¬Л¶ НҝОҚ НҠПІ Л¶О’ ЛҙП§ ЛөП’ ЛөОі П® ЛөПі Л¶Оұ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ ЛөПЎ ЛҙО® ЛёПӣ ЛҙО„ ЛҙП“ НҝОҚ Л¶Пһ Пҙ Л¶П  ЛҙО§ Л¶ПҰ ЛёО‘ ОҚ Л¶Нҝ ОҚ НӢПІ Л¶О’ ЛҙП§В« (Then the most honorable person is Yusuf, Allah' s Prophet, the son of Allah's Prophet, the son of Allah's Prophet, the son of Allah's Khalil.) The y said, ' We did not ask you about this.' He said,

В»ПІНӢ П§П®Лө ПҹЛҙ О„Лё ОҙЛҙ О— Л¶ОҸ ЛҙО® ЛҙПҢ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶ПҘ Л¶О© ОҺ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙПЈ ЛёПҰ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙП“В« Л® (Then you want to ask me about the Arab lineage) They said, ' Yes.' He said,

В» ОҚЛҙ О«Л¶ ОҮ Л¶ ПЎОҺЛҙ П Лё ОіЛ¶ ОҲЛё ПҹОҚ ПІЛ¶ П“ ЛёПў ЛөПӣ ЛөОӯ ОҺ ЛҙПҙ Л¶О§ Л¶О” НүПҙ Л¶П  Л¶П« ОҺ ЛҙО  ЛёПҹ ОҚ ПІЛ¶ П“ ЛёПў ЛөПӣ ЛөОӯ ОҺ ЛҙПҙ Л¶ОЁ ЛҙП“ ЛөП¬ ЛөПҳ ЛҙП“ОҚП®В« (Those among you who were best in Jahiliyyah, are t he best among you in Islam, if they attain religious understanding.)'' Al-Bukha ri collected this Hadith in several places of his Sahih, AnNasa'i did as well in the Tafsir section of his Sunan. Muslim recorded that Abu Hurayrah said

that the Messenger of Allah said,

В» ЛёПҰ Л¶Пң Пҹ ЛҙПӯ Лё ПўЛө ПңЛ¶ ПҹОҚЛҙ П®Лё ПЈЛҙ ОғЛҙ Пӯ ЛёПў ЛөПӣ Л¶Оӯ ЛҙП® ЛөО» П°Лҙ ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ ЛөО® ЛөПҲ ЛёПЁ ЛҙПі ОҺЛҙ Пҹ ЛҙНҝ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ Пў ЛөПң Л¶Пҹ ОҺ ЛҙПӨ ЛёПӢ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў ЛөПң Л¶О‘ П® ЛөП  ЛөП— П°Лҙ ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ ЛөО® ЛөПҲ ЛёПЁ ЛҙПіВ« (Verily, Allah does not look at your shapes or wealth, but he looks at your hearts and actions.) Ibn Maj ah also collected this Hadith. Ibn Abi Hatim r ecorded that ' Abdullah bin ' Umar said,

"On the day Makkah was conquered, the Messen ger of Allah performed Tawaf around the Ka'bah while riding his camel, Al-Qaswa', and wa s touching the corners with a stick he had in his hand. He did not find a place in the Masj id where his camel could si t and had to dismount on men's hands. He led his camel to the bottom of t he valley, where he had her sit down. The Messenger of Allah later gave a speech while ridi ng Al-Qaswa' and said, after thanking Allah the Exalted and praising Him as He should be praised,

В» ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛёПЁ ЛҙПӢ ЛҙОҗ ЛҙП« ЛёО« ЛҙОғ ЛёОӘ ЛҙП— П° ЛҙПҹ ОҺ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙО— ЛҙНҝ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛөОұ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ П¬НҠ ПіЛҙ Оғ ОҺЛҙ Пі Л¶ПҘ ОҺ ЛҙП  ЛөОҹ ЛҙОӯ ЛөОұ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҺ ЛҙП“ Л¬ ОҺ ЛҙП¬ Л¶ОӢ ОҺ ЛҙО‘ ОӮ Л¶О‘ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лҙ ПӨНҠ ПҲЛҙ ПҢЛҙ О—Лҙ Пӯ Л¶О” НүПҙ Л¶П  Л¶П« ОҺ ЛҙО  ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙО” НүПҙ НӢО’ ЛөПӢ :

ЛІПһ ЛөОҹ ЛҙОӯ ЛҙПӯ Л¬П°Лҙ ПҹОҺЛҙ ПҢЛҙ О— Л¶Нҝ ОҚ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛІПў Пі Л¶О® ЛҙПӣ НҮПІ Л¶Пҳ ЛҙО— НҮО® ЛҙО‘ ЛІПһ ЛөОҹ ЛҙОӯ НүО° ЛҙПӢ ЛҙНҝ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ Л¬П°Лҙ ПҹОҺЛҙ ПҢЛҙ О— Л¶Нҝ ОҚ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛІПҰ НӢПҙ ЛҙП« НҮПІ Л¶Пҳ ЛҙО· ЛІО® Л¶Оҹ ОҺ ЛҙП“ ЛөПқ П® ЛөПҳ ЛҙПі НүПһ ЛҙОҹ ЛҙПӯ:

П°Лҙ ОңЛё П§Лө ОғЛҙ Пӯ ЛіО® ЛҙПӣ ЛҙО« ПҰНӢ ПЈ Пў ЛөПң Л° ЛҙПЁ ЛёПҳ ЛҙП  ЛҙО§ ОҺНү П§Л¶ ОҮ ЛөОұ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ П¬НҠ ПіЛҙ О„ПіНҪ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөП“ ЛҙО® Л° ЛҙПҢ ЛҙОҳ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙПһ Л¶ОӢ ОӮ ЛҙО’ ЛҙП— ЛҙПӯ Лұ ОҺО‘П®Лө ПҢЛө О· Лё ПўЛө ПңЛ°Лҙ ПЁЛё П Лҙ ПҢЛҙ ОҹЛҙ Пӯ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛҙПЈ ЛҙО® ЛёПӣ ЛҙОғ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ Нј ЛІ О®ПҙЛ¶ О’Лҙ О§ ЛІПў Пҙ Л¶П  ЛҙПӢ ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛёПў ЛөПң Л° ЛҙПҳ ЛёО— ЛҙОғ Л¶ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ ЛҙОӘ ПЁ ЛҙПӢВ« (Opeople! Allah the Exalted has removed from you the slogans of Jahiliyyah and its tradition of honoring forefathers. Men are of two types, a man who is righteous, fearful of Allah and honorable to Allah, or a man wh o is vicious, miserable and littl e to Allah the Exalted. Verily,

Allah the Exalted and Most Honored said, (Omankind! We have created you from a male and a female, and made you into nations and tribes, that you may know one another. Verily, the most honorable of you with Allah is that (bel iever) who has At-Taqwa. Verily, Allah is AllKnowing, All-Aware.)) The Prophet said,

В»ПўЛө ПңЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ПІЛ¶ Пҹ ЛҙНҝ ОҚ ЛөО® Л¶П” ЛёПҗ ЛҙОҳ ЛёОі ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ ОҚЛҙ О¬П« ПІЛ¶ ПҹЛё П®Лҙ П— ЛөПқ П® ЛөП— ЛҙОғВ« (Isay this and invoke Allah for forgiveness for me and for you.)'' This was recorded by ' Abd bin Humayd. Allah the Exalted said,

НјЛІ О®ПҙЛ¶ О’Лҙ О§ ЛІПў Пҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ Лҙ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ НҪ (Verily, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Aware.) means, ' He is All-Knowing of you and All-Aware of all of your affairs.' Allah guides whom He wills, mi sguides whom He wills, grants mercy to whom He wills, tor- ments whom He wills, elevates whom He wills above whom He wills. He is the AllWise, the All-Knowing, the All-Aware in all this. Several scholars relied on this honorable Ayah

and the honora- ble Hadiths we menti- oned as ev idence that compatibility in marriage is not a condition of marriage contracts. They said that th e only condition required in this regard is the adherence to the religion, as in the statement of Allah, the Exalted,

Нј ЛёПў ЛөПң Л° ЛҙПҳ ЛёО— ЛҙОғ Л¶ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ ЛҙОӘ ПЁ ЛҙПӢ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛҙПЈ ЛҙО® ЛёПӣ ЛҙОғ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ НҪ (Verily, the most honorable of you with Allah is the one with the most Taqwa. ) ЛёОҚ П® ЛөПҹ П® ЛөП— ПҰ Л¶Пң Л° ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ Лё ОҚП®Лө ПЁЛ¶ ПЈЛё ОҶЛө О— ЛёПў НүПҹ ПһЛө П— ОҺНү ПЁЛҙ ПЈОҚЛҙ ЛҜ Лө ОҸОҚЛҙ О®Лё ПӢНҮ П»ОҚ Л¶О– ЛҙПҹ ОҺ ЛҙП— НҪ Л¶ О‘П®Лө П Лө П— П°Л¶ П“ ЛөПҰ Л° ЛҙПӨ Пі П» ОҚ Л¶Пһ ЛөО§ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПі ОҺНү ПӨЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПӨЛҙ П Лё ОіЛҙ Оғ ПҘЛ¶ ОҮЛҙ Пӯ ЛёПў ЛөПң Лұ ОҺОҢЛё ПҙЛҙ О· Лё ПўЛө ПңЛ¶ П Л°Лҙ ПӨЛё ПӢЛҙ Оғ ЛёПҰ НӢПЈ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛёОҳ Л¶П  ЛҙПі ЛҙП» Лө ПӘЛҙ ПҹП®Лө ОіЛҙ ОӯЛҙ Пӯ ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөПҢ Пҙ Л¶П„ ЛөО— ЛІПў Пҙ Л¶ОЈ НүОӯ ЛІОӯ П® ЛөП” ЛҙПҸ ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ- ЛҙПҰ Пі Л¶О¬ НүПҹ ОҚ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө ПЁЛ¶ ПЈЛё ОҶЛө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ ОҺЛҙ ПӨНү П§Л¶ ОҮ ЛёОҚ Пӯ ЛөОӘ ЛҙП¬ Л° ЛҙОҹ ЛҙПӯ Лё ОҚП®Лө О‘ОҺЛҙ О—Лё О®Лҙ Пі ЛёПў ЛҙПҹ НүПў ЛөОӣ Л¶ПӘ Л¶Пҹ П® ЛөОі ЛҙОӯ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ Лё ОҚП®Лө ПЁЛҙ ПЈОҚЛҙ ЛҜ ЛҙОі П°Л¶ П“ ЛёПў Л¶П¬ Л¶Оҙ ЛөП” П§ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў Л¶П¬ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙП® ЛёПЈ ЛҙО„ Л¶О‘ ЛөПў ЛөП« ЛҙПҡ Л¶ОҢ Л° ЛҙПҹ ЛёПӯ ЛөОғ Л¶ПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ Л¶Пһ Пҙ Л¶О’ ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөП— Л¶ОӘ Л° НүОј Пҹ ОҚ- ЛөПў ЛҙП  ЛёПҢ ЛҙПі Лө ПӘНү П ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ Лё ПўЛө ПңЛ¶ ПЁПіЛ¶ ОӘЛ¶ О‘ ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө ПӨНӢ П Лҙ ПҢЛө О—Лҙ Оғ ЛёПһ ЛөП— НӢПһ ЛөПң Л¶О‘ Лө ПӘНү П ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ Л¶ОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ОҺЛҙ ПЈЛҙ Пӯ Л¶О• ЛҙП® Л° ЛҙПӨ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ЛІПў Пҙ Л¶П  ЛҙПӢ ЛіЛҜ ЛёП° ЛҙО·- Лё ОҚП®НҠ ПЁЛө ПӨЛҙ О— НүП» ПһЛө П— Лё ОҚП®Лө ПӨЛҙ П Лё ОіЛҙ Оғ ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПҡ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ Лҙ ПҘП®НҠ ПЁЛө ПӨЛҙ Пі Лҙ ПӨЛ°Лҙ П Лё ОіЛ¶ ОҮ НүП° ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ ЛёПў ЛөПӣ ОҚ ОӘ ЛҙП« ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ НҠПҰ ЛөПӨ ЛҙПі ЛөПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ Л¶Пһ ЛҙО‘ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛҙПҰ Пҙ Л¶П— Л¶ОӘ Л° ЛҙО» Лё ПўЛө ОҳПЁЛө Пӣ ЛөПҘ Л¶ОҮ Л¶ ПҰЛ°Лҙ ПӨПіПјЛ¶ Пҹ- ЛҙОҗ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПҸ ЛөПў ЛҙП  ЛёПҢ ЛҙПі ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ Нј Лҙ ПҘП®Лө П Лҙ ПӨЛё ПҢЛҙ О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ О‘ ЛІО® Пҙ Л¶Ој ЛҙО‘ Лө ПӘНү П ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ Л¶ОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Л¶О• ЛҙП® Л° ЛҙПӨ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ (14. The bedouins say: "We believe.'' Say: "You do not believe. But say, ' We have submitted,'

for Faith has not yet entered your hearts. But if you obey Allah and His Messenger, He will not decrease anything in reward for your deeds. Verily , Allah is Forgiving, Most Merciful.'') (15. Only those are the believers who have believed in Allah and His Messenger, and afterward doubt not but strive with their wealth and their lives for the cause of Allah. Those! They are the truthful.) (16. Say: "Will you info rm Allah of your religion while Allah knows all that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth, and Allah is All-Aware of everything.'') (17. They regard as a favor to you that they have embraced Islam. Say: " Do not count your Islam as a favor to me.

Nay, but Allah has conferred a favor upon you that He has guided you to the Faith if you indeed are true. '') (18. "Verily, Allah knows the Unseen of the heavens and the earth. And Allah is the All-Seer of what you do.'')

There is a Difference between a Believer and a Muslim Allah chastises the bedouins who, when they em braced Islam, claimed for themselves the grade of faithful believers. However, Faith had not yet f irmly entered their hearts,

ЛёОҚ П® ЛөПҹ П® ЛөП— ПҰ Л¶Пң Л° ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ Лё ОҚП®Лө ПЁЛ¶ ПЈЛё ОҶЛө О— ЛёПў НүПҹ ПһЛө П— ОҺНү ПЁЛҙ ПЈОҚЛҙ ЛҜ Лө ОҸОҚЛҙ О®Лё ПӢНҮ П»ОҚ Л¶О– ЛҙПҹ ОҺ ЛҙП— НҪ НјЛё ПўЛө ПңЛ¶ О‘П®Лө П Лө П— П°Л¶ П“ ЛөПҰ Л° ЛҙПӨ Пі П» ОҚ Л¶Пһ ЛөО§ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПі ОҺНү ПӨЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПӨЛҙ П Лё ОіЛҙ Оғ (The bedouins say: "We believe.'' Say: "You do not believe, bu t say, ' We have submitted,' for Faith has not yet entered your hearts...'') This honorable Ayah provides proof that Faith is a higher grade than Islam, according to the scho lars of the Ahl us-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah. This is also demostrated in the Hadith of Jibril, peace be upon him, when he questioned the Prophet about Islam, then Iman then Ihsan. Thus moving the general matter to one more specific, then even more specific. Imam Ahmad recorded that ' Amir bin S a'd bin Abi Waqqas said, "The Messenger of Allah gave (something to) some men and did not give one of them. S a'd said, ' O Allah's Messenger, you gave to so-and-so and so-and-so. However, you gave nothing to so-andso, even though he is a believer.' The Prophet said ,

В»Л®ЛІ ПўЛ¶ П Лё ОҙЛө ПЈ ЛёПӯ ЛҙОғВ« (Or say, a Muslim.) S a'd repeated his statement thrice each time the Prophet answered,

В»Л®ЛІ ПўЛ¶ П Лё ОҙЛө ПЈ ЛёПӯ ЛҙОғВ« (Or say, a Muslim.) The Prophet then said,

В» НүПІ ЛҙПҹ Л¶ОҮ НүОҗ ЛҙОЈ ЛҙОғ ЛҙП® ЛөП« ЛёПҰ ЛҙПЈ ЛөПү ЛҙО© ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ ОҺ ЛұПҹ ОҺ ЛҙОҹ Л¶Оӯ ПІЛ¶ П„Лё ПӢЛө О„Лҙ Пҹ ПІНӢ П§Л¶ ОҮ Лҙ П“Л¬Лё ПўЛө П¬Лё ПЁЛ¶ ПЈ Л¶Оӯ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ ПІЛ¶ П“ ОҚП®НҠ О’Лҙ ПңЛө Пі ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ ЛҙО” ЛҙП“ ОҺ ЛҙОЁ ЛҙПЈ ОҺЛұ ОҢЛё ПҙЛҙ О· Л¶ПӘ Л¶П„ ЛёПӢ ЛөОғ ЛёПў ЛҙП  Пў Л¶П¬ Л¶П« П® ЛөОҹ ЛөПӯ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢВ« (Imight give some men and give nothing to others, even though the latter are dearer to me than the former. Ido not give them things for fe ar that they might be thrown on their faces in the Fire.)'' This Hadith is recorded in the Two Sahihs. Therefore, the Prophet made a distinction between the grade of believer and the grade of Muslim, indicating that Iman is a more exclusive grade than Islam. Imentioned this su bj ect in detail supported by evidence, in the beginning of the explanation of the chapter on Im an in Sahih Al-Bukhari, all praise is due to Allah and all the favors are from Him. S o this prov es that the bedouins whom the Ayah mentioned were not hypocrites, rather they were Mus lims in whose hearts Faith was not yet firmly established. They claimed a higher gr ade for themselves than the grade that they earned, and they were taught a lesson as a consequence. This meaning agrees with the meaning given by Ibn ' Abbas, Ibrahim An-Nakha'i , Qatadah and that preferred by Ibn Jarir.

These bedouins were taught a lesson,

Л¶Пһ ЛөО§ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПі ОҺНү ПӨЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПӨЛҙ П Лё ОіЛҙ Оғ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөПҹ П® ЛөП— ПҰ Л¶Пң Л° ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ Лё ОҚП®Лө ПЁЛ¶ ПЈЛё ОҶЛө О— ЛёПў НүПҹ ПһЛө П—НҪ НјЛё ПўЛө ПңЛ¶ О‘П®Лө П Лө П— П°Л¶ П“ ЛөПҰ Л° ЛҙПӨ Пі П» ОҚ (Say: "You do not believe, but say ' We are Muslims,' for Faith has not yet entered your hearts...'') meaning, ' you have not yet achieved the reality of Faith.' Allah the Exalted said,

Лё ПўЛө ПңЛ¶ П Л°Лҙ ПӨЛё ПӢЛҙ Оғ ЛёПҰ НӢПЈ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛёОҳ Л¶П  ЛҙПі ЛҙП» Лө ПӘЛҙ ПҹП®Лө ОіЛҙ ОӯЛҙ Пӯ ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөПҢ Пҙ Л¶П„ ЛөО— ПҘЛ¶ ОҮЛҙ ПӯНҪ НјЛұ ОҺОҢЛё ПҙЛҙ О· (But if you obey Allah and His Messenger, He wi ll not decrease anything in reward for your deeds...) ' He will not decrease any of your rewards,' as Allah said;

НјЛі ЛҜЛё П°Лҙ О· ПҰНӢ ПЈ ПўЛ¶ П¬Л¶ П Лҙ ПӨЛҙ ПӢ ЛёПҰ НӢПЈ Лё ПўЛө П¬Л°Лҙ ПЁЛё ОҳЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Оғ ОӮЛҙ ПЈЛҙ ПӯНҪ (We shall not decrease the reward of thei r deeds in anything.) (52:21) Allah said:

Лҙ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ НҪНјЛІ ПўПҙЛ¶ ОЈНү Оӯ ЛІОӯ П® ЛөП” ЛҙПҸ (Verily, Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.) for those who repent and return to Him. Allah's statement,

Нј ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөПЁ Л¶ПЈ ЛёОҶ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ ПӨНү П§Л¶ ОҮНҪ (Only those are the believers), who have perfect Fa ith,

Л¶ПӘ Л¶Пҹ П® ЛөОі ЛҙОӯ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ Лё ОҚП®Лө ПЁЛҙ ПЈОҚЛҙ ЛҜ Лҙ ПҰПіЛ¶ О¬Нү ПҹОҚНҪНјЛё ОҚП®Лө О‘ОҺЛҙ О—Лё О®Лҙ Пі ЛёПў ЛҙПҹ НүПў ЛөОӣ (who have believed in Allah and His Messenger , and afterward doubt not) They do not have doubts and their Faith was not shaken. Rather, thei r Faith remained on conviction,

ПҹОҚ Л¶ ПһПҙЛ¶ О’Лҙ Оі П°Л¶ П“ ЛёПў Л¶П¬ Л¶Оҙ ЛөП” П§ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў Л¶П¬ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙП® ЛёПЈ ЛҙО„ Л¶О‘ ЛёОҚ Пӯ ЛөОӘ ЛҙП¬ Л° ЛҙОҹ ЛҙПӯ НҪНјЛ¶ ПӘНү П  (but strive for the cause of Allah with their weal th and their lives) meaning, they gladly gave away their life and the most precious of their weal th in obedience to Allah as a means of seeking His pleasure,

НјЛҙ ПҘП®Лө П—Л¶ ОӘЛ°Нү ОјПҹОҚ ЛөПў ЛөП« Лҙ ПҡЛ¶ ОҢЛ°Лҙ ПҹЛё ПӯЛө ОғНҪ (Those! They are the truthful. ) ' in their statemen t if they say that they are believers, unlike some bedouins who are faithful only by words outwar dly!' Allah said,

НјЛё ПўЛө ПңЛ¶ ПЁПіЛ¶ ОӘЛ¶ О‘ ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө ПӨНӢ П Лҙ ПҢЛө О—Лҙ Оғ ЛёПһ ЛөП— НҪ (Say: "Will you inform Allah of your religion...'') ' will you inform Alla h of what is in your hearts,'

НјЛ¶ ОҪЛё ОӯНҮ П»ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ОҺЛҙ ПЈЛҙ Пӯ Л¶О• ЛҙП® Л° ЛҙПӨ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ЛөПў ЛҙП  ЛёПҢ ЛҙПі Лө ПӘНү П ПҹОҚЛҙ ПӯНҪ (while Allah knows all that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth,) Nothing in the heavens and earth, even the weight of a speck of dust, all that is bigger or smaller, ever escapes His observation,

Нј ЛІПў Пҙ Л¶П  ЛҙПӢ ЛіЛҜ ЛёПІ ЛҙО· НӢПһ ЛөПң Л¶О‘ Лө ПӘНү П ПҹОҚЛҙ ПӯНҪ (and Allah is All-Aware of everything.) Allah said,

НүП° ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ Лё ОҚП®НҠ ПЁЛө ПӨЛҙ О— НүП» ПһЛө П— ЛёОҚ П® ЛөПӨ ЛҙП  ЛёОі ЛҙОғ ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПҡ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ Лҙ ПҘП®НҠ ПЁЛө ПӨЛҙ ПіНҪ Нј ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛҙПӨ Л° ЛҙП  ЛёОі Л¶ОҮ (They regard as a favor to you that they have em braced Islam. Say: "Do not count your Islam as a favor to me...'') meaning the bedouins who consid ered embracing Islam, following and supporting the Messenger as a favor to him. Allah the Exalted refuted their false statement,

Нј ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛҙПӨ Л° ЛҙП  ЛёОі Л¶ОҮ НүП° ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ Лё ОҚП®НҠ ПЁЛө ПӨЛҙ О— НүП» ПһЛө П—НҪ (Say: "Do not count your Islam as a favor to me... '') ' for the benefit of your Islam will only be yours, and this is a fa vor from Allah to you,'

ЛөПҘ Л¶ОҮ Л¶ ПҰЛ°Лҙ ПӨПіПјЛ¶ Пҹ ЛёПў ЛөПӣ ОҚ ОӘ ЛҙП« ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ НҠПҰ ЛөПӨ ЛҙПі Лө ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ Л¶Пһ ЛҙО‘ НҪ Лё ПўЛө ОҳПЁЛө Пӣ Нј ЛҙПҰ Пҙ Л¶П— Л¶ОӘ Л° ЛҙО» (Nay, but Allah has conferred a favor upon you that He has guided you to the Faith if you indeed are true.) ' in your claim that you are believers.' The Prop het said to the Ansar on the day of the battle of Hunayn,

В»ЛҙО„ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙО® ЛҙОё ЛёПҢ ЛҙПЈ ОҺЛҙ Пі ЛөНҝ ОҚ ЛөПў ЛөПӣ ОҚ ЛҙОӘ ЛҙП¬ ЛҙП“ ОҺЛұ ПҹОҺНү П Лө Оҝ ЛёПў ЛөПӣ ЛёОӘ Л¶Оҹ ЛҙОғ ЛёПў ЛҙПҹ ЛҙОғ Л¶Оӯ ОҺ ЛҙОј ЛёП§ ЛұО” ЛҙПҹ ОҺ ЛҙПӢ ЛёПў ЛөОҳ ЛёПЁ ЛөПӣ ЛҙПӯ Л®ПІЛ¶ О‘ ЛөНҝ ОҚ ЛөПў ЛөПң ЛҙП” НүПҹ ЛҙО„ ЛҙП“ Лҙ ПҰПҙЛ¶ П—НӢ О®Лҙ П”Лҙ ОҳЛө ПЈ ЛёПў ЛөОҳ ЛёПЁ ЛөПӣ ЛҙПӯ Л®ПІЛ¶ О‘ Л®ПІЛ¶ О‘ ЛөНҝ ОҚ ЛөПў ЛөПӣ ОҺ ЛҙПЁ ЛёПҸ ЛҙО„ ЛҙП“В« (O Ansar! Have Inot found you astray and Alla h guided you through me Were you not divided and Allah united you around me Were you not poor and Allah gave you riches through me) Whenever the Prophet asked them any of these questions, they would reply, "Allah and His Messenger have most favored us.'' Al-Hafiz Abu Ba kr Al-Bazzar recorded that Ibn ' Abbas said,

"Banu Asad came to the Messenger of Allah and said, ' O Allah's Messenger! We embraced Islam,

and before that, the Arabs fought against you, yet we did not fight against you.' The Messenger of Allah said,

В»Лҙ ПҘОҺЛҙ П„Лё ПҙНү ОёПҹОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛҙПӯ ЛІПһ Пҙ Л¶П  ЛҙП— ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛҙП¬ ЛёПҳ Л¶П“ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛөП– Л¶П„ ЛёПЁ ЛҙПі ПўЛ¶ П¬Л¶ ОҳЛҙ ПЁЛ¶ ОҙЛё ПҹЛҙ ОғВ« (Verily, they understand but little and the Shayta n speaks through their words.) This Ayah was later revealed,

НүП° ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ Лё ОҚП®НҠ ПЁЛө ПӨЛҙ О— НүП» ПһЛө П— ЛёОҚ П® ЛөПӨ ЛҙП  ЛёОі ЛҙОғ ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПҡ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ Лҙ ПҘП®НҠ ПЁЛө ПӨЛҙ ПіНҪ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ НҠПҰ ЛөПӨ ЛҙПі Лө ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ Л¶Пһ ЛҙО‘ Лё ПўЛө ПңЛҙ ПӨЛ°Лҙ П Лё ОіЛ¶ ОҮ ЛөПҘ Л¶ОҮ Л¶ ПҰЛ°Лҙ ПӨПіПјЛ¶ Пҹ ЛёПў ЛөПӣ ОҚ ОӘ ЛҙП« ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ Нј ЛҙПҰ Пҙ Л¶П— Л¶ОӘ Л° ЛҙО» Лё ПўЛө ОҳПЁЛө Пӣ (They regard as a favor to you that they have em braced Islam. Say: "Do not count your Islam as a favor to me.'' Nay, but Allah has conferred a favor upon you that He has guided y ou to the Faith if you indeed are true.'')''' Then Allah reminds that He has complete knowledge of all creations and that He sees them all;

Лө ПӘНү П ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ Л¶ОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Л¶О• ЛҙП® Л° ЛҙПӨ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙОҗ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПҸ ЛөПў ЛҙП  ЛёПҢ ЛҙПі ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ НҪ Нј Лҙ ПҘП®Лө П Лҙ ПӨЛё ПҢЛҙ О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ О‘ ЛІО® Пҙ Л¶Ој ЛҙО‘ (Verily, Allah knows the Unseen of the heavens an d the earth. And Allah is the All-Seer of what you do.) This is the end of the Tafsir of Surat Al -Huj urat. Verily, all praise is due to Allah, all the favors are from Him, and from Him comes the suc cess and protection from error.

The Tafsir of Surah Qaf (Chapter - 50) Which was revealed in Makkah The Beginning of the Mufassal Section of the Qur'an This Surah is the first Surah in the Mufassal se ction of the Qur'an, according to the correct view. It is said that the Mufassal starts with Surat Al-Huj urat. Some common people say that the Mufassal starts with Surah ' Amma An-Naba, (chapter 78), however, this is not true because none of the respected scholars ever supported this opinion. Aws (bin Hudhayfah) said; "Iasked the Companions of Allah's Messenger () how they divided the Qur'an. They said; ' Three, five,

seven, nine, eleven, thirteen, and the Mufassal section as one.' '' This was recorded by Ibn Maj ah and Imam Ahmad. If one counts forty-eight Surahs, the next Surah will be Surah Qaf. The details are as follows: The НҪfirst Нј three Surahs are Al-Baqarah (chapter 2), Al ' Imran (3), then An-Nisa' (4). The five are Al-Ma'idah (5), Al-An'am (6), Al-A'raf (7), Al-Anfal (8) and Bara'ah (or At-Tawbah) (9). The seven next Surahs are S u rah Yunus (10), Hud (11 ), Yusuf (12), Ar-R a'd (13), Ibrahim (14), Al-Hij r (15) and An-Nahl (16). The nine next Surahs are, Subhan (or Al-Isra'

(17)Нј, Al-Kahf (18), Maryam (19), Ta Ha (20), Al -Anbiya' (21), Al-Haj j (22), Al-Mu'minun (23),

An-Nur (24) and Al-Furqan (25). The next elev en Surahs are Surat Ash-Shu'ara (26), An-Naml (27), Al-Qasas (28), Al-' Ankabut (29), Ar-Rum (30), Luqman (31), Alif Lam Mim As-Saj dah (32),

Al-Ahzab (33), Saba' (34), Fatir (35) and Ya Sin (36). The next thirteen are Surat As-Saffat (37),

Sad (38), Az-Zumar (39), Ghafir (40), Ha Mim As -Saj dah (or Fussilat) (41), Ash-Shura (42), AzZukhruf (43), Ad-Dukhan (44), Al-Jathiyah (45), Al- Ahqaf (46), Al-Qital (or Muhammad) (47), AlFath (48) and Al-Huj urat (49). After that co mes the Mufassal section, according to the

Companions, may Allah be pleased with them all. Therefore, Sura h Qaf (chapter 50 ) is the first of the Mufassal, j ust as we stated, and all praise is due to Allah and all favors are from Him.

The Virtues of Surah Qaf Imam Ahmad recorded that ' Umar bin Al-Khattab asked Abu Waqid Al-Laythi, "What did the Prophet recite during the ' Id Prayer'' Abu Waqid said, "Surah Qaf and Surat Iqtarabat НҪi.e. Surat Al-Qamar (54) Нј.'' Muslim and the Four Collectors of the Sunan collected this Hadith. Imam Ahmad recorded that Umm Hisham bint Harithah said, "For around two years, or a year and a part of another year, our oven and the oven of the Prophet was one and the same. Imemorized Surah,

Нј Л¶ОӘ Пҙ Л¶О  ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶ПҘ ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ ЛёО® ЛөПҳ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ П•НҪ (Qaf. By the Glorious Qur'an.) from the tongue of t he Messenger of Allah who used to recite it every Friday while standing on the Minbar delive ring the Friday sermon to the people.'' Muslim collected this Hadith. Abu Dawud also recorded that the daughter of Al-Harith bin An-Nu'man said, "Ionly memorized Surah Qaf from the mouth of the Messenger of Allah who used to recite it in every Friday Khutbah. Our oven and the oven of the Messenger was one and the same.''

Muslim and An-Nasa'i collected this Hadith. Theref ore, the Messenger of Allah used to recite this Surah during large gatherings such as th e ' Ids and during Friday sermons. He did so because this Surah contains news of the begi nning of creation, Resurrec- tion, the Return,

Standing (before Allah), the Reckoning, Paradi se, the Fire, Allah's reward and punishment,

lessons of encouragement, and lessons of discouragement. Allah knows best.

НјЛ¶ ПўПҙЛ¶ ОЈНү О®ПҹОҚ Л¶ ПҰЛ°Лҙ ПӨЛё ОЈНү О®ПҹОҚ Л¶ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ Л¶Пў ЛёОҙ Л¶О‘ НҪ In the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

Л¶ОӘ Пҙ Л¶О  ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶ ПҘОҚЛҜЛё О®Лө ПҳЛё ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ П•НҪ- ЛёПў ЛөП« ЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙОҹ ПҘЛҙ Оғ Лё ОҚП®Лө О’Л¶ О Лҙ ПӢ ЛёПһ ЛҙО‘ ЛІОӯ Л¶О¬ ПЁ НҠПЈЛІОҗ Пҙ Л¶О  ЛҙПӢ ЛҜЛё П°Лҙ О· ОҚЛҙ О¬Л°Лҙ П« ЛҙПҘ Пӯ ЛөО® Л¶П” Л° ЛҙПң ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПқ ОҺ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙП“ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛёПЁ Л·ПЈ ЛІ ОӘПҙЛ¶ ПҢЛҙ О‘ ЛөПҠ ЛёОҹ ЛҙОӯ ЛҙПҡ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙО« ЛұОҺ О‘ ОҚ ЛҙО® ЛөО— ОҺНү ПЁЛө ПӣЛҙ Пӯ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ОҳЛ¶ ПЈ ОҚЛҙ О«ЛҜЛҙ Оғ- ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПӨЛ¶ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛёОӘ ЛҙП—

ЛІПҶ Пҙ Л¶П” ЛҙОЈ ЛІОҗ Л° ЛҙОҳ Л¶Пӣ ОҺ ЛҙП§ ЛҙОӘ ПЁ Л¶ПӢ ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛёПЁ Л¶ПЈ ЛҙОҪ ЛёОӯ ЛёП» ОҚ ЛөОә ЛөПҳ ПЁ ЛҙО—- ЛёПһ ЛҙО‘ ЛіОһ Пі Л¶О® НүПЈ ЛіО® ЛёПЈ ЛҙОғ П°Л¶ П“ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛҙП“ ЛёПў ЛөП« ЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙОҹ ОҺНү ПӨЛҙ Пҹ Л·П– ЛҙОӨ ЛёПҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ Лё ОҚП®Лө О‘Нү О¬Лҙ Пӣ-Нј (1. Qaf. By the Glorious Qur'an.) (2. Nay, they won der that there has come to them a warner from among themselves. S o the disbelievers say: "This is a strange thing!'') (3. "When we are dead and have become dust. (Shall we be resurrect ed) That is a far return.'') (4. We know that which the earth takes of them, and with Us is a Book preserved.) (5. Nay, but they have denied the truth when it has come to them, so they are in a Marij state.) НјП•НҪ (Qaf.) which is one of the letters of the alphabet that are mentioned in the beginning of some Surahs, such as,

НјО№НҪ (Sad.) (38:1) НјПҘНҪ (Nun. ) (68:1) Нј ПўПҹОҚНҪ (Alif Lam Mim.) (2:1),

Нј ПўЛ°ОЈНҪ (Ha Mim.) (40:1), and НјОІПғНҪ (Ta Sin) (28:1) and etc., Muj ahid and several othe rs said this. We also discussed this in the beginning of the explanation of Surat Al-Baqarah, a nd therefore, it is not necessary to repeat it here.

The Disbelievers wonder at the Message and Resurrection Allah said,

Нј Л¶ОӘ Пҙ Л¶О  ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶ ПҘОҚЛҙ ЛҜЛё О®Лө ПҳЛё ПҹОҚЛҙ ПӯНҪ (By the Glorious Qur'an.) means by the Honorable and Great Qur'an, which,

Л¶ПӘ Л¶П” ЛёП  ЛҙО§ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ Л¶ПӘ ЛёПі ЛҙОӘ ЛҙПі Л¶ПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ Лө ПһЛ¶ П„Л°Лҙ О’Лё ПҹОҚ Л¶ ПӘПҙЛ¶ О—Лё О„Лҙ Пі НүП» НҪ Нј Лі ОӘПҙЛ¶ ПӨЛҙ ОЈ Лі ПўПҙЛ¶ ПңЛҙ ОЈ ЛёПҰ НӢПЈ ЛІПһ Пі Л¶О° ПЁ ЛҙО— (Falsehood cannot come to it from before it or behi nd it: (it is) sent down by the All-Wise,

Worthy of all praise.)(41:42) The subj ect of th e oath contained in this Ayah is specified afterwards, even though it does not appear by word, emphasizing prophethood, resurrection and affirming that they are true. There are similar kinds of oaths in the Qur'an, whose subj ect is included in the meaning but not by word, such as ,

Л¶О® ЛёПӣ НӢО¬ Пҹ ОҚ ПҜЛ¶ О« Л¶ ПҘОҚЛҙ ЛҜЛё О®Лө ПҳЛё ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ О№НҪ- П°Л¶ П“ ЛёОҚ Пӯ ЛөО® ЛҙП” ЛҙПӣ ЛҙПҰ Пі Л¶О¬ НүПҹ ОҚ Л¶Пһ ЛҙО‘ Нј Лі П•ОҺЛҙ ПҳЛ¶ О·Лҙ Пӯ ЛіО“ НүО° Л¶ПӢ ( S a d . B y t h e Q u r ' a n f u l l o f r e m i n d i n g . N a y , those who disbelieve are in false pride and opposition.)(38:1-2) Allah said here,

ЛҙЛҜ ЛёО® ЛөПҳ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ П•НҪ Л¶ОӘ Пҙ Л¶О  ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶ПҘ ОҚ- ЛёПў ЛөП« ЛҙЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙОҹ ПҘЛҙ Оғ Лё ОҚП®Лө О’Л¶ О Лҙ ПӢ ЛёПһ ЛҙО‘ Нј ЛІОҗ Пҙ Л¶О  ЛҙПӢ ЛІЛҜ ЛёП° ЛҙО· ОҚЛҙ О¬Л°Лҙ П« ЛҙПҘ Пӯ ЛөО® Л¶П” Л° ЛҙПң ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПқ ОҺ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙП“ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛёПЁ НӢПЈ ЛІОӯ Л¶О¬ ПЁ НҠПЈ (Qaf. By the Glorious Qur'an. Nay, they wonder that there has come to them a warner from among themselves. S o the disbelievers say: "Thi s is a strange thing!'') They wondered at the wisdom behind sending a Messenger who is a human being. Allah the Exalted and Most Honored said in another Ayah ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛёПЁ НӢПЈ ЛіПһ ЛөОҹ ЛҙОӯ П°Лҙ ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ ОӮЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ ОЈЛё ПӯЛҙ Оғ ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ ОҺЛұ О’Лҙ О Лҙ ПӢ Л¶Оұ ОҺ НүПЁ П  Л¶Пҹ ЛҙПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПӣ ЛҙОғ НҪ НјЛҙ ОұОҺНү ПЁПҹОҚ Л¶Оӯ Л¶О¬ П§ ЛҙОғ (Is it a wonder for mankind that We have sent Our revelation to a man from among themselves (saying): "Warn mankind.'') (10:2), meaning, this is not strange, for Allah chooses Messengers from angels and humans. Allah the Exalted and Most Honored mentioned that the disbelievers also wondered about the Resurrection and discounted its coming,

Нј ЛІ ОӘПҙЛ¶ ПҢЛҙ О‘ ЛөПҠ ЛёОҹ ЛҙОӯ ЛҙПҡ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙО« ЛұОҺ О‘ ОҚ ЛҙО® ЛөО— ОҺНү ПЁЛө ПӣЛҙ Пӯ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ОҳЛ¶ ПЈ ОҚЛҙ О«Л¶ ЛҜЛҙ ОғНҪ (When we are dead and have become dust. That is a f ar return.) They said, ' after we die,

disintegrate, with our organs torn apart and we become dust, how can we be brought back to our original shape and bodies,'

Нј ЛІОӘ Пҙ Л¶ПҢ ЛҙО‘ ЛөПҠ ЛёОҹ ЛҙОӯ ЛҙПҡ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙО« НҪ (That is a far return.) ' it is not likely that it will ever occur.' They thought that Resurrection was far from happening and will never occur. Allah the Exalted responded to their statement by saying,

НјЛё ПўЛө П¬Лё ПЁЛ¶ ПЈ ЛҙОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ЛөОә ЛөПҳ ПЁ ЛҙО— ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПӨЛ¶ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛёОӘ ЛҙП— НҪ (We know that which the earth takes of them.) meaning, ' We know what the earth consumes of their dead bodies.' Where and how the bodies disintegrated, what they turned into and how they have become, all of this is never absent from Allah's knowledge.

Нј ЛІПҶ Пҙ Л¶П” ЛҙОЈ ЛІОҗ Л° ЛҙОҳ Л¶Пӣ ОҺ ЛҙП§ ЛҙОӘ ПЁ Л¶ПӢ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (and with Us is a Book preserved,) ' that ke eps all records. Therefore, Our knowledge is encompassing and the Book of decrees has everything precisely recorded in it. Al -' Awfi narrated that ' Abdullah bin ' Abbas commented on the statement of Allah the Exalted,

НјЛё ПўЛө П¬Лё ПЁЛ¶ ПЈ ЛҙОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ЛөОә ЛөПҳ ПЁ ЛҙО— ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПӨЛ¶ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛёОӘ ЛҙП— НҪ (We know that which the earth takes of them,) "I t refers to what the earth consumes of their flesh, skin, bones and hair.'' Asimilar view wa s recorded from Muj ahid, Qatadah, Ad-Dahhak and several others. Allah, the Exalted and Most Honored, explained the reason behind their disbelief, rebellion and discounting the possibilit y of what is truly possible,

ЛіОһ Пі Л¶О® НүПЈ ЛіО® ЛёПЈ ЛҙОғ П°Л¶ П“ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛҙП“ ЛёПў ЛөП« ЛҙЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙОҹ ОҺНү ПӨЛҙ Пҹ НӢП– ЛҙОӨ ЛёПҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ Лё ОҚП®Лө О‘Нү О¬Лҙ Пӣ ЛёПһ ЛҙО‘ НҪ Нј (Nay, but they have denied the truth when it ha s come to them, so they are in a Marij state.) This is the state of all those who defy the Trut h: whatever they say and utter after denying the Truth, is utterly false. Marij means, in disarray, in a confused state and defying the characteristics of the Truth. Allah the Exalted sai d in another Ayah,

ЛіП’ Л¶П  ЛҙОҳ ЛёОЁ НҠПЈ ЛіПқ ЛёП® ЛҙП— П°Л¶ П”Лҙ Пҹ ЛёПў ЛөПң НүП§ Л¶ОҮ НҪ-ЛөПӘ ЛёПЁ ЛҙПӢ ЛөПҡ ЛҙП“ ЛёОҶ ЛөПі Нј ЛҙПҡ Л¶П“ ЛөОғ ЛёПҰ ЛҙПЈ (Certainly, you have different ideas. Turned asid e therefrom is he who is turned aside.)(51:8-9) ОҺ ЛҙП¬ Л° ЛҙПЁ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПЁ ЛҙО‘ ЛҙП’ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПӣ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛҙП— ЛёП® ЛҙП“ Л¶ЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙПӨ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ П°Лҙ ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ ЛёОҚ Пӯ ЛөО® ЛөПҲ ПЁ ЛҙПі ЛёПў ЛҙП  ЛҙП“ ЛҙОғ НҪ ЛіОқ Пӯ ЛөО® ЛөП“ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лҙ Пҹ ОҺЛҙ ПЈЛҙ Пӯ ОҺ ЛҙП¬ Л° НүПЁ НүПі ЛҙОҜ ЛҙПӯ- ОҺ ЛҙП¬ Л° ЛҙП§ ЛёО© ЛҙОӘ ЛҙПЈ ЛҙОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙПҳ ЛёПҹ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ ЛіОқ ЛёПӯ ЛҙОҜ НӢПһ ЛөПӣ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ОҺЛҙ П¬ПҙЛ¶ П“ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ОҳЛҙ О’П§Лҙ ОғЛҙ Пӯ ЛҙП° Л¶Оі ЛҙПӯ ЛҙОӯ ОҺЛҙ П¬ПҙЛ¶ П“ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё Пҙ Лі ОһПҙЛ¶ П¬Лҙ О‘- Лі ОҗПҙЛ¶ ПЁНҠ ПЈ ЛіОӘ ЛёО’ ЛҙПӢ НӢПһ ЛөПң Л¶Пҹ ПҜЛҙ О®Лё ПӣЛ¶ О«Лҙ Пӯ ЛұО“ ЛҙО® Л¶Ој ЛёО’ ЛҙО— - ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҹНү О°Лҙ П§Лҙ Пӯ НүОҗ ЛҙОЈ ЛҙПӯ ЛіО– Л° НүПЁ ЛҙОҹ Л¶ПӘ Л¶О‘ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ОҳЛҙ О’П§Лҙ О„Лҙ П“ ЛұОҺ Пӣ ЛҙО® Л° ЛҙО’ НҠПЈ ЛұЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙПЈ Л¶ ЛҜОӮЛҙ ПӨНү ОҙПҹОҚ ЛҙПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛёП  НӢПҹ Лұ ОҺП—Лё ОҜНӢ ОӯЛІ ОӘПҙЛ¶ ПҖНү П§ ЛІПҠ ЛёП  ЛҙПғ ОҺЛҙ П¬Нү Пҹ ЛіО– Л° ЛҙПҳ Л¶Оҙ Л° ЛҙО‘ Л¶ОӘ Пҙ Л¶Ој ЛҙОӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶О© ОҺ ЛҙО’ Л¶ПҢ Нј ЛөОқ Пӯ ЛөО® ЛөОЁ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҡ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӣ Лұ ОҺОҳЛё ПҙНү ПЈ ЛұО“ ЛҙОӘ ЛёП  ЛҙО‘ Л¶ПӘ Л¶О‘ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ ПҙЛё ОЈЛҙ ОғЛҙ Пӯ (6. Have they not looked at the heaven above them, how We have made it and adorned it, and there are no Furuj in it) (7. And the earth! We have spread it out, and set thereon Rawasi standing firm, and We planted in it every lovely (Bahij ) pair.) (8. An insight and a Reminder for every servant who turns to Allah in repentance .) (9. And We send down blessed water (rain) from the sky, then We produce therewith Jannat and grain that are reaped.) (10. And date palms Basiqat, with arranged c lusters.) (11. Aprovision for the servants. And We give life therewith to a dead land. Thus will be the Resurrec tion.)

Allah's Power and Ability over what is Greater than Resurrection Allah the Exalted notifies the servants of Hi s infinite power, which He demonstrated by creating what is bigger than that which they wonder ed about and whose possibility they discount,

ОҺ ЛҙП¬ Л° ЛҙПЁ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПЁ ЛҙО‘ ЛҙП’ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПӣ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛҙП— ЛёП® ЛҙП“ Л¶ЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙПӨ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ П°Лҙ ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ ЛёОҚ Пӯ ЛөО® ЛөПҲ ПЁ ЛҙПі ЛёПў ЛҙП  ЛҙП“ ЛҙОғ НҪ НјОҺЛҙ П¬Л°Нү ПЁНү ПіЛҙ ОҜЛҙ Пӯ (Have they not looked at the heaven above them, how We have made it and adorned it,) with lamps;

Нј ЛіОқ Пӯ ЛөО® ЛөП“ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лҙ Пҹ ОҺЛҙ ПЈЛҙ ПӯНҪ (and there are no Furuj in it) i.e., clefts, accord ing to Muj ahid. Others said that Furuj means,

rifts, or cracks. All these meanings are close to o ne another. Allah the Exalted and Most Honored said,

Л¶П– ЛёП  ЛҙО§ П°Л¶ П“ ПҜЛҙ О®Лҙ О— ОҺНү ПЈ ОҺ ЛұП— ОҺ ЛҙО’ Л¶Пғ ЛіО• ЛҙП® Л° ЛҙПӨ ЛҙОі ЛҙПҠ ЛёО’ ЛҙОі ЛҙП– ЛҙП  ЛҙО§ ПҜЛ¶ О¬Нү ПҹОҚНҪ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ПҜЛҙ О®Лҙ О— ЛёПһ ЛҙП« ЛҙО® ЛҙОј ЛҙО’ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶ПҠ Л¶Оҹ ЛёОӯ ОҺ ЛҙП“ ЛіО• ЛөП® Л° ЛҙП” ЛҙО— ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ Л¶ ПҰЛ°Лҙ ПӨЛё ОЈНү О®ПҹОҚ ЛіОӯ П® ЛөП„ ЛөП“- ЛҙПҡ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПҹ Л¶ОҮ ЛёОҗ Л¶П  ЛҙПҳ ПЁ ЛҙПі Л¶ПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО— НүО® ЛҙПӣ ЛҙО® ЛҙОј ЛҙО’ Пҹ ОҚ Л¶ПҠ Л¶Оҹ Оӯ ЛёОҚ НүПў ЛөОӣ ОҚНј ЛІО® Пҙ Л¶Оҙ ЛҙОЈ ЛҙП® ЛөП« ЛҙПӯ ОҺЛұ ОҢЛ¶ ОіОҺЛҙ О§ ЛөО® ЛҙОј ЛҙО’ Пҹ (Who has created the seven heavens one above another; you can see no fault in the creation of the Most Gracious. Then look again: "Can you see any rifts'' Then look again and yet again, your sight will return to you in a state of humiliati on and worn out.) (67:2-4) fatigued, unable to discover any imperfection or shortcomings. The statement of Allah the Exalted, the Blessed,

НјОҺЛҙ П¬Л°Лҙ П§Лё О©Лҙ ОӘЛҙ ПЈ ЛҙОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (And the earth! We have spread it out, ) me ans, ' We made it spacious and spread it out,'

НјЛҙ ПІЛ¶ ОіЛҙ ПӯЛҙ Оӯ ОҺЛҙ П¬ПҙЛ¶ П“ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ ПҳЛё ПҹЛҙ ОғЛҙ ПӯНҪ (and set thereon Rawasi standing firm.) whic h are the mountains to save the earth from shaking along with its inhabitants,

Нј ЛіОһ Пҙ Л¶П¬ ЛҙО‘ ЛіОқ ЛёПӯ ЛҙОҜ НӢПһ ЛөПӣ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ОҺЛҙ П¬ПҙЛ¶ П“ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ОҳЛҙ О’П§Лҙ ОғЛҙ ПӯНҪ (and We planted in it every lovely (Bahij ) pair.) e very kind and species of plant, fruit and vegetation,

Нј Лҙ ПҘПӯЛө О®Нү ПӣЛҙ О¬Лҙ О— ЛёПў ЛөПң НүП  ЛҙПҢ ЛҙПҹ Л¶ПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙОҹ ЛёПӯ ЛҙОҜ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҳЛҙ П Лҙ О§ ЛіЛҜ ЛёП° ЛҙО· НӢПһ ЛөПӣ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈЛҙ ПӯНҪ (And of everything We have created pairs that you may reflect.)(51:49) Allah's saying ' Bahij ',

meaning a beautiful scene,

Нј Лі ОҗПҙЛ¶ ПЁНҠ ПЈ ЛіОӘ ЛёО’ ЛҙПӢ НӢПһ ЛөПң Л¶Пҹ ПҜЛҙ О®Лё ПӣЛ¶ О«Лҙ Пӯ ЛұО“ ЛҙО® Л¶Ој ЛёО’ ЛҙО— НҪ (An insight and a Reminder for every servant who tu rns to Allah in repentance.) Allah says that observing the creation of the heavens and earth an d all the great things that He has placed in them provides insight, proof and a lesson for every penitent servant who submits in humbleness and repentance to Allah feeling fear, in awe of Him. Allah the Exalted said,

НјЛұ ОҺПӣЛҙ О®Л°Лҙ О’НҠ ПЈ ЛұЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙПЈ Л¶ ЛҜОӮЛҙ ПӨНү ОҙПҹОҚ ЛҙПҰ Л¶ПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҹНү О°Лҙ П§Лҙ ПӯНҪ (And We send down blessed water from the sky,) meaning beneficial,

НјЛі О–Л°Нү ПЁЛҙ Оҹ Л¶ПӘ Л¶О‘ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ОҳЛҙ О’П§Лҙ О„Лҙ П“НҪ (then We produce therewith Jannat), means sp ecial and public parks, gardens, etc.

Нј Л¶ОӘ Пҙ Л¶Ој ЛҙОӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ НүОҗ ЛҙОЈ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (and grain that are reaped) grains that are ha rvested for food and for storage for later use,

Нј ЛіО– Л° ЛҙПҳ Л¶Оҙ Л° ЛҙО‘ НҪ (And date palms Basiqat,) meaning, tall and high, a ccording to the explanation reported from Ibn ' Abbas, Muj ahid, ' Ikrimah, Al-Hasan, Qatadah and As-Suddi. Allah said,

НјЛІ ОӘПҙЛ¶ ПҖНү П§ ЛІПҠ ЛёП  ЛҙПғ ОҺЛҙ П¬Нү ПҹНҪ (with arrranged clusters.) producin g fruits arranged in clusters,

Нј Л¶О© ОҺ ЛҙО’ Л¶ПҢ ЛёП  НӢПҹ Лұ ОҺП—Лё ОҜНӢ ОӯНҪ (Aprovision for (Allah's) servants.) for (Allah's) creation,

НјЛұ ОҺОҳЛё ПҙНү ПЈ ЛұО“ ЛҙОӘ ЛёП  ЛҙО‘ Л¶ПӘ Л¶О‘ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ ПҙЛё ОЈЛҙ ОғЛҙ ПӯНҪ (And We give life ther ewith to a dead land.) this is the la nd that was barren. However, when rain falls upon it, it is stirred to life; it swe lls and produces all of the lovely pairs such as flowers and the like -- amazing on account of their beauty. All of this comes into existence after the land was without greenery, yet it was stirred back to life and became green. Indeed,

this should provide proof of Resurrection after dea th and disintegration; thus Allah resurrects the dead. This sign of Allah's ability that is s een and witnessed is greater than the denial of those who discount the possibility of Resurrection. Allah the Exalted and Most Honored said in other Ayat,

Л¶П– ЛёП  ЛҙО§ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ Лө О®Лҙ О’Л°Лё ПӣЛҙ Оғ Л¶ОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Л¶О• ЛҙП® Л° ЛҙПӨ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ ЛөП– ЛёП  ЛҙОЁ ЛҙПҹ НҪ НјЛ¶ ОұОҺНү ПЁПҹОҚ (The creation of the heavens and the earth is indeed greater than the creation of mankind;) (40:57),

НүПҘ ЛҙОғ ЛёОҚ ЛёПӯ ЛҙО® ЛҙПі ЛёПў ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙОғ НҪ Л¶О• ЛҙП® Л° ЛҙПӨ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙП– ЛҙП  ЛҙО§ ПҜЛ¶ О¬Нү ПҹОҚ ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ Л¶П° ЛёОӨ ЛөПі ПҘЛҙ Оғ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛіОӯ Л¶О© ОҺ ЛҙПҳ Л¶О‘ НүПҰ Л¶П¬ Л¶Пҳ ЛёП  ЛҙОЁ Л¶О‘ ЛҙП° ЛёПҢ ЛҙПі ЛёПў ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Нј ЛІО® Пі Л¶ОӘ ЛҙП— ЛіЛҜ ЛёП° ЛҙО· НӢПһ ЛөПӣ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛөПӘ НүП§ Л¶ОҮ П°Лҙ П Лҙ О‘ П°Лҙ О—Лё П®Лҙ ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ (Do they not see that Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth, and was not wearied by their creation, is able to give life to the dead Yes, He surely is Able to do all things.)(46:33) and,

ОҒЛҙ О«Л¶ ОҲЛҙ П“ ЛұО” ЛҙПҢ Л¶Оё Л° ЛҙО§ ЛҙОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ПҜЛҙ О®Лҙ О— ЛҙПҡ НүП§ ЛҙОғ Л¶ПӘ Л¶Оҳ Л° ЛҙПі ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛҙПӯ НҪ ОҺ ЛҙП¬ Л° ЛҙПҙ ЛёОЈ ЛҙОғ ПҜЛ¶ О¬Нү ПҹОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛёО– ЛҙО‘ ЛҙОӯ ЛҙПӯ ЛёО• НүО° ЛҙОҳ ЛёП« ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лё ПҙЛҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҹЛҙ О°П§Лҙ Оғ Л¶ОҮ П°Лҙ О—Лё П®Лҙ ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ Л¶П° ЛёОӨ ЛөПӨ ЛҙПҹНј ЛІО® Пі Л¶ОӘ ЛҙП— ЛіЛҜ ЛёП° ЛҙО· НӢПһ ЛөПӣ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛөПӘ НүП§ (And among His signs (is this), that you see the ea rth barren; but when We send down water to it, it is stirred to life and growth. Verily, He Wh o gives it life, s urely is able to give life to the dead. Indeed He is Able to do all things.)(41:39) Лө О©П®Лө ПӨЛҙ ОӣЛҙ Пӯ НӢОұ НүО® Пҹ ОҚ ЛөОҗ Л° ЛҙОӨ ЛёО» ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ ЛіОЎ П® ЛөП§ ЛөПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙП— ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛҙП  ЛёО’ ЛҙП— ЛёО– ЛҙО‘ НүО¬ ЛҙПӣ НҪЛіПҒ П® ЛөПҹ ЛөПҘ ЛҙП® ЛёО§ Л¶ОҮ ЛҙПӯ ЛөПҘ ЛёП® ЛҙПӢ ЛёО® Л¶П“ ЛҙПӯ ЛІО© ОҺ ЛҙПӢ ЛҙПӯ- Л¶О” ЛҙПң ЛёПі НҮП» ОҚ Лө ОҗЛ°Лҙ ОӨЛё О»Лҙ ОғЛҙ Пӯ

Л¶ОӘ Пҙ Л¶ПӢ ЛҙПӯ НүП– ЛҙОӨ ЛҙП“ ЛҙПһ ЛөОі НҠО® Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙОҸ НүО¬ ЛҙПӣ НҮПһ ЛөПӣ ЛіПҠ НүО’ НҠО— ЛөПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙП— ЛҙПӯ- ОҺЛҙ ПЁПҙЛ¶ ПҙЛҙ ПҢЛҙ П“Лҙ Оғ Л¶Пқ НүПӯ НҮП» ОҚ Л¶П– ЛёП  ЛҙОЁ ЛёПҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘Нј ЛіОӘ Пі Л¶ОӘ ЛҙОҹ ЛіП– ЛёП  ЛҙО§ ЛёПҰ НӢПЈ ЛіОІ ЛёО’ ЛҙПҹ П°Л¶ П“ ЛёПў ЛөП« ЛёПһ ЛҙО‘ (12. Denied before them the people of Nuh, and the Dwellers of Rass, and Thamud,) (13. And ' Ad, and Fir'awn, and the brethren of Lut,) (14. And the Dwellers of Al-Aykah, and the people of Tubba' . Everyone of them denied (their) Mess engers, so My threat took effect.) (15. Were We then tired with the first creation Nay, th ey are in confused doubt about a new creation.)

Reminding the Quraysh of the Destruction of earlier Disbelieving Nations Allah the Exalted warns the disbelievers of Qu raysh and reminds them of the punishment and painful torment that He sent in this life on their likes, who disbelieved before them. For instance, Allah the Exalted punished the people of Nuh by drowning them in the encompassing flood that touched all the people of earth. There is also the end that struck the people of ArRass, and we mentioned their story before in Surat Al-Furqan,

Нј ЛіПҒ П® ЛөПҹ ЛөПҘ ЛҙП® ЛёО§ Л¶ОҮ ЛҙПӯ ЛөПҘ ЛёП® ЛҙПӢ ЛёО® Л¶П“ ЛҙПӯ ЛІО© ОҺ ЛҙПӢ ЛҙПӯ ЛөО© П® ЛөПӨ ЛҙОӣ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (...and Thamud, and ' Ad, and Fir'awn, and the breth ren of Lut,) the people of Sadum (Sodom) and the surrounding areas, to whom Lut was se nt. Allah the Exalted shook the earth beneath them and turned their area into a reeking lake , as stinking as the disbelief, tyranny and defiance of the Truth that they had,

НјЛ¶ О”Лҙ ПңЛё ПіНҮ П»ОҚ Лө ОҗЛ°Лҙ ОӨЛё О»Лҙ ОғЛҙ ПӯНҪ (And the Dwellers of Al-Aykah), they are th e nation of Shu'ayb, peace be upon him,

НјЛі ПҠНү О’НҠ О— ЛөПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙП— ЛҙПӯ НҪ (and the people of Tubba' ), King of Yemen; we explained his story in the Tafsir of Surat AdDukhan, and therefore, we do not need to re peat it here. All praise is due to Allah.

НјЛҙ ПһЛө ОіНҠ О®ПҹОҚ ЛҙОҸ НүО¬ ЛҙПӣ НҮПһ ЛөПӣ НҪ (Everyone of them denied (their) Messengers,) means, all of these nations and their generations belied their respective Messenger, and whoever deni es even one Messenger, is as if he has denied all Messengers. Allah the Exalted and Most Honored said,

Нј Лҙ ПҰПҙЛ¶ П Лҙ ОіЛё О®Лө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ ЛіОЎ П® ЛөП§ ЛөПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙП— ЛёО– ЛҙО‘ НүО¬ ЛҙПӣ НҪ (The people of Nuh denied the Messengers.)(26:105) even though only one Messenger was sent to them, and indeed, if all the Messengers were sent to them, they would have disbelieved in them as well. Allah said,

Нј Л¶ОӘ Пҙ Л¶ПӢ ЛҙПӯ НүП– ЛҙОӨ ЛҙП“ НҪ (so My threat took effect.) meaning, the promise of torment and punishment that Allah delivered to them on account of their denial took e ffect. Therefore, all those who fear suffering the same end should be warned, es pecially since the latte r have denied their Messenger, j ust as the former denied their Messenger.

Repeating the Creation is Easier than originating It Allah the Exalted said,

ЛҙОЁ ЛёПҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ОҺЛҙ ПЁПҙЛ¶ ПҙЛҙ ПҢЛҙ П“Лҙ ОғНҪНјЛ¶ ПқНү ПӯНҮ П»ОҚ Л¶П– ЛёП  (Were We then tired with the first creation) mean ing, ' did starting the creation bring fatigue to Us, so as to cause doubt in them that repeating the creation was not possible'

Нј ЛіОӘ Пі Л¶ОӘ ЛҙОҹ ЛіП– ЛёП  ЛҙО§ ЛёПҰ НӢПЈ ЛіОІ ЛёО’ ЛҙПҹ П°Л¶ П“ ЛёПў ЛөП« ЛёПһ ЛҙО‘ НҪ (Nay, they are in confused doubt about a new creation.) meaning, ' starting the creation did not tire Us, and repeating it is even easier .' Allah the Exalted and Most Honored said,

ЛөПҘ ЛҙП® ЛёП« ЛҙОғ ЛҙП® ЛөП« ЛҙПӯ ЛөП© ЛөОӘ Пҙ Л¶ПҢ ЛөПі НүПў ЛөОӣ ЛҙП– ЛёП  ЛҙОЁ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөОғ ЛҙОӘ ЛёО’ ЛҙПі ПҜЛ¶ О¬Нү ПҹОҚ ЛҙП® ЛөП« ЛҙПӯ НҪ НјЛ¶ ПӘЛё ПҙЛҙ П Лҙ ПӢ (And He it is Who originates the creation, th en He will repeat it; and this is easier for Him.)(30:27), and,

Л¶П° ОӨ ЛөПі ПҰЛҙ ПЈ ЛҙПқ ОҺ ЛҙП— ЛөПӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛёП  ЛҙО§ ЛҙП° Л¶Оҙ ЛҙП§ ЛҙПӯ ЛұПј ЛҙОң ЛҙПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛҙ Пҹ ЛҙОҸ ЛҙО® ЛҙОҝ ЛҙПӯ НҪ Нј ЛІ ПўПҙЛ¶ ПЈЛҙ Оӯ ЛҙП° Л¶П« ЛҙПӯ Лҙ ПўЛ°Лҙ ПҲЛ¶ ПҢЛё ПҹОҚ ЛіП– ЛёП  ЛҙО§ Л·Пһ ЛөПң Л¶О‘ ЛҙП® ЛөП« ЛҙПӯ ЛіО“ НүО® ЛҙПЈ ЛҙПқ НүПӯ ЛҙОғ ОӮЛҙ П«Лҙ О„Лҙ ОёП§Лҙ Оғ ПҜЛ¶ О¬Нү ПҹОҚ ОҺЛҙ П¬ПҙЛ¶ ПҙЛё ОӨЛө Пі ЛёПһ ЛөП— ЛІПў Пҙ Л¶П  ЛҙПӢ-Нј (And he puts forth for Us a parable, and forgets his own creation. He says: "Who will give life to these bones after they are rotten and have become dus t'' Say: "H e will g ive life to them Who created them for the first time! And He is the All-Knower of every creation!'')(36:78-79) aWe mentioned earlier a Hadith collected in the Sahih,

В»ЛөПқ П® ЛөПҳ ЛҙПі ЛҙПЎ ЛҙО© ОҒ ЛөПҰ ЛёО‘ ОҚ ПІЛ¶ ПЁПіЛ¶ О«Лё ОҶЛө Пі П° ЛҙПҹ ОҺ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙО— ЛөНҝ ОҚ ЛөПқ П® ЛөПҳ ЛҙПі : ЛёПҰ ЛҙПҹ ПІЛ¶ П§Лҙ ОғЛҙ ОӘЛҙ О‘ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛҙ Пӣ ПІЛ¶ П§Лҙ ОӘПҙЛ¶ ПҢЛө Пі . ЛҙПҘ ЛҙП® ЛёП« ЛҙО„ Л¶О‘ Л¶П– ЛёП  ЛҙОЁ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөПқ НүПӯ ЛҙОғ ЛҙОІ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ ПӘ Л¶О— ЛҙО© ОҺ ЛҙПӢ Л¶ОҮ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ НүПІ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢВ« (Allah the Exalted said, "Son of ' Adam insults Me when he says, ' He will not resurrect me as He created me before!' But surely, originating creatio n is not easier for Me than reproducing it again.'') ЛҙП§ Л¶ПӘ Л¶О‘ ЛөОұ Л¶П® ЛёОі ЛҙП® ЛөО— ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ЛөПў ЛҙП  ЛёПҢ ЛҙП§ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙПҰ Л° ЛҙОҙ П§ Л¶П№ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҳЛҙ П Лҙ О§ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ НҪ ЛөПӘ ЛөОҙ ЛёП” Л¶ОӘ Пі Л¶Оӯ ЛҙП® ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶Пһ ЛёО’ ЛҙОЈ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ Л¶ПӘ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПҹ Л¶ОҮ ЛөОҸ ЛҙО® ЛёП— ЛҙОғ ЛөПҰ ЛёОӨ ЛҙП§ ЛҙПӯ- П°Нү ПҳЛҙ П Лҙ ОҳЛҙ Пі ЛёО« Л¶ОҮ ЛІ ОӘПҙЛ¶ ПҢЛҙ П— Л¶ ПқОҺЛҙ ПӨНӢ ОёПҹОҚ Л¶ПҰ ЛҙПӢ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ПҰ Пҙ Л¶ПӨ ЛҙПҙ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶ПҰ ЛҙПӢ Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ПҙНӢ ПҳЛҙ П Лҙ ОҳЛө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ- ОҺНү ПЈ ЛІ ОӘПҙЛ¶ ОҳЛҙ ПӢ ЛІОҗ Пҙ Л¶П— ЛҙОӯ Л¶ПӘ ЛёПі ЛҙОӘ ЛҙПҹ НүП» Л¶ОҮ ЛіПқ ЛёП® ЛҙП— ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ЛөПҶ Л¶П” ЛёП  ЛҙПі- ЛёО• ЛҙЛҜ ОҺ ЛҙОҹ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ПЈ ЛҙО– ПЁ ЛөПӣ ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ЛҙПҡ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙО« НӢП– ЛҙОӨ ЛёПҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ Л¶О• ЛёП® ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөО“ ЛҙО® ЛёПң ЛҙОі ЛөОӘ Пҙ Л¶ОӨ ЛҙО— ЛөПӘ ЛёПЁЛ¶ОӘ Пҙ Л¶ПӢ ЛҙП® ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі ЛҙПҡ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙО« Л¶Оӯ П® НҠОј Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ЛҙОҰ Л¶П” ЛөП§ ЛҙПӯ- НҠПһ ЛөПӣ ЛёО• ЛҙЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙОҹ ЛҙПӯ

ЛІ ОӘПҙЛ¶ П¬Лҙ О·Лҙ Пӯ ЛІП– Л¶ОӢ ОӮ ЛҙОі ОҺЛҙ П¬Лҙ ПҢНү ПЈ ЛіОІ ЛёП” ЛҙП§- ЛёПҰ НӢПЈ ЛіО” ЛҙП  ЛёП” ЛҙПҸ П°Л¶ П“ ЛҙО– ПЁ ЛөПӣ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ НүПҹ Нј ЛІОӘ Пі Л¶ОӘ ЛҙОЈ ЛҙПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПҙ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҷ ЛөО® ЛҙОј ЛҙО’ ЛҙП“ ЛҙПҷ ЛҙЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙП„ Л¶ПҸ ЛҙПҡ ПЁ ЛҙПӢ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё П”Лҙ ОёЛҙ ПңЛҙ П“ ОҚЛҙ О¬Л°Лҙ П« (16. And indeed We have created man, and We know what his self whispers to him. And We are nearer to him than his j ugular vein.) (17. (Remem ber) that the two receivers (recording angels) receive (each human being), one sitting on the righ t and one on the left.) (18. Not a word does he (or she) utter but there is a watcher by h im ready (to record it).) (19. And the stupor of death will come in truth: "This is what you have been avoiding!'') (20. And the Trumpet will be blown -- that will be the Day of the threat.) (21. And every person will come forth along with a S a'iq and a Shahid.) (22. (It will be said to th e sinners): "Indeed you were heedless of this. Now We have removed from you, your covering, and sharp is your sight this Day!'')

Allah encompasses and watches all of Man's Activity Allah the Exalted affirms His absolute dominance ov er mankind, being their Creator and the Knower of everything about them. Allah the Ex alted has complete knowledge of all thoughts that cross the mind of man, be they good or ev il. In the Sahih, the Messenger of Allah said,

В» ОҺЛҙ П¬Лҙ ОҙЛө П”Лё П§Лҙ Оғ Л¶ПӘ Л¶О‘ ЛёО– ЛҙОӣ НүОӘ ЛҙОЈ ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ПІЛ¶ ОҳНү ПЈЛө О„Л¶ Пҹ ЛҙОҜ ЛҙПӯ ОҺ ЛҙО  ЛҙО— П° ЛҙПҹ ОҺ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙО— ЛҙНҝ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ПһЛҙ ПӨЛё ПҢЛҙ О— ЛёПӯ ЛҙОғ ЛёПһ ЛөПҳ ЛҙО— ЛёПў ЛҙПҹ ОҺЛҙ ПЈВ« (Verily, Allah the Exalted has forgiven my Um mah (Muslims) for what they talk (think) to themselves about, as long as they do not u tter or implement it.) The statement of Allah the Exalted,

Нј Л¶ОӘ Пі Л¶Оӯ ЛҙП® ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶Пһ ЛёО’ ЛҙОЈ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ Л¶ПӘ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПҹ Л¶ОҮ ЛөОҸ ЛҙО® ЛёП— ЛҙОғ ЛөПҰ ЛёОӨ ЛҙП§ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (And We are nearer to him than his j ugular vein .) means, His angels are nearer to man than his j ugular vein. Those who explained ' We' in the Ayah to mean ' Our knowledge,' have done so to avoid falling into the idea of incarnation or indwe lling; but these two creeds are false according to the consensus of Muslims. Allah is pr aised and glorified, He is far hallowed beyond what they ascribe to Him. The words of this Ayah do not need this explanation (that ' We'

refers to ' Allah's knowledge'), fo r Allah did not say, ' and Iam closer to him than hi s j ugular vein.' Rather, He said,

Нј Л¶ОӘ Пі Л¶Оӯ ЛҙП® ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶Пһ ЛёО’ ЛҙОЈ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ Л¶ПӘ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПҹ Л¶ОҮ ЛөОҸ ЛҙО® ЛёП— ЛҙОғ ЛөПҰ ЛёОӨ ЛҙП§ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (And We are nearer to him than his j ugular vein. ) j ust as He said in the case of dying persons,

Нј ЛҙПҘ Пӯ ЛөО® Л¶Ој ЛёО’ ЛөО— НүП» ПҰ Л¶Пң Л° ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў ЛөПң ПЁ Л¶ПЈ Л¶ПӘ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПҹ Л¶ОҮ ЛөОҸ ЛҙО® ЛёП— ЛҙОғ ЛөПҰ ЛёОӨ ЛҙП§ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (But We are nearer to him than you, but you se e not.) (56:85), in reference to His angels (who take the souls). Allah the Exalted the Blessed said ,

Нј ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөПҲ Л¶П” Л° ЛҙОӨ ЛҙПҹ ЛөПӘ ЛҙПҹ ОҺНү П§Л¶ ОҮЛҙ Пӯ ЛҙО® ЛёПӣ НӢО¬ Пҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҹНү О°Лҙ П§ ЛөПҰ ЛёОӨ ЛҙП§ ОҺНү П§Л¶ ОҮНҪ (Verily, We, it is We Who have sent down the Dhikr and surely, We will guard it.) (15:9) Therefore, the angels brought down the revela tion, the Qur'an, by the leave of Allah, the Exalted, the Most Honored. Thus, the angels ar e closer to man than his own j ugular vein, by the power and leave of Allah. Consequently, th e angel touches mankind, j ust as the devil touches them, for the devil is as clo s e to them a s the blo o d tha t runs in their vein s; jus t a s Allah's Prophet , who is truthful and was told the truth, told us. Allah's statement here,

Нј Л¶ПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПҙ НӢПҳ ЛҙП  ЛҙОҳ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ П°Нү ПҳЛҙ П Лҙ ОҳЛҙ Пі ЛёО« Л¶ОҮ НҪ ((Remember) that the two receivers receive,) in reference to the two angels who receive and record the deeds of mankind.

Нј ЛІОӘ Пҙ Л¶ПҢ ЛҙП— Л¶ ПқОҺЛҙ ПӨНӢ ОёПҹОҚ Л¶ПҰ ЛҙПӢ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ ПҰПҙЛ¶ ПӨЛҙ ПҙЛё ПҹОҚ Л¶ПҰ ЛҙПӢ НҪ (one sitting on the right and one on the left.) mea ns ready to note,

НјЛө ПҶЛ¶ П”Лё П Лҙ Пі ОҺНү ПЈНҪ (whatever he utters), in reference to the human,

НјЛі ПқЛё П®Лҙ П— ПҰЛ¶ ПЈНҪ (of a word), means any word that he or she speaks,

Нј ЛІОӘ Пҙ Л¶Оҳ ЛҙПӢ ЛІОҗ Пҙ Л¶П— ЛҙОӯ Л¶ПӘ ЛёПі ЛҙОӘ ЛҙПҹ НүП» Л¶ОҮ НҪ (but there is a watcher by him ready.) means, bu t there is a scribe whose j ob is to record it,

leaving no word or movement unr ecorded. Allah the Exalted said,

ЛҙПҰ Пҙ Л¶ПҲ Л¶П” Л° ЛҙОӨ ЛҙПҹ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛҙПӯ НҪ-Л¶Пӣ Лҙ ПҰПҙЛ¶ О’Л¶ ОҳЛ°Лҙ Пӣ ЛұОҺ ПЈ ОҚ ЛҙО®- Лҙ ПҘП®Лө ПӨЛҙ П Лё ПҢЛҙ Пі Нј Лҙ ПҘП®Лө П Лҙ ПҢЛё П”Лҙ О— ОҺЛҙ ПЈ (But verily, over you (are appointed angels) to watch you, Kiraman (hon orable) Katibin writing down (your deeds), they know all that you do.) (82:10-12) Therefore, the scribe records every word that is spoken, according to the explanation of Al-Hasan and Qatadah. This is also the apparent meaning of this Ayah. Imam Ahmad reco rded that Bilal bin Al-Harith Al-Muzani said,

"The Messenger of Allah said,

В»ЛҙП® ЛёОҝ Л¶Оӯ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ Л¶О” ЛҙПӨ Л¶П  ЛҙПң ЛёПҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ЛөПў НүП  ЛҙПң ЛҙОҳ ЛҙПҙ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПһ ЛөОҹ НүО® Пҹ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ Л¶Нҝ ОҚ Л¶ПҘ ОҚ НүО° ЛҙПӢ ЛөНҝ ОҚ ЛөОҗ ЛөОҳ ЛёПң ЛҙПі ЛёО– ЛҙПҗ ЛҙП  ЛҙО‘ ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ЛҙПҺ ЛөП  ЛёО’ ЛҙО— ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ НҠПҰ ЛөПҲ ЛҙПі ОҺЛҙ ПЈ П° ЛҙПҹ ОҺ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙО— ЛҙПһ ЛөОҹ НүО® Пҹ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛҙПӯ Л¬ ЛөП© ОҺ ЛҙПҳ ЛёП  ЛҙПі Л¶ПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі П°Лҙ ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ ЛөПӘ ЛҙП§ ОҚ ЛҙП® ЛёОҝ Л¶Оӯ ОҺЛҙ П¬Л¶ О‘ ЛөПӘ ЛҙПҹ НүПһ ЛҙОҹ ЛҙПӯ ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ НҠПҰ ЛөПҲ ЛҙПі ОҺЛҙ ПЈ П° ЛҙПҹ ОҺ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙО— Л¶Нҝ ОҚ Л¶ПӮ ЛҙОЁ ЛҙОі ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ Л¶О” ЛҙПӨ Л¶П  ЛҙПң ЛёПҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ЛөПў НүП  ЛҙПң ЛҙОҳ ЛҙПҙ ЛҙПҹ ЛөОҗ ЛөОҳ ЛёПң ЛҙПі Л¬Лё О–Лҙ ПҗЛҙ П Лҙ О‘ ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ЛҙПҺ ЛөП  ЛёО’ ЛҙО— ЛөПӘ ЛҙП„ ЛҙОЁ ЛҙОі ОҺЛҙ П¬Л¶ О‘ Л¶ПӘ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ П° ЛҙПҹ ОҺ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙО— ЛөНҝ ОҚ П© ОҺ ЛҙПҳ ЛёП  ЛҙПі Л¶ПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі П°Лҙ ПҹЛ¶ ОҮВ« (Verily, a man might utter a word that pleases Allah the Exalted, unaware of how highly it will be regarded, and on its account Allah the Exal ted and Most Honored decrees His pleasure of him until the Day he meets Him. Aman might indeed utter a word that angers Allah the Exalted, unaware of how dreadful it will be an d on its account Allah the Exalted decrees for him His anger until the Day he meets Him.)'' ' Alqam ah used to say, "How many words did Inot utter because of the Hadith that Bilal bin Al-Harith narrated.'' At-Tirmidhi, An-Nasa'i and Ibn Maj ah collected this Hadith. At-Tirmidhi said, "Has an Sahih.'' There is a Hadith similar to this in the Sahih.
