Л¶Пҹ ЛҙПҡ Л¶П§ ОҺ ЛҙОҙ ЛҙП  Л¶О‘ Лө ПӘЛ°Лҙ П§Лё О®Нү ОҙЛҙ Пі ОҺЛҙ ПӨНү П§Л¶ ОҲЛҙ П“НҪ Л¶ПӘ Л¶О‘ ЛҙОӯ Л¶О¬ ЛёПЁ ЛөО— ЛҙПӯ ЛҙПҰ Пҙ Л¶Пҳ НүОҳ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶ПӘ Л¶О‘ ЛҙО® НӢОё ЛҙО’ ЛөОҳ Нј ЛұОҚ Л·ОӘ НҠПҹ Лұ ОҺПЈЛё П®Лҙ П— ( S o W e h a v e m a d e th is ( th e Q u r' a n ) e a s y in your own tongue, only th at you may give glad tidings to those who have Taqwa and warn with it the most quarrels ome people.)(19:97) Allah said,

ЛҙП¬ ЛҙП“ НҪНјЛі О®Л¶ ПӣНү ОӘНҠ ПЈ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ЛёПһ (then is there any that will remember), meaning, ' i s there anyone who will remember through this Qur'an, which We made easy to memorize and eas y to understand' Muhammad bin Ka'b AlQurazi commented on this Ayah, "Is there anyone who will avoid evil''

Л¶Оӯ ЛөО¬ ЛөП§ ЛҙПӯ П° Л¶О‘ ОҚ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӢ ЛҙПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПӣ ЛҙП’ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПң ЛҙП“ ЛІО© ОҺ ЛҙПӢ ЛёО– ЛҙО‘ НүО¬ ЛҙПӣ НҪ- ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё П Лҙ ОіЛё ОӯЛҙ Оғ ОӮНү П§Л¶ ОҮ НҲО® Л¶ПӨ ЛҙОҳ ЛёОҙ НҠПЈ ЛіОІ ЛёОӨ ЛҙП§ Л¶ПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі П°Л¶ П“ Лұ ОҚО®Лҙ О»Лё О®Лҙ О» ЛұОҺ ОӨ Пі Л¶Оӯ ЛёПў Л¶П¬ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢЛҙП’ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПң ЛҙП“ ЛіО® Л¶ПҢ ЛҙПҳ ПЁ НҠПЈ ЛіПһ ЛёОЁ ЛҙП§ ЛөОҜ ОҺ ЛҙО  ЛёПӢ ЛҙОғ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ НүП§ ЛҙО„ ЛҙПӣ ЛҙОұ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ ЛөПү Л¶О° ПЁ ЛҙО—

ЛҙП§ ЛёО® НүОҙ ЛҙПі ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ Л¶Оӯ ЛөО¬ ЛөП§ ЛҙПӯ П° Л¶О‘ ОҚ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӢ ЛҙПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПӣ ЛёПһ ЛҙП¬ ЛҙП“ Л¶О® ЛёПӣ НӢО¬ П  Л¶Пҹ Лҙ ПҘОҚЛҙ ЛҜЛё О®Лө ПҳЛё ПҹОҚ ОҺ Нј ЛіО® Л¶Пӣ НүОӘ НҠПЈ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ (18. ' Ad denied; then how was My torment and My warnings) (19. Verily, We sent against them a violently cold (Sarsar) wind on a day of calamit y, continuous.) (20. Plucking out men as if they were uprooted stems of date palms.) (2 1. Then, how was My torment and My warnings) (22. And We have indeed made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember; then is there any that will remember)

The Story of ' Ad Allah states that ' Ad, the People of Hud, denied their Messenger, just as the people of Nuh did.

S o, Allah sent on them,

Нј ЛұОҚ О® ЛҙО» ЛёО® ЛҙО» ЛұОҺ ОӨ Пі Л¶Оӯ ЛёПў Л¶П¬ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ НҪ (against them a violently cold (Sarsar) wind), means, a bitterly cold and furious wind,

НјЛі ОІЛё ОӨЛҙ П§ Л¶ПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі П°Л¶ П“НҪ (on a day of calamity), agai nst them, according to Ad-Dahhak, Qatadah and As-Suddi,

НјНҮ О®Л¶ ПӨЛҙ ОҳЛё ОҙНҠ ПЈНҪ (continuous), upon them because the calamity, to rment and destruction that they suffered in this life on that day continued with that of the He reafter,

ПЁНҠ ПЈ ЛіПһ ЛёОЁ ЛҙП§ ЛөОҜ ОҺ ЛҙО  ЛёПӢ ЛҙОғ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ НүП§ ЛҙО„ ЛҙПӣ ЛҙОұ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ Лө ПүЛ¶ О°ПЁЛҙ О—НҪНј ЛіО® Л¶ПҢ ЛҙПҳ (Plucking out men as if they were uprooted stem s of date palms.) The wind would pluck one of them and raise him high, until he could no longer be seen, and then violently send him down on his head to the ground. His head would be smashed and only his body would be left, headless,

П° Л¶О‘ ОҚ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӢ ЛҙПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПӣ ЛҙП’ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПң ЛҙП“ ЛіО® Л¶ПҢ ЛҙПҳ ПЁ НҠПЈ ЛіПһ ЛёОЁ ЛҙП§ ЛөОҜ ОҺ ЛҙО  ЛёПӢ ЛҙОғ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ НүП§ ЛҙО„ ЛҙПӣ НҪ ЛіО® Л¶Пӣ НүОӘ НҠПЈ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ЛёПһ ЛҙП¬ ЛҙП“ Л¶О® ЛёПӣ НӢО¬ П  Л¶Пҹ Лҙ ПҘОҚЛҙ ЛҜЛё О®Лө ПҳЛё ПҹОҚ ОҺЛҙ П§Лё О®Нү ОҙЛҙ Пі ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ Л¶Оӯ ЛөО¬ ЛөП§ ЛҙПӯ Нј (a s if th e y we re u pro o te d s te ms o f da te pa lms . T h e n, h o w wa s M y to rme n t a n d M y wa rn in g s And We have indeed made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember; then is there any that will remember) Л¶Оӯ ЛөО¬ НҠПЁ Пҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ Лө О©П®Лө ПӨЛҙ Оӣ ЛёО– ЛҙО‘ НүО¬ ЛҙПӣ НҪ- ЛұОҚ ОӘ Л¶ОЈ ЛҙПӯ ОҺНү ПЁНӢ ПЈ Лұ ОҚО®Лҙ ОёЛҙ О‘Лҙ Оғ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөПҹ ОҺ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙП“ ЛіО® ЛөПҢ ЛөОі ЛҙПӯ Лі ПһЛ°Лҙ П Лҙ Оҝ П°Л¶ П”Нү Пҹ ЛұОҚ О« Л¶ОҮ ОӮНү П§Л¶ ОҮ ЛөПӘ ЛөПҢ Л¶О’ НүОҳ НүП§-ЛёПӣ НӢО¬ Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙП° Л¶Пҳ ЛёПҹ ЛөЛҜ ЛҙОғ ЛөО® ЛІО® Л¶О· ЛҙОғ ЛІОҸ ОҚ НүО¬ ЛҙПӣ ЛҙП® ЛөП« ЛёПһ ЛҙО‘ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛ¶ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ О‘ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ Л¶ПӘ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ- ЛұОҚ ОӘ ЛҙПҸ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө ПӨЛҙ П Лё ПҢЛҙ ПҙЛҙ Оі ЛөО® Л¶О· НҮП» ОҚ ЛөОҸ ОҚ НүО¬ ЛҙПң ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶ПҰ НүПЈ- ЛёПў ЛөП¬ НүПҹ ЛұО” ЛҙПЁ ЛёОҳ Л¶П“ Л¶О” ЛҙП— ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ Лё ОҚП®Лө П Л¶ ОіЛё О®Лө ПЈ ОҺНү П§Л¶ ОҮ ЛёО® Л¶О’ ЛҙП„ ЛёО» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛёО’ Л¶Пҳ ЛҙО— ЛёОӯ ОҺ ЛҙП“- ЛІО” ЛҙПӨ ЛёОҙ Л¶П— ЛҙЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ НүПҘ ЛҙОғ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛёОҢ НӢО’ ЛҙП§ ЛҙПӯ ЛІО® ЛҙПҖ ЛҙОҳ ЛёОӨ НҠПЈ ЛіОҸ ЛёО® Л¶О· НҠПһ ЛөПӣ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛҙПЁ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘- ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛҙО’ Л¶ОӨ Л° ЛҙО» ЛёОҚ ЛёПӯ ЛҙО© ОҺ ЛҙПЁ ЛҙП“ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё П Лҙ ОіЛё ОӯЛҙ Оғ ОӮНү П§Л¶ ОҮ Л¶Оӯ ЛөО¬ ЛөП§ ЛҙПӯ П° Л¶О‘ ОҚ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӢ ЛҙПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПӣ ЛҙП’ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПң ЛҙП“ ЛҙО® ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙП“ П° ЛҙПғ ОҺ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙОҳ ЛҙП“ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ Л¶О® Л¶ПҲ ЛҙОҳ ЛёОӨ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶ ПўПҙЛ¶ ОёЛҙ П¬Лҙ Пӣ Лё ОҚП®Лө П§ОҺЛҙ ПңЛҙ П“ ЛұО“ ЛҙОӘ Л¶ОЈ ЛҙПӯ ЛұО” ЛҙОӨ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО» ЛёПў Л¶П¬ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ Нј ЛіО® Л¶Пӣ НүОӘ НҠПЈ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ЛёПһ ЛҙП¬ ЛҙП“ Л¶О® ЛёПӣ НӢО¬ П  Л¶Пҹ Лҙ ПҘОҚЛҙ ЛҜЛё О®Лө ПҳЛё ПҹОҚ ОҺЛҙ П§Лё О®Нү ОҙЛҙ Пі i(23. Thamud denied the warnings.) (24. And th ey said: "Aman, alone among us -- shall we follow him Truly, then we should be in error and di stress!'') (25. "Is it that the Reminder is sent to him alone from among us Nay, he is an insolent liar!'') (26. Tomorrow they will come to know who is the liar, the insolent one!) (27. Verily, We are sending the she-camel as a test for them.

S o watch them, and be patient!) (28. And inform the m that the water is to be shared between (her and) them, each one's right to drink bein g established (by turns).) (29. But they called their comrade and he took (a sword) and kille d (her).) (30. Then, how was My torment and My warnings) (31. Verily, We sent against them a single Sayhah, and they became like straw AlMuhtazir.) (32. And indeed, We have made the Qu r'an easy to understand and remember; then

is there any that will remember)

The Story of Thamud Allah states here that the people of Thamud denied their Messenger Salih,

Л°Лҙ П Лҙ Оҝ П°Л¶ П”Нү Пҹ ЛұОҚ О« Л¶ОҮ ОӮНү П§Л¶ ОҮ ЛөПӘ ЛөПҢ Л¶О’ НүОҳ НүП§ ЛұОҚ ОӘ Л¶ОЈ ЛҙПӯ ОҺНү ПЁНӢ ПЈ Лұ ОҚО®Лҙ ОёЛҙ О‘Лҙ Оғ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөПҹ ОҺ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙП“ НҪ ЛіПһ Нј ЛіО® ЛөПҢ ЛөОі ЛҙПӯ (And they said: "Aman, alone among us -- shall we follow him Truly, then we should be in error and distress!'') They said, ' We would have earned failure and loss if we all submitted to a man from among us.' They were amazed that the Reminder was sent to him alone among them, and therefore, accused hi m of being a liar,

НјЛІ О®Л¶ О·Лҙ Оғ ЛІОҸ ОҚ НүО¬ ЛҙПӣ ЛҙП® ЛөП« ЛёПһ ЛҙО‘ НҪ (Nay, he is an insolent liar!), means, he has tres spassed the limits in his lies. Allah the Exalted responded,

НүО¬ ЛҙПң ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶ПҰ НүПЈ ЛұОҚ ОӘ ЛҙПҸ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө ПӨЛҙ П Лё ПҢЛҙ ПҙЛҙ ОіНҪНј ЛөО® Л¶О· НҮП» ОҚ ЛөОҸ ОҚ (Tomorrow they will come to know who is the liar, t he insolent one!), thus warning and threatening them and delivering a sure promise to t hem,

НјЛё ПўЛө П¬Нү Пҹ ЛұО” ЛҙПЁ ЛёОҳ Л¶П“ Л¶О” ЛҙП— ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ Лё ОҚП®Лө П Л¶ ОіЛё О®Лө ПЈ ОҺНү П§Л¶ ОҮНҪ (Verily, We are sending the she-camel as a test for them.) To test and try the people of Thamud, Allah sent to them a superb, pregnant female camel that emerged from solid rock,

according to their request, so that it would become a proof against them from Allah, the Exalted. Thereafter, they were supposed to beli e v e in w h a t w a s b ro u g h t to th e m b y S a lih ,

peace be upon him. Allah ordered His servant and Messenger Salih,

НјЛё О®Л¶ О’Лҙ П„Лё О»ОҚЛҙ Пӯ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛёО’ Л¶Пҳ ЛҙО— ЛёОӯ ОҺ ЛҙП“ НҪ (S o watch them, and be patient !) Allah commanded, ' await, O Salih, and see what will become of them and be patient; verily the better end wi ll be yours and you will have success in this life and the Hereafter,'

НјЛё ПўЛө П¬Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ О‘ ЛІО” ЛҙПӨ ЛёОҙ Л¶П— ЛҙЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ НүПҘ ЛҙОғ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛёОҢ НӢО’ ЛҙП§ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (And inform them that the water is to be sh ared between them) one day for her to drink and one day for them to drink,

Лі ПЎП®Лө П Лё ПҢНү ПЈ ЛіПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі ЛөОҸ ЛёО® Л¶О· ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ ЛІОҸ ЛёО® Л¶О· ОҺЛҙ П¬Нү Пҹ ЛІО” ЛҙП— ОҺ ЛҙП§ Л¶П© Л¶О¬ Л° ЛҙП« ЛҙПқ ОҺ ЛҙП— НҪ Нј (He said: "Here is a she-camel: it has a right to drink, and you have a right to drin k (water) on a day, known.'') (26:155) Allah's statement,

ЛіОҸ ЛёО® Л¶О· НҠПһ ЛөПӣ НҪНјЛІ О®Лҙ ПҖЛҙ ОҳЛё ОӨНҠ ПЈ (each one's right to drink being established.) Mu j ahid said, "When she did not drink, they would drink the water, and when she drank, they would dri nk her milk.'' Allah the Exalted said;

Нј ЛҙО® ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙП“ П°Лҙ ПғОҺЛҙ ПҢЛҙ ОҳЛҙ П“ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛҙО’ Л¶ОӨ Л° ЛҙО» ЛёОҚ ЛёПӯ ЛҙО© ОҺ ЛҙПЁ ЛҙП“ НҪ (But they called their comrade and he took and ki lled.) According to the Scholars of Tafsir, his name was Qudar bin Salif; he was the evilest among them,

Нј ОҺ ЛҙП¬ Л° ЛҙПҳ ЛёО· ЛҙОғ ЛҙОҡ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙО’ П§ ОҚ Л¶О« Л¶ОҮ НҪ (When the most wicked man among them went fo rth (to kill the she-camel).)(91:12) Allah said here,

НјП°Лҙ ПғОҺЛҙ ПҢЛҙ ОҳЛҙ П“НҪ (and he took) meaning to harm,

Нј Л¶Оӯ ЛөО¬ ЛөП§ ЛҙПӯ П° Л¶О‘ ОҚ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӢ ЛҙПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПӣ ЛҙП’ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПң ЛҙП“ ЛҙО® ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙП“ НҪ (and killed (her). Then, how was My torment and My warnings), ' Itormented them, so how was the torment Isent on them because of their disbe- lief in Me and denying My Messenger'

Л¶ ПўПҙЛ¶ ОёЛҙ П¬Лҙ Пӣ Лё ОҚП®Лө П§ОҺЛҙ ПңЛҙ П“ ЛұО“ ЛҙОӘ Л¶ОЈ ЛҙПӯ ЛұО” ЛҙОӨ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО» ЛёПў Л¶П¬ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё П Лҙ ОіЛё ОӯЛҙ Оғ ОӮНү П§Л¶ ОҮНҪ Нј Л¶О® Л¶ПҲ ЛҙОҳ ЛёОӨ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ (Verily, We sent against them a single Sayhah, and they became like straw Al-Muhtazir.) They all perished and none of them remained. They were n o more, they died out, just as plants and grass dry and die out. As-Suddi said that they be came like the dry grass in the desert when it becomes burned and the wind scatters it all ab out. Ibn Zayd said, "The Arabs used to erect fences (Hizar, from which the word, Al-Muhtazir, is derived) made of dried bushes, around their camels and cattle, so Allah said,

НјЛ¶ О®Л¶ ПҲЛҙ ОҳЛё ОӨЛө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ Л¶ ПўПҙЛ¶ ОёЛҙ П¬Лҙ ПӣНҪ (like straw Al-Muhtazir.) Л¶Оӯ ЛөО¬ НҠПЁ Пҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ЛіПҒ П® ЛөПҹ ЛөПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙП— ЛёО– ЛҙО‘ НүО¬ ЛҙПӣ НҪ- ЛёПў Л¶П¬ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё П Лҙ ОіЛё ОӯЛҙ Оғ ОӮНү П§Л¶ ОҮ ЛҙПқ ОҒ НүП» Л¶ОҮ ЛұОҺ О’ Л¶Ој Л° ЛҙОЈ ЛіО® ЛҙОӨ ЛҙОҙ Л¶О‘ Пў ЛөП¬ Л° ЛҙПЁ ЛёПҙ НүО  НүП§ ЛіПҒ П® ЛөПҹ - ЛёПҰ НӢПЈ ЛұО” ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҢ НӢП§ ЛҙО® ЛҙПң ЛҙО· ПҰЛҙ ПЈ ПҜЛ¶ О°Лё О Лҙ П§ ЛҙПҡ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӣ ОҺЛҙ П§Л¶ ОӘПЁЛ¶ ПӢ- ЛёПў ЛөП« ЛҙОӯ ЛҙО¬ П§ ЛҙОғ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ Л¶Оӯ ЛөО¬ НҠПЁ Пҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ЛёОҚ ЛёПӯ ЛҙОӯ ОҺ ЛҙПӨ ЛҙОҳ ЛҙП“ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛҙ ОҳЛҙ ОёЛё П„Лҙ О‘- Л¶ПӘ Л¶П” ЛёПҙ ЛҙОҝ ПҰЛҙ ПӢ Лө П©ПӯЛө О©Лҙ ПӯОҚЛҙ Оӯ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ Л¶Оӯ ЛөО¬ ЛөП§ ЛҙПӯ П° Л¶О‘ ОҚ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӢ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөП— Пӯ ЛөО¬ ЛҙП“ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛҙПЁ ЛөПҙ ЛёПӢ ЛҙОғ ОӮЛҙ ПЁЛё ОҙЛҙ ПӨЛҙ П„Лҙ П“- ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ ЛөП¬ ЛҙОӨ НүО’ ЛҙО» НҮО® Л¶Пҳ ЛҙОҳ ЛёОҙ НҠПЈ ЛІОҸ ОҚ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӢ ЛұО“ ЛҙО® ЛёПң ЛөО‘ Пў- П° Л¶О‘ ОҚ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӢ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөП— Пӯ ЛөО¬ ЛҙП“ ЛіО® Л¶Пӣ НүОӘ НҠПЈ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ЛёПһ ЛҙП¬ ЛҙП“ Л¶О® ЛёПӣ НӢО¬ П  Л¶Пҹ Лҙ ПҘОҚЛҙ ЛҜЛё О®Лө ПҳЛё ПҹОҚ ОҺЛҙ П§Лё О®Нү ОҙЛҙ Пі ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ Л¶Оӯ ЛөО¬ ЛөП§ ЛҙПӯ Нј (33. The people of Lut denied the warnings.) (34. Verily, We sent against them Hasib (a violent storm of stones), except the family of Lut, them We saved in the last hour of the night,) (35. As a favor from Us. Thus do We reward him who gives th anks.) (36. And he indeed had warned them of Our punishment, but they doubted the warnings!) (37. And they indeed sought to shame his guests. S o, We blinded their eyes (saying), "Then taste you My torment and My warnings.'') (38. And verily, an abiding torment seized them early in the morning.) (39. Then taste you My torment and My warnings.) (40. And ind eed, We have made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember; then is there any that wil l remember)

The Story of the People of the Prophet Lut Allah the Exalted states that the people of Lut defied and denied their Messenger and committed sodomy, the awful immoral sin that no peo ple in the history of mankind had committed before. This is why Allah destroyed them with a type of torment that He never inflicted upon any nation before them. Allah the Exalted commanded Jibril, peace be upon him, to raise their cities to the sky and then turn them upside down over them, followed by stones made of marked Sij j il. S o He said here:

Пў ЛөП¬ Л° ЛҙПЁ ЛёПҙ НүО  НүП§ ЛіПҒ П® ЛөПҹ ЛҙПқ ОҒ НүП» Л¶ОҮ ЛұОҺ О’ Л¶Ој Л° ЛҙОЈ ЛёПў Л¶П¬ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё П Лҙ ОіЛё ОӯЛҙ Оғ ОӮНү П§Л¶ ОҮНҪ Нј ЛіО® ЛҙОӨ ЛҙОҙ Л¶О‘ (Verily, We sent against them Ha sib (a violent storm of stones) except the family o f Lut, them We saved in the last hour of the night.) They left the city in the last part of the night and were saved from the torment that struck their people, none of whom believed in Lut. And even Lut's wife suffered the same end as her people. Allah' s Prophet Lut left Sodom with his daughters in safety, unharmed. Allah said,

Нј ОҺ ЛҙПЁ ЛҙОҳ ЛҙОё ЛёП„ ЛҙО‘ ЛёПў ЛөП« ЛҙОӯ ЛҙО¬ П§ ЛҙОғ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙО® ЛҙПң ЛҙО· ПҰЛҙ ПЈ ПҜЛ¶ О°Лё О Лҙ П§ ЛҙПҡ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӣ НҪ (Thus do We reward him who gives thanks. And he indeed had warned them of Our punishment,) meaning, before the torment stru ck his people, he warned them of Allah's torment and punishment. They did not heed the wa rning, nor listen to Lut, but instead doubted and disputed the warning.

НјЛ¶ ПӘЛ¶ П”Лё ПҙЛҙ Оҝ ПҰЛҙ ПӢ Лө П©ПӯЛө О©Лҙ ПӯОҚЛҙ Оӯ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (And they indeed sought to sh ame his guests) that is the nigh t the angels Jibril, Mika'il and Israfil came to him in the shape of handsome young men, as a test from Allah for Lut's people.

Lut hosted his guests, while his wife, the evil old one, sent a message to her people informing them of Lut's guests. They came to him in hast e from every direction, and Lut had to close the door in their faces. They came during the night and tried to break the door; Lut tried to fend them off, while shielding hi s guests from them, saying,

ЛҙП“ ЛёПў ЛөОҳ ЛёПЁ ЛөПӣ ПҘЛ¶ ОҮ П° Л¶О— ОҺ ЛҙПЁ ЛҙО‘ Л¶ЛҜ Пө ЛөОҶ Л° ЛҙП« НҪНј ЛҙПҰ Пҙ Л¶П  Л¶ПҢ Л° (These are my daughters, if you must act (so).) (15 :71), in reference to their women,

НјНҲ П–Лҙ ОЈ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛҙПҡ Л¶О— ОҺ ЛҙПЁ ЛҙО‘ П°Л¶ П“ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛҙ Пҹ ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ЛҙО– ЛёПӨ Л¶П  ЛҙПӢ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөПҹ ОҺ ЛҙП— НҪ (They said: "Surely, you know that we ha ve neither any desire nor need of your daughters!'')(11:79), meaning, ' we do not have any desire for women,'

НјЛө ОӘПіЛ¶ О®Лө П§ ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ЛөПў ЛҙП  ЛёПҢ ЛҙОҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПҡ НүП§ Л¶ОҮ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (and indeed you know well what we want!)(11:79) Whe n the situation became serious and they insisted on coming in, Jibril went out to them and struck their eyes with the tip of his wing,

causing them to lose their sight. They went back feeling for the walls to guide them,

threatening Lut with what would befall him in the morning. Allah the Exalted said,

Нј НҮО® Л¶Пҳ ЛҙОҳ ЛёОҙ НҠПЈ ЛІОҸ ОҚ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӢ ЛұО“ ЛҙО® ЛёПң ЛөО‘ ПўЛө П¬Лҙ ОӨНү О’Лҙ О» ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (And verily, an abiding torment seized them ea rly in the morning.) meaning, a torment that they had no way of escaping or avoiding,

Л¶О® ЛёПӣ НӢО¬ П  Л¶Пҹ Лҙ ПҘОҚЛҙ ЛҜЛё О®Лө ПҳЛё ПҹОҚ ОҺЛҙ П§Лё О®Нү ОҙЛҙ Пі ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ Л¶Оӯ ЛөО¬ ЛөП§ ЛҙПӯ П° Л¶О‘ ОҚ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӢ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөП— Пӯ ЛөО¬ ЛҙП“ НҪ Нј ЛіО® Л¶Пӣ НүОӘ НҠПЈ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ЛёПһ ЛҙП¬ ЛҙП“ (Then taste you My torment and My warnings. An d indeed, We have made the Qur'an easy to understand and remember; then is there any that wil l remember) ЛөОӯ ЛөО¬ НҠПЁ Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҘ ЛёП® ЛҙПӢ ЛёО® Л¶П“ ЛҙПқ ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ ЛҙЛҜ ОӮ ЛҙОҹ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ НҪ- ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛ¶ ОҳЛ°Лҙ ПіОҺЛҙ Л°Л¶ О‘ Лё ОҚП®Лө О‘Нү О¬Лҙ Пӣ ЛіОӯ Л¶ОӘ ЛҙОҳ ЛёПҳ НҠПЈ ЛіО° Пі Л¶О° Л¶ПӢ ЛҙО¬ ЛёО§ ЛҙОғ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ Л° ЛҙП§ ЛёО¬ ЛҙО§ ЛҙО„ ЛҙП“ ОҺЛҙ П¬НӢ П Лө Пӣ-ЛёПў ЛөПӣ ЛөО® Л° НүП” ЛөПӣ ЛҙОғ ЛІО® ЛёПҙ ЛҙО§ Л¶О® ЛөО‘ НҠО° Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ЛІО“ ЛҙЛҜ ОҒ ЛҙО® ЛҙО‘ ПўЛө ПңЛҙ Пҹ ЛёПЎ ЛҙОғ Лё ПўЛө ПңЛ¶ ОҢЛ°Лҙ ПҹЛё ПӯЛө Оғ ЛёПҰ НӢПЈ- ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөПҹ П® ЛөПҳ ЛҙПі ЛёПЎ ЛҙОғ ЛІО® Л¶Ој ЛҙОҳ ПЁ НҠПЈ ЛІ ПҠПҙЛ¶ ПӨЛҙ Оҹ ЛөПҰ ЛёОӨ ЛҙП§- Лҙ ПҘП®НҠ ПҹЛҙ П®Лө ПіЛҙ Пӯ ЛөПҠ ЛёПӨ ЛҙО  ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөПЎ ЛҙО° ЛёП¬ ЛөПҙ ЛҙОі ЛҙО® ЛөО‘ НҠОӘ Пҹ ОҚ- П°Лҙ П«Лё О©Лҙ Оғ ЛөО” ЛҙПӢ ОҺ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў ЛөП« ЛөОӘ Л¶ПӢ ЛёП® ЛҙПЈ ЛөО” ЛҙПӢ ОҺ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ Л¶Пһ ЛҙО‘ Нј НҠО® ЛҙПЈ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ (41. And indeed, warnings came to the people of Fir'awn.) (42. (They) denied all Our signs, so We seized them with a punishment of the Almig hty, All-Capable.) (43. Are your disbelievers better than these Or have you immunity in the Divine Scriptures) (44.Or say they: "We are a great multitude, victorious'') (45. Their multitud e will be put to flight, and they will show their backs.) (46. Nay, but the Hour is their appoin ted time, and the Hour will be more grievous and more bitter.)

The Story of Fir'awn and His People Allah the Exalted narrates to us the story of Fi r'awn and his people. A Messenger came to them from Allah, Musa supported by his brother Harun. Their Messengers delivered good news if they believe, and a warning if they rej ected the Message. Allah supported Musa and Harun with tremendous miracles and great signs, but Fir'aw n and his people rej ected all of them. Allah took them the way the All-Mighty, the All-Capa ble would; He destroyed them all leaving none surviving to tell the story of what happened to them. Advising and Threatening the Quraysh Allah said,

НјЛё ПўЛө ПӣЛө О®Л°Нү П”Лө ПӣЛҙ ОғНҪ (Are your disbelievers) meaning, ' Oidolators of th e Quraysh,'

НјЛё ПўЛө ПңЛ¶ ОҢЛ°Лҙ ПҹЛё ПӯЛө Оғ ЛёПҰ НӢПЈ ЛІО® ЛёПҙ ЛҙО§ НҪ (better than these) meaning better than the nations that were mentioned here, who were destroyed on account of their disbelief in the Me ssengers and rej ecting the Scriptures. ' Are you better than these'

НјЛ¶ О®Лө О‘НҠ О°ПҹОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ЛІО“ ЛҙЛҜ ОҒ ЛҙО® ЛҙО‘ ПўЛө ПңЛҙ Пҹ ЛёПЎ ЛҙОғ НҪ (Or have you immunity in the Di vine Scriptures), ' do you have immunity from Allah that the torment and punishment will not touch you' Allah said about the Quraysh,

Нј ЛІО® Л¶Ој ЛҙОҳ ПЁ НҠПЈ ЛІ ПҠПҙЛ¶ ПӨЛҙ Оҹ ЛөПҰ ЛёОӨ ЛҙП§ ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөПҹ П® ЛөПҳ ЛҙПі ЛёПЎ ЛҙОғ НҪ (Or say they: "We are a great multitude, victor ious'') stating that they believed they will support each other and their great gathering will avail them against those who intend to harm them. Allah the Exalted responded,

Нј ЛҙО® ЛөО‘ НҠОӘ Пҹ ОҚ Лҙ ПҘП®НҠ ПҹЛҙ П®Лө ПіЛҙ Пӯ ЛөПҠ ЛёПӨ ЛҙО  ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөПЎ ЛҙО° ЛёП¬ ЛөПҙ ЛҙОі НҪ (Their multitude will be put to flight, and they will show their backs.) affirming that their gathering shall scatter, and they shall be defe ated. Al-Bukhari recorded that Ibn ' Abbas said,

"The Prophet , while in a dome-shaped tent on the d ay of the battle of Badr, said,

В» ЛёОӘ ЛҙО’ ЛёПҢ ЛөО— ЛёПў ЛҙПҹ ЛҙО– ЛёОҢ Л¶О· ЛёПҘ Л¶ОҮ Нү ПўЛө П¬Лё П ПҹОҚ Л¬Лҙ ПҷЛҙ ОӘЛё ПӢЛҙ ПӯЛҙ Пӯ ЛҙПҷ ЛҙОӘ ЛёП¬ ЛҙПӢ ЛҙПҷ ЛөОӘ ЛөОё ЛёП§ ЛҙОғ ОҚЛұ ОӘЛҙ О‘Лҙ Оғ Л¶ОҪ ЛёОӯ ЛҙО„ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ПІЛ¶ П“ Л¶ПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПҙ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙОӘ ЛёПҢ ЛҙО‘В« (O Allah! Iask you for the fulfillment of Your covenant and promise. O Allah! If You wish (to destroy the believers), You will never be wors hipped on the earth after today.) Abu Bakr caught him by the hand and said, ' This is suffic ient, O Allah's Messenger! You have sufficiently asked and petitioned Allah.' The Prophet was clad in his armor at that time and went out,

saying,

ЛҙО® ЛөО‘ НҠОӘ Пҹ ОҚ Лҙ ПҘП®НҠ ПҹЛҙ П®Лө ПіЛҙ Пӯ ЛөПҠ ЛёПӨ ЛҙО  ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөПЎ ЛҙО° ЛёП¬ ЛөПҙ ЛҙОі НҪ- ЛөО” ЛҙПӢ ОҺ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ Л¶Пһ ЛҙО‘ Нј НҠО® ЛҙПЈ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ П°Лҙ П«Лё О©Лҙ Оғ ЛөО” ЛҙПӢ ОҺ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў ЛөП« ЛөОӘ Л¶ПӢ ЛёП® ЛҙПЈ (Their multitude will be put to flight and they wi ll show their backs. Nay, but the Hour is their appointed time and that Hour will be more grievous and more bitter.)'' Al-Bukhari also recorded that Yusuf bin Mahak said, "Iwas with the Moth er of the faithful, ' A'ishah, when she said,

' When Iwas still a young playful girl in Makka h, this Ayah was revealed to Muhammad ,

Нј НҠО® ЛҙПЈ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ П°Лҙ П«Лё О©Лҙ Оғ ЛөО” ЛҙПӢ ОҺ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў ЛөП« ЛөОӘ Л¶ПӢ ЛёП® ЛҙПЈ ЛөО” ЛҙПӢ ОҺ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ Л¶Пһ ЛҙО‘ НҪ (Nay, but the Hour is their appointed time and that Hour will be more grievous and more bitter.)'' This is the abridged na rration that Al-Bukhari collected, but he also collected a longer narration of it in the Book of the Virtues of the Qur'an. Muslim did not collect this Hadith.

ЛіО® ЛөПҢ ЛөОі ЛҙПӯ ЛіПһ Л° ЛҙП  ЛҙОҝ П°Л¶ П“ Лҙ ПҰПҙЛ¶ ПЈЛ¶ О®Лё О Лө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ НҪ- Лҙ ПҘП®Лө О’Лҙ ОӨЛё ОҙЛө Пі ЛҙПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі ЛёОҚ П® ЛөП— Пӯ ЛөО« ЛёПў Л¶П¬ Л¶П« П® ЛөОҹ ЛөПӯ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ Л¶Оӯ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“Нј ЛҙО® ЛҙПҳ ЛҙОі НүОІ ЛҙПЈ ЛіОӯ ЛҙОӘ ЛҙПҳ Л¶О‘ ЛөПӘ Л° ЛҙПЁ ЛёПҳ ЛҙП  ЛҙО§ ЛҜЛё П°Лҙ О· НүПһ ЛөПӣ ОҺНү П§Л¶ ОҮ- ЛІО“ ЛҙОӘ Л¶ОЈ Пӯ НүП» Л¶ОҮ ОӮЛҙ П§Лө О®Лё ПЈЛҙ Оғ ОӮЛҙ ПЈЛҙ Пӯ Л¶О® ЛҙОј ЛҙО’ ЛёПҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ЛіОў ЛёПӨ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӣ- ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ЛёПһ ЛҙП¬ ЛҙП“ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛҙПҢ Л° ЛҙПҙ ЛёО· ЛҙОғ ОӮЛҙ ПЁЛё ПңЛҙ П Лё П«Лҙ Оғ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ ЛіО® Л¶Пӣ НүОӘ НҠПЈ-Л¶О® ЛөО‘ НҠО° Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ Лө П©П®Лө П Лҙ ПҢЛҙ П“ ЛҜЛё П°Лҙ О· НҠПһ ЛөПӣ ЛҙПӯ - Лі О®ПҙЛ¶ ПҗЛҙ О» НҠПһ ЛөПӣ ЛҙПӯ ЛІО® ЛҙП„ ЛҙОҳ ЛёОҙ НҠПЈ Лі О®ПҙЛ¶ О’Лҙ ПӣЛҙ Пӯ-ЛёПҹ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ ЛіО® ЛҙП¬ ЛҙП§ ЛҙПӯ ЛіО– Л° НүПЁ ЛҙОҹ П°Л¶ П“ Лҙ ПҰПҙЛ¶ ПҳНү ОҳЛө ПӨ- П°Л¶ П“ Л¶Оӯ Л¶ОӘ ЛҙОҳ ЛёПҳ НҠПЈ ЛіПҡ Пҙ Л¶П  ЛҙПЈ ЛҙОӘ ПЁ Л¶ПӢ ЛіП• ЛёОӘ Л¶О» Л¶ОӘ ЛҙПҢ ЛёПҳ ЛҙПЈ-Нј (47. Verily, the criminals are in error and will bu rn.) (48. The Day they will be dragged on their faces into the Fire (it will be said to them): "Taste you the touch of Hell!'') 6(49. Verily, We have created all things with Qadar.) (50. And Our commandment is but one as the twinkling of an eye.) (51. And indeed, We have destroyed your li kes; then is there any that will remember) (52. And everything they have done is noted in Az-Zubur.) (53. And everything, small and large,

is written down.) (54. Verily, those who have Taqwa , will be in the midst of Gardens and Rivers.) (55.In a seat of truth, near the Muqtadir King.)

The Destination of the Criminals Allah the Exalted states that the criminals are mis guided away from the truth and engulfed in confusion, because of the doubts and uncertainty they are in. This description befits every disbeliever and innovator of all types and forms of sects. Allah the Exalted said,

ЛөПі ЛҙПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі НҪНј ЛёПў Л¶П¬ Л¶П« П® ЛөОҹ ЛөПӯ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ Л¶Оӯ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө О’Лҙ ОӨЛё Оҙ (The Day they will be dragged on their faces into t he Fire), meaning, j ust as they were consumed in doubt, suspicion and hesitation, they ended up in the Fire. And j ust as they were misguided, they will end up being dragged on thei r faces, unaware of where they will be taken.

They will be admonished and criticized,

НјЛҙ О®Лҙ ПҳЛҙ Оі НүОІ ЛҙПЈ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөП— Пӯ ЛөО« НҪ ("Taste you the touch of Hell!'')

Everything was created with Qadar Allah's statement,

Л¶О‘ Лө ПӘЛ°Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҳЛҙ П Лҙ О§ ЛіЛҜ ЛёП° ЛҙО· НүПһ ЛөПӣ ОҺНү П§Л¶ ОҮНҪНј ЛіОӯ ЛҙОӘ ЛҙПҳ (Verily, We have created all things with Qadar.) is similar to several other Ayat,

НјЛұ ОҚО®ПіЛ¶ ОӘЛё ПҳЛҙ О— ЛөП© ЛҙОӯ НүОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙП“ ЛіЛҜ ЛёП° ЛҙО· НүПһ ЛөПӣ ЛҙП– ЛҙП  ЛҙО§ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (He has created everything, and has measured it exactly according to its due measurements (Faqaddarahu Taqdir).) (25:2) and,

П°Лҙ П Лё ПӢЛ· П»ОҚ ЛҙПҡ НӢО‘ ЛҙОӯ ЛҙПў ЛёОі ОҚ Л¶Оў НӢО’ ЛҙОі НҪ- ПҜНү П®Лҙ ОҙЛҙ П“ ЛҙП– ЛҙП  ЛҙО§ ПҜЛ¶ О¬Нү ПҹОҚ Нј ПҜЛҙ ОӘЛҙ П¬Лҙ П“ ЛҙОӯ НүОӘ ЛҙП— ПҜЛ¶ О¬Нү ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ ( G l o r i f y t h e N a m e o f y o u r L o r d , t h e M o s t High. Who has created (everything), and then proportioned it. And Who has measured (Qaddara) and then guided.)(87:1-3), i.e., He measured out the total sum (Qadar) of everythi ng and then guided the creation to it. The Imams of the Sunnah relied on this honorable Ayah as evidence that Allah created the creation with destined limits before they were created. He k new everything that will occur before it occurred and recorded everything that will occur, before they occurred. They used this Ayah and similar Ayat and Hadiths to refute the Qada riyyah sect, who started their sect during the latter time of the Companions. Imentioned this subj ect in detail in my explanation on the chapter on faith of Sahih Al-Bukhari. Iwill me ntion here some Hadiths pertaining to this honorable Ayah. Imam Ahmad recorded that Abu Hurayra h said, "The idolators of the Quraysh came to the Messenger of Allah arguing with him and discounting the Qadar. This Ayah was revealed,

ЛёОҚ П® ЛөП— Пӯ ЛөО« ЛёПў Л¶П¬ Л¶П« П® ЛөОҹ ЛөПӯ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ Л¶Оӯ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө О’Лҙ ОӨЛё ОҙЛө Пі ЛҙПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі НҪ ЛҙО® ЛҙПҳ ЛҙОі НүОІ ЛҙПЈ-Нј ЛіОӯ ЛҙОӘ ЛҙПҳ Л¶О‘ Лө ПӘЛ°Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҳЛҙ П Лҙ О§ ЛіЛҜ ЛёП° ЛҙО· НүПһ ЛөПӣ ОҺНү П§Л¶ ОҮ (The Day they will be dragged on their faces into the Fire: "Taste you the touch of Hell!'' Verily,

We have created all things with Qadar.)'' Mu slim, At-Tirmidhi and Ibn Maj ah collected this Hadith. Al-Bazzar recorded that ' Amr bin Shu'ayb sa id that his father narrated that his grandfather said, "These Ayat were revealed about t hose who deny Al-Qadar,

ЛіО® ЛөПҢ ЛөОі ЛҙПӯ ЛіПһ Л° ЛҙП  ЛҙОҝ П°Л¶ П“ Лҙ ПҰПҙЛ¶ ПЈЛ¶ О®Лё О Лө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ НҪ- Лҙ ПҘП®Лө О’Лҙ ОӨЛё ОҙЛө Пі ЛҙПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі Нј ЛҙО® ЛҙПҳ ЛҙОі НүОІ ЛҙПЈ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөП— Пӯ ЛөО« ЛёПў Л¶П¬ Л¶П« П® ЛөОҹ ЛөПӯ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ Л¶Оӯ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ Нј ЛіОӯ ЛҙОӘ ЛҙПҳ Л¶О‘ Лө ПӘЛ°Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҳЛҙ П Лҙ О§ ЛіЛҜ ЛёП° ЛҙО· НүПһ ЛөПӣ ОҺНү П§Л¶ ОҮНҪ (Verily, the criminals are in error and will burn . The Day they will be dragged on their faces into the Fire: "Taste you the touch of Hell!'' Veri ly, We have created all things with Qadar.)'' Ibn Abi Hatim also recorded that Zurarah said that his father said that the Prophet recited this Ayah,

ЛөПі ЛҙПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі НҪ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөП— Пӯ ЛөО« ЛёПў Л¶П¬ Л¶П« П® ЛөОҹ ЛөПӯ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ Л¶Оӯ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө О’Лҙ ОӨЛё Оҙ ЛҙО® ЛҙПҳ ЛҙОі НүОІ ЛҙПЈ-Нј ЛіОӯ ЛҙОӘ ЛҙПҳ Л¶О‘ Лө ПӘЛ°Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҳЛҙ П Лҙ О§ ЛіЛҜ ЛёП° ЛҙО· НүПһ ЛөПӣ ОҺНү П§Л¶ ОҮ ("Taste you the touch of Hell!'' Verily, We have created all things with Qadar.) and then said,

В»Л¶П“ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө П§П®Лө ПңЛҙ Пі ПІЛ¶ ОҳНү ПЈЛө Оғ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛіОұ ОҺ ЛҙП§ ЛөОғ ПІЛ¶ П“ ЛёО– ЛҙПҹ ЛҙО° ЛҙП§ Л¶О® Л¶О§ ОҒ ПІ НҝОҚ Л¶Оӯ ЛҙОӘ ЛҙПҳ Л¶О‘ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө Пі Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ПЈНү О°ПҹОҚВ« (These Ayat were revealed about some members of my Ummah. They will come before the end of time and deny Al-Qadar.) Ata' bin Abi Raba h said, "Iwent to Ib n ' Abbas and found him drawing water from the well of Zamzam. The bottom o f his clothes were wet with the water of Zamzam and Isaid to him, ' They talked about Al-Qada r (some denied it).' He asked, ' Have they done this' Isaid, ' Yes.' He said, ' By Allah! This Ayah was revealed only about them,

ЛҙОӨ ЛёОҙ ЛөПі ЛҙПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі НҪ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөП— Пӯ ЛөО« ЛёПў Л¶П¬ Л¶П« П® ЛөОҹ ЛөПӯ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ Л¶Оӯ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөО’ ЛҙО® ЛҙПҳ ЛҙОі НүОІ ЛҙПЈ-Нј ЛіОӯ ЛҙОӘ ЛҙПҳ Л¶О‘ Лө ПӘЛ°Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҳЛҙ П Лҙ О§ ЛіЛҜ ЛёП° ЛҙО· НүПһ ЛөПӣ ОҺНү П§Л¶ ОҮ ("Taste you the touch of Hell!'' Verily, We have created all things with Qadar.) They are the worst members of this Ummah. Do not visit those who fall ill among them or pray th e Funeral prayer for those among them who die. If Isaw one of them, Iwould pluck out his eyes with these two fingers of mine.''' Imam Ahmad record ed that Nafi'said, " ' Abdullah bin ' Umar had a friend in the area of Ash-Sham who used to write to him. ' Abdullah bin ' Umar wrote to him, ' I was told that you started talking about Al-Qadar. Therefore, do not dare write to me any more.

Iheard the Messenger of Allah say,

В»ОӯЛҙ ОӘЛҙ ПҳЛё ПҹОҺЛ¶ О‘ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө Пі ЛІПЎ ОҚ ЛҙП® ЛёП— ЛҙОғ ПІЛ¶ ОҳНү ПЈЛө Оғ ПІЛ¶ П“ Лө ПҘП®Лө ПңЛҙ ПҙЛҙ ОіВ« (There will be some members of my Ummah wh o will deny Al-Qadar.)'' Abu Dawud collected this Hadith from Ahmad bin Hanbal. Imam Ahmad recorded that ' Abdullah bin ' Umar said that the Messenger of Allah said,

В»ОІЛё ПҙЛҙ ПңЛё ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ ЛөО° ЛёО  ЛҙПҢ ЛёПҹ ОҚ П°Лё ОҳЛҙ ОЈ ЛіОӯ ЛҙОӘ ЛҙПҳ Л¶О‘ ЛіЛҜ ЛёПІ ЛҙО· НҠПһ ЛөПӣВ« (Every thing is predetermined, even laziness and intelligence.) Muslim collected this Hadith using a chain of narration throug h Imam Malik. There is also an authentic Hadith in w hich the Messenger of Allah said,

В»ЛҙО» ЛҙОғ ЛёПҘ Л¶ОҲ ЛҙП“ Л¬Лё О°Лҙ О Лё ПҢЛҙ О— ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ Л¶Нҝ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПҢ ЛҙОҳ ЛёОі ОҚЛёПһ ЛөПҳ ЛҙП“ ЛІО® ЛёПЈ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПҡ ЛҙО‘ ОҺ :

ЛёПһ ЛөПҳ ЛҙО— ОҺЛҙ ПҹЛҙ Пӯ Л¬Лҙ ПһЛҙ ПҢЛҙ П“ ЛҙЛҜ ОҺ ЛҙО· ОҺЛҙ ПЈЛҙ Пӯ ЛөНҝ ОҚ ЛҙОӯ НүОӘ ЛҙП— : ЛөО– ЛёП  ЛҙПҢ ЛҙП“ ПІНӢ П§Лҙ Оғ ЛёП® ЛҙПҹ ПҘОҺЛҙ П„Лё ПҙНү ОёПҹОҚ ЛҙПһ ЛҙПӨ ЛҙПӢ ЛөОў ЛҙОҳ ЛёП” ЛҙО— ЛёП® ЛҙПҹ НүПҘ Л¶ОҲ ЛҙП“ Л¬ ОҚ ЛҙО¬ ЛҙПӣ ЛҙПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПң ЛҙПҹ ОҚЛҙ О¬Лҙ ПӣВ« (Seek the help of Allah and do not succumb to f eebleness. And when an affliction strikes you,

say, "Allah has decreed this, and He does as He wil ls.'' Do not say, "Had Idone this or that, this or that would have happened, because "if'' opens the door wide for the work of Ash-Shaytan.)''

In a Hadith from ' Abbas, the Messenger of Allah said to him,

В»ЛёПӢ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Лҙ ПҷП®Лө ПҢЛҙ П”Лё ПЁЛҙ Пі ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ОҚ П® ЛөПҢ ЛҙПӨ ЛҙОҳ ЛёОҹ ОҚ Л¶П® ЛҙПҹ ЛҙО” НүПЈ ЛөО„ ЛёПҹ ОҚ НүПҘ ЛҙОғ ЛёПў ЛҙП  ОҚ П® ЛөПҢ ЛҙПӨ ЛҙОҳ ЛёОҹ ОҚ Л¶П® ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ Л¬ ЛҙПҷ П® ЛөПҢ ЛҙП” ЛёПЁ ЛҙПі ЛёПў ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПҡ ЛҙПҹ ЛөНҝ ОҚ ЛөПӘ ЛёО’ ЛөОҳ ЛёПң ЛҙПі ЛёПў ЛҙПҹ Л¬Лі ЛҜЛё ПІЛҙ ОёЛ¶ О‘ ЛёПў ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПҡ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӢ ЛөНҝ ОҚ ЛөПӘ ЛёО’ ЛөОҳ ЛёПң ЛҙПі ЛёПў ЛҙПҹ Л¬Лі ЛҜЛё ПІЛҙ ОёЛ¶ О‘ ЛҙПҷ Пӯ НҠО® ЛөПҖ ЛҙПі ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ П’Лө ОӨНҠ ОјПҹОҚ Л¶О– ЛҙПі Л¶П® ЛөПғ ЛҙПӯ Лө ПЎОҺЛҙ П Лё П—Лҙ О„Лё ПҹОҚ Л¶О– НүП” ЛҙОҹ Л¬Лҙ ПҷПӯНҠ О®Лө ПҖЛҙ ПіВ« (Know that if the Ummah were to all gather thei r strength to cause you some benefit that Allah has not decreed for you, they will never be able to bring you that benefit. And if they gather their strength to bring a harm to you that Allah has not written on you, they will never be able to harm you. The pens have gone dry and the Books o f Record have been closed.) Imam Ahmad recorded that Ubadah bin Al-Walid bin Ubadah sa id that his father said to him, "Iwent to ' Ubadah when he was ill, and Ithought that he was going to die. S o Isaid, ' Omy father,

advis e us and make the bes t effort in this regard.' He s aid, ' Help me s it up,' and when he was helped up, he said, ' Omy son! Know that you will not taste the delight of Faith or earn true knowledge in Allah until you believe in Al-Qadar, the good and the not so good parts of it.' I asked, ' Omy father! How can Iknow (or believe in) Al-Qadar, the good and the not so good parts of it' He said, ' When you know that what has missed you, would never have come to you and what has befallen you would never have misse d you. Omy son! Iheard the Messenger of Allah say,

В»ЛөПӘ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПқ ОҺ ЛҙП— НүПў ЛөОӣ Л¬Лө ПўЛҙ П Лҙ ПҳЛё ПҹОҚ ЛөНҝ ОҚ ЛҙП– ЛҙП  ЛҙО§ ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ЛҙПқ НүПӯ ЛҙОғ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ : Л¬Лё ОҗЛө ОҳЛё ПӣОҚ Л¶ПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі П°Лҙ ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ ЛІПҰ Л¶ОӢ ОҺ ЛҙПӣ ЛҙП® ЛөП« ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ О‘ Л¶О” ЛҙПӢ ОҺ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҡ ЛёП  Л¶О— ПІЛ¶ П“ ПҜЛҙ О®Лҙ О Лҙ П“ О” ЛҙПЈ ОҺ ЛҙПҙ Л¶Пҳ ЛёПҹ ОҚВ« (The first thing Allah created was the Pen, ri ght after that commanded it, ' Record!' and the Pen recorded everything that will occur until the Day of Resurrection.) Omy son! If you die not having this belief, you will enter the Hellfire.''' At-Tirmidhi also recorded it and said: "Hasan Sahih Gharib.'' It is confirmed in Sahih Muslim from ' Abdullah bin ' Amr that the Messenger of Allah said,

В» ЛҙП– ЛөП  ЛёОЁ ЛҙПі ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПһ ЛёО’ ЛҙП— Л¶П– ЛёП  ЛҙОЁ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙО® Пі Л¶О© ОҺ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПЈ ЛҙОҗ ЛҙОҳ ЛҙПӣ ЛҙНҝ ОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ О”Лҙ ПЁЛҙ Оі ЛҙП’ ЛёПҹ ЛҙОғ Лҙ ПҰПҙЛ¶ ОҙЛё ПӨЛҙ ОЁЛ¶ О‘ ЛҙОҪ ЛёОӯ ЛҙО„ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Л¶О• ОҚ ЛҙП® ПӨ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚВ« (Verily, Allah recorded the measurements for the creatures fifty thousand years before He created the heavens and earth.) Ibn Wahb added,

НјЛ¶ ЛҜОӮЛҙ ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛөПӘ ЛөО· ЛёО® ЛҙПӢ ЛҙПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПӣ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (And His Throne was over the water.)(11:7) At-Tirmidhi also recorded it, and he said: "Hasan,

Sahih Gharib.''

A Warning to beware of Allah's Threats Allah said,

Нј Л¶О® ЛҙОј ЛҙО’ ЛёПҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ЛіОў ЛёПӨ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӣ ЛІО“ ЛҙОӘ Л¶ОЈ ЛҙПӯ НүП» Л¶ОҮ ОӮЛҙ П§Лө О®Лё ПЈЛҙ Оғ ОӮЛҙ ПЈЛҙ ПӯНҪ (And Our commandment is but one as the twinkling of an eye.) This is information about the execution of His will in His creation, j ust as He informed us the execution of His decree in them,

НјЛІ О“Лҙ ОӘЛ¶ ОЈЛҙ Пӯ НүП» Л¶ОҮ ОӮЛҙ П§Лө О®Лё ПЈЛҙ Оғ ОӮЛҙ ПЈЛҙ ПӯНҪ (And Our commandment is but one) meaning, ' We only command a thing once, without needing to repeat the command; and whatever We command comes to existence faster than the blinking of an eye without any delay, not even for an instant.' Allah said,

НјЛё ПўЛө ПңЛҙ ПҢЛ°Лҙ ПҙЛё О·Лҙ Оғ ОӮЛҙ ПЁЛё ПңЛҙ П Лё П«Лҙ Оғ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (And indeed, We have destroyed your likes ), i.e. the earlier nations who denied their Messengers,

НјЛі О®Л¶ ПӣНү ОӘНҠ ПЈ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ЛёПһ ЛҙП¬ ЛҙП“ НҪ (then is there any that will remember) meaning, is there any that will receive admonition by remembering the humiliation and torment that Allah decreed for them ЛҙПһ Л¶ПҢ ЛөП“ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛҙ Пӣ Лҙ ПҘП®Лө П¬Лҙ ОҳЛё ОёЛҙ Пі ОҺЛҙ ПЈ ЛҙПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘ ЛҙПӯ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛҙПЁ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘ Лҙ ПһПҙЛ¶ ОЈЛҙ ПӯНҪ НјЛө ПһЛё О’Лҙ П— ПҰНӢ ПЈ Пў Л¶П¬ Л¶ПҢ Л° ЛҙПҙ ЛёО· ЛҙО„ Л¶О‘ (And a barrier will be set between them and that which they desire, as was done in the past with the people of their kind.)(34:54) Allah's stat ement,

Нј Л¶О® ЛөО‘ НҠО° Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ Лө П©П®Лө П Лҙ ПҢЛҙ П“ ЛіЛҜ ЛёП° ЛҙО· НҠПһ ЛөПӣ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (And everything they have done is noted in Az-Zubur.) meaning, everything they did is recorded in the Books of Record entrusted to the angels, peace be upon them,

Нј ЛіО® Пҙ Л¶О’ ЛҙПӣ ЛҙПӯ Лі О®ПҙЛ¶ ПҗЛҙ О» НҠПһ ЛөПӣ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (And everything, small and large ,) meaning, of their actions,

НјЛІ О®Лҙ П„Лҙ ОҳЛё ОҙНҠ ПЈНҪ (is written down.) everything that they do is recorded and written in their Record of deeds,

which leave nothing, whether large or small, but it is recorded and counted. Imam Ahmad recorded that ' A'ishah said that the Messenger of Allah said,

В»ЛҙПҰ Л¶ПЈ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лҙ Пҹ НүПҘ Л¶ОҲ ЛҙП“ Л¬Л¶ ОҸП®Лө П§НҠ О¬ПҹОҚ Л¶ О•ОҚЛҙ О®Нү ПҳЛҙ ОӨЛө ПЈЛҙ Пӯ Л¶Пҷ ОҺ НүПі Л¶ОҮ ЛөО” ЛҙОё Л¶ОӢ ОҺ ЛҙПӢ ОҺЛҙ Пі ОҺЛұ О’Л¶ ПҹОҺЛҙ Пғ Л¶Нҝ ОҚВ« (O ' A'ishah! Beware of small sins, because there is someone assigned by Allah who records them.) An-Nasa'i and Ibn Maj ah also collected this Hadith.

The Good End for Those with Taqwa Allah said,

ЛҙП¬ ЛҙП§ ЛҙПӯ ЛіО– Л° НүПЁ ЛҙОҹ П°Л¶ П“ Лҙ ПҰПҙЛ¶ ПҳНү ОҳЛө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ НүПҘ Л¶ОҮ НҪНј ЛіО® (Verily, those who have Taqwa, will be in th e midst of Gardens and Rivers.), unlike the end that the miserable are facing, lo ss, confusion and being dragged in the Fire on their faces, as well as being disgraced, punished and threatened. Allah said,

НјЛі П•Лё ОӘЛ¶ О» Л¶ОӘ ЛҙПҢ ЛёПҳ ЛҙПЈ П°Л¶ П“НҪ (In a seat of truth,) in the Dwelling of Alla h's honor, encompassed by His pleasure, favors,

bounties, generosity and compassion,

НјЛ¶ ОӯЛ¶ ОӘЛҙ ОҳЛё ПҳНҠ ПЈ ЛіПҡ Пҙ Л¶П  ЛҙПЈ ЛҙОӘ ПЁ Л¶ПӢ НҪ (near the Muqtadir King.) meaning with the Magnific ent King Who created everything and meas ured its des tiny; H e is able to g rant them whatever they wis h and as k for. Imam Ahmad recorded that ' Abdullah bin ' Amr said that the Prop het said,

В» ЛёПҰ ЛҙПӢ ЛіОӯ П® ЛөП§ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛҙО® Л¶О‘ ОҺ ЛҙПЁ ЛҙПЈ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ Л¶Нҝ ОҚ ЛҙОӘ ЛёПЁ Л¶ПӢ ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөП„ Л¶Оҙ ЛёПҳ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙОҳ ЛёП  Л¶Пӣ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ ПҰПӨЛё ОЈНү О®ПҹОҚ Л¶ ПҰПҙЛ¶ ПӨЛҙ Пі Лҙ ПҘП®Лө ПҹЛ¶ ОӘЛё ПҢЛҙ Пі ЛҙПҰ Пі Л¶О¬ НүПҹ ОҚ Л¬ ЛІПҰ Пҙ Л¶ПӨ ЛҙПі Л¶ПӘ ЛёПі ЛҙОӘ ЛҙПі ОҺ ОҚП®Лө ПҹЛҙ Пӯ ОҺЛҙ ПЈЛҙ Пӯ Лё ПўЛ¶ П¬ПҙЛ¶ П Лё П«Лҙ ОғЛҙ Пӯ ЛёПў Л¶П¬ Л¶ПӨ ЛёПң ЛөОЈ ПІЛ¶ П“В« (Verily, the j ust will be with Allah on podiums of light, to the right of Ar-Rahman, and both of His Hands are right. They are those who are j u st and fair in their j udgement and with their families and those whom they are responsible fo r.) Muslim and An-Nasa'i also recorded this Hadith.

This is the end of the Tafsir of Surah Iqtaraba t (Al-Qamar). All praise and thanks are due to Allah, and success and immunity from error come fro m Him.

The Tafsir of Surat Ar-Rahman (Chapter - 55) Which was revealed in Makkah The Introduction to Surat Ar-Rahman Imam Ahmad recorded that Zirr said that a man said НҪto Ibn Mas'ud Нј: "How is this recited:

"Ma'in Ghayri Yasin or Asin'' He asked him, "Are you that proficient in reciting the whole Qur'an''

He replied, "Irecite the Mufassal section in one Rak'ah.'' S o he said, "Woe to you! Do you recite the Qur'an in haste, as if it is poetry Iknow that the Prophet us ed to recite two Surahs from the beginning of the Mufassal section (in one Rak'ah).'' And Ibn Mas'ud considered Surat Ar-Rahman to be the beginning of the Mufas sal section. Abu ' Isa At-Tirmidhi recorded that Jab ir said, "The Messenger of Allah went to his Companions and recited Surat Ar-Rahman from beginning to end for them, but they did not say anything. The Prophe t said,

В» ЛҙПҰ ЛҙОҙ ЛёОЈ ЛҙОғ ОҚП®Лө П§ОҺЛҙ ПңЛҙ П“ НӢПҰ Л¶О  ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙО” ЛҙП  ЛёПҙ ЛҙПҹ НӢПҰ Л¶О  ЛёПҹ ОҚ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лө О—Лё ОғЛҙ О®Лҙ П— ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ Л¶ПӘ Л¶Пҹ ЛёП® ЛҙП— П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛөО– ЛёПҙ ЛҙО— ЛҙОғ ОҺЛҙ ПӨНү П Лө Пӣ ЛөО– ЛёПЁ ЛөПӣ Л¬Лё ПўЛө ПңЛё ПЁЛ¶ ПЈ ОҚЛұ О©ПӯЛө О©Лё О®Лҙ ПЈ:

ЛөО— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ НҪНј Л¶ПҘ ОҺ ЛҙО‘ НӢО¬ ЛҙПң ОҚП®Лө ПҹОҺЛҙ П— : ЛҙПҡ ЛҙП  ЛҙП“ ЛөОҸ НӢО¬ ЛҙПң ЛөП§ ОҺЛҙ ПЁНү О‘Лҙ Оӯ ЛҙПҡ Л¶ПӨ ЛҙПҢ Л¶П§ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛіЛҜ ЛёПІ ЛҙОё Л¶О‘ ОҺЛҙ Пҹ ОӘЛё ПӨЛҙ ОӨЛё ПҹОҚВ« (Irecited it to the Jinns, on the night of th e Jinns and their response was better than yours!

Whenever Irecited Allah's statement: (Then which of the blessings of yo ur Lord will you both deny)( They said, "None of Your favors do we de ny, our Lord! All praise is due to you.'') AtTirmidhi recorded it and he said, "This Hadith is Gharib.'' Al-Hafiz Abu Bakr Al-Bazzar also

collected this Hadith. Abu Ja'f ar Ibn Jarir recorded that ' Abdullah bin ' Umar said that the Messenger of Allah recited Surat Ar-Rahman, or it was recited before him, and he said,

В»Л®Лё ПўЛө ПңЛё ПЁЛ¶ ПЈ ОҺЛҙ П¬НӢ О‘Лҙ О®Л¶ Пҹ ОҺ ЛұО‘ ОҚ ЛҙП® ЛҙОҹ ЛҙПҰ ЛҙОҙ ЛёОЈ ЛҙОғ НүПҰ Л¶О  ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөПҠ ЛҙПӨ ЛёОі ЛҙОғ ЛҙПІ Л¶Пҹ ОҺЛҙ ПЈВ« (Why do Ihear the Jinn giving a better response to their Lord than you) They said, "Why is that,

O Allah's Messenger'' He said,

В»П° ЛҙПҹ ОҺ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙО— Л¶Нҝ ОҚ Л¶Пқ ЛёП® ЛҙП— П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛөО– ЛёПҙ ЛҙО— ЛҙОғ ОҺЛҙ ПЈ:

Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ НҪ НҠПҰ Л¶О  ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶О– ЛҙПҹ ОҺ ЛҙП— ОҺНү ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ :ОҸНӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө П§ ОҺЛҙ ПЁНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶Пў ЛҙПҢ Л¶П§ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛіЛҜ ЛёПІ ЛҙОё Л¶О‘ ОҺЛҙ ПҹВ« (Whenever Irecited the statement of Allah the Ex alted, (Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny)( The Jinn responded by sa ying, "None of the favors of our Lord do we deny.'')'' Al-Hafiz Al-Bazzar also collected this Hadith.

ПҹОҚ Л¶ ПҰЛ°Лҙ ПӨЛё ОЈНү О®ПҹОҚ Л¶ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ Л¶Пў ЛёОҙ Л¶О‘ НҪНј Л¶Пў Пҙ Л¶ОЈ НүО® In the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

Лө ПҰЛ°Лҙ ПӨЛё ОЈНү О®ПҹОҚНҪ- Лҙ ПҘОҚЛҙ ЛҜЛё О®Лө ПҳЛё ПҹОҚ ЛҙПў НүП  ЛҙПӢ - ЛҙПҰ Л° ЛҙОҙ П§ Л¶П№ ОҚ ЛҙП– ЛҙП  ЛҙО§ - ЛөПӘ ЛҙПӨ НүП  ЛҙПӢ Лҙ ПҘОҺЛҙ ПҙЛҙ О’ПҹОҚ- ЛіПҘ ОҺ ЛҙО’ ЛёОҙ ЛөОӨ Л¶О‘ ЛөО® ЛҙПӨ ЛҙПҳ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ ЛөОІ ЛёПӨ НүОё Пҹ ОҚ - Лө ПўЛё О Нү ПЁПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ Л¶ПҘ ОҚ ЛҙОӘ ЛөО  ЛёОҙ ЛҙПі Лө О®Лҙ О Нү ОёПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ-ЛҙОҝ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙПӯ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лҙ ПҢЛҙ П“Лҙ Оӯ Лҙ ЛҜОӮЛҙ ПӨНү ОҙПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ ЛҙПҠ ЛҙПҘ ОҚ ЛҙО° Пҙ Л¶ПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ- Л¶ПҘ ОҚ ЛҙО° Пҙ Л¶ПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ЛёОҚ ЛёП® ЛҙПҗ ЛёП„ ЛҙО— НүП» ЛҙОғ - ЛёОҚ П® ЛөПӨ Пҙ Л¶П— ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙПҘ ОҚ ЛҙО° Пҙ Л¶ПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛёОҚ Пӯ ЛөО® Л¶Оҙ ЛёОЁ ЛөО— ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ Л¶ПӮ ЛёОҙ Л¶Пҳ ЛёПҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ЛҙПҘ ЛёОҜ ЛҙП® ЛёПҹ ОҚ- ЛҙОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ПЎ ОҺ ЛҙП§ НҮПј Л¶Пҹ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лҙ ПҢЛҙ ОҝЛҙ Пӯ- Л¶ ПЎОҺЛҙ ПӨЛё ПӣНҮ П»ОҚ ЛөО• ОҚ ЛҙО« Лө ПһЛё ОЁНү ПЁПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ ЛІО” ЛҙП¬ Л¶Пң Л° ЛҙП“ ОҺЛҙ П¬ПҙЛ¶ П“ - Лө ПҘОҺЛҙ ОӨЛё ПіНү О®ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ Л¶П’ ЛёОј ЛҙПҢ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ПӯЛө О« НҠОҗ ЛҙОӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ -НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ (1. Ar-Rahman!) (2. He has taught the Qur'an.) (3. He created man.) (4. He taught him AlBayan.) (5. The sun and the moon (run) on fixed cou rses.) (6. And the Naj m and the trees

prostrating.) (7. And the heaven He has raised high, and He has set up the balance.) (8. In order that you may not transgress the balance.) (9. And observe the weight with equity and do not make the balance deficient.) (10. And the ea rth He has put down (laid) for Al-An'am.) (11.

Therein are fruits, date palms producing Akmam .) (12. And also corn, with (its) ' Asf, and Rayhan.) (13. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny)

Ar-Rahman revealed and taught the Qur'an Allah informs of His favors and His mercy for His creatures, for He revealed the Qur'an to His servants, He made memorizing and understanding of i t easy for those on whom He has bestowed His mercy,

Лө ПҰЛ°Лҙ ПӨЛё ОЈНү О®ПҹОҚНҪ- Лҙ ПҘОҚЛҙ ЛҜЛё О®Лө ПҳЛё ПҹОҚ ЛҙПў НүП  ЛҙПӢ - ЛҙПҰ Л° ЛҙОҙ П§ Л¶П№ ОҚ ЛҙП– ЛҙП  ЛҙО§ - ЛөПӘ ЛҙПӨ НүП  ЛҙПӢ Нј Лҙ ПҘОҺЛҙ ПҙЛҙ О’ПҹОҚ (Ar-Rahman! He has taught the Qur'an. He crea ted man. He taught him Al-Bayan.) Al-Hasan said: "Eloquent speech.'' This refers to Allah teaching the Qur'an, that is, teaching the servants ho w to recite it by ma king it ea s y fo r them to speak and pronounce letters with the various parts of the mouth, such as the alveolar bridge, th e tongue and the lips Among Allah's Signs: the Sun, the Moon, the Sky and the Earth Allah said,

Нј ЛіПҘ ОҺ ЛҙО’ ЛёОҙ ЛөОӨ Л¶О‘ ЛөО® ЛҙПӨ ЛҙПҳ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Лө ОІЛё ПӨНү ОёПҹОҚНҪ (The sun and the moon (run) on fixed courses.) They move in their orbit in perfect succession,

according to precise calculation that is never delayed nor disturbed,

ЛөПһ ЛёПҙ НүПҹ ОҚ ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ ЛҙО® ЛҙПӨ ЛҙПҳ Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҷ Л¶Оӯ ЛёОӘ О— ПҘЛҙ Оғ ОӮЛҙ П¬Лҙ Пҹ П°Л¶ ПҗЛҙ О’ПЁЛҙ Пі ЛөОІ ЛёПӨ НүОё Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙП» НҪ Нј Лҙ ПҘП®Лө ОӨЛҙ О’Лё ОҙЛҙ Пі ЛіПҡ ЛҙП  ЛҙП“ П°Л¶ П“ НҮПһ ЛөПӣ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ ОӯОҺЛҙ П¬Нү ПЁПҹОҚ ЛөП– Л¶О‘ ОҺ ЛҙОі (It is not for the sun to overtake the moon, nor does the night outstrip the day. The y all float,

each in an orbit.)(36:40),

Лҙ ОІЛё ПӨНү ОёПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ Лұ ОҺПЁЛҙ ПңЛҙ Оі ЛҙПһ ЛёПҙ НүПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПһ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙОҹ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ОЎ ОҺ ЛҙО’ ЛёО» Л¶П№ ОҚ ЛөП– Л¶Пҹ ОҺ ЛҙП“ НҪ Нј Л¶ ПўПҙЛ¶ П Лҙ ПҢЛё ПҹОҚ Л¶О° Пі Л¶О° ЛҙПҢ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөО® Пі Л¶ОӘ ЛёПҳ ЛҙО— ЛҙПҡ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙО« ЛұОҺ П§ ОҺ ЛҙО’ ЛёОҙ ЛөОЈ ЛҙО® ЛҙПӨ ЛҙПҳ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ ((He is the) Cleaver of the daybreak. He has ap pointed the night for resting, and the sun and the moon for calculating. Such is the measuring of the Almighty, the All-Knowing.) (6:96), Allah said,

Нј Л¶ПҘ ОҚ ЛҙОӘ ЛөО  ЛёОҙ ЛҙПі Лө О®Лҙ О Нү ОёПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ Лө ПўЛё О Нү ПЁПҹОҚЛҙ ПӯНҪ (And the Naj m and the trees prostrating.) Ibn Ja rir commented, "Scholars of Tafsir disagreed over the meaning of Allah's statement, ' And th e Naj m.' They agreed, however, that the trees mentioned here are those that stand on trunks.'' ' Ali bin Abi Talhah reported that Ibn ' Abbas said, "An-Naj m refers to the plants that lay on the ground.'' Similar was said by S a'id bin Jubayr, As-Suddi and Sufyan Ath-Thawri. This is what Ibn Jarir preferred, may Allah have mercy upon him. Muj ahid said, "An-Naj m (the star); the one that is in the sky.'' Al-Hasan and Qatadah said similarly. This is the saying that is th e most obvious, and Allah knows best, for Allah the Exalted said,

ПҰЛҙ ПЈЛҙ Пӯ Л¶О• ЛҙП® Л° ЛҙПӨ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ПҰЛҙ ПЈ ЛөПӘ ЛҙПҹ ЛөОӘ ЛөО  ЛёОҙ ЛҙПі ЛҙПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ НүПҘ ЛҙОғ ЛҙО® ЛҙО— ЛёПў ЛҙПҹ ЛҙОғ НҪ Лө ПқОҺЛҙ О’Л¶ О Лё ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ Лө ПЎП®Лө О НҠ ПЁПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ ЛөО® ЛҙПӨ ЛҙПҳ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Лө ОІЛё ПӨНү ОёПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ Л¶ОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ОҚ ЛҙПҰ НӢПЈ ЛІ О®ПҙЛ¶ ОңЛҙ ПӣЛҙ Пӯ НҠ ОҸОҒЛҙ ПӯНү ОӘПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ Лө О®Лҙ О Нү ОёПҹОҚЛҙ ПӯНјЛ¶ ОұОҺНү ПЁПҹ (See you not that whoever is in the heavens and who ever is on the earth, and the sun, and the moon, and the stars, and the mountains, and the tr ees, and the moving creatures, and many of mankind prostrate themselves to Allah.)(22:18) Alla h's statement,

Нј ЛҙПҘ ОҚ ЛҙО° Пҙ Л¶ПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҠ ЛҙОҝ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙПӯ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лҙ ПҢЛҙ П“Лҙ Оӯ Лҙ ЛҜОӮЛҙ ПӨНү ОҙПҹОҚЛҙ ПӯНҪ (And the heaven He has raised high, and He has set up the balance.) meaning the j ustice, as He said in another Ayah,

ЛҙОҗ Л° ЛҙОҳ Л¶Пң ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөПў ЛөП¬ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙПЈ ОҺ ЛҙПЁ ЛёПҹ ЛҙО° П§ ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ О–Л°Лҙ ПЁНӢ ПҙЛҙ О’Лё ПҹОҺЛ¶ О‘ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛҙ П Лө ОіЛө Оӯ ОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё П Лҙ ОіЛё ОӯЛҙ Оғ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПҹ НҪ ОҚЛҙ ПӯНјЛ¶ ПӮЛё ОҙЛ¶ ПҳЛё ПҹОҺЛ¶ О‘ ЛөОұ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ Лҙ ПЎП®Лө ПҳЛҙ ПҙЛ¶ Пҹ ЛҙПҘ ОҚ ЛҙО° Пҙ Л¶ПӨ ЛёПҹ (Indeed, We have sent Our Messengers with clea r proofs, and sent down with them the Book and the balance that mankind may keep up equity.)(57:25) Allah said here,

Нј Л¶ПҘ ОҚ ЛҙО° Пҙ Л¶ПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ЛёОҚ ЛёП® ЛҙПҗ ЛёП„ ЛҙО— НүП» ЛҙОғ НҪ (In order that you may not transgress the balance.) meaning, He created the heavens and earth in j ustice and truth so that everything is found ed on, and observing, j ustice and truth. Allah's statement,

ОҚЛҙ О°ПҙЛ¶ ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ Лё ОҚПӯЛө О®Л¶ ОҙЛё ОЁЛө О— ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ Л¶ПӮ ЛёОҙ Л¶Пҳ ЛёПҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ЛҙПҘ ЛёОҜ ЛҙП® ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛёОҚ П® ЛөПӨ Пҙ Л¶П— ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ НҪНј ЛҙПҘ (And observe the weight with equity and do no t make the balance deficient.) meaning, do not cheat in the weights and measures, but rather obser ve j ustice and fairness,

Нј Л¶ ПўПҙЛ¶ ПҳЛҙ ОҳЛё ОҙЛө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ Л¶ ОұОҺЛҙ П„Лё ОҙЛ¶ ПҳЛё ПҹОҺЛ¶ О‘ Лё ОҚП®Лө П§Л¶ ОҜЛҙ ПӯНҪ (And weigh with the true and stra ight balance.)(26:182) Allah said,

Нј Л¶ПЎ ОҺ ЛҙП§ НҮПј Л¶Пҹ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лҙ ПҢЛҙ ОҝЛҙ Пӯ ЛҙОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (And the earth He has put down (laid) for Al-Ana 'm.) Allah raised the heavens and put down, or laid, the earth and balanced it wi th firm mountains, so that it would be stable for i ts residents that live on it, i.e. the various types and kinds of creatures, different in species, shape, color and language. Ibn ' Abbas, Muj a hid, Qatadah and Ibn Zayd sa id that Al-An'am means the creatures.

НјЛІ О”Лҙ П¬Л¶ ПңЛ°Лҙ П“ ОҺЛҙ П¬ПҙЛ¶ П“НҪ (Therein are fruits,) of various colors, taste and scent,

НјЛ¶ ПЎОҺЛҙ ПӨЛё ПӣНҮ П»ОҚ ЛөО• ОҚ ЛҙО« Лө ПһЛё ОЁНү ПЁПҹОҚЛҙ ПӯНҪ (and date palms producing Akmam.) Allah mentioned the date tree here specifically because of its benefit, both fresh and dry. Ibn Jurayj repo rted that Ibn ' Abbas said said Al-Akmam, means sheathed fruit stalks. Similar was said by more tha n one of the scholars of Tafsir, it refers to the stalks that the seeds grow in to become a cluster of dates, unripe green dates then they ripen and ripen more. Allah said,

ЛөПҘ ОҺ ЛҙОӨ ЛёПі НүО® Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Л¶П’ ЛёОј ЛҙПҢ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ПӯЛө О« НҠ ОҗЛҙ ОӨЛё ПҹОҚЛҙ ПӯНҪНј (And also corn, with (its) ' Asf, and Rayhan.) ' Ali bin Abi Talhah said that Ibn ' Abbas said that in,

НјЛ¶ П’Лё ОјЛҙ ПҢЛё ПҹОҚ ПӯЛө О« НҠ ОҗЛҙ ОӨЛё ПҹОҚЛҙ ПӯНҪ (And also corn, with (its) ' Asf), ' Asf means stra w.'' Al-' Awfi reported from Ibn ' Abbas, "' Asf is green leaves cut from the stem, so it is called ' Asf when it dries out.'' Similarly, Qatadah, AdDahhak and Abu Malik said that ' Asf means stra w. Ibn ' Abbas, Muj ahid and others said that

Rayhan means leaves, while Al-Hasan said that it means sweet-scented plants. ' Ali bin Abi Talhah reported that Ibn ' Abbas said that Rayhan means green leaves. The meanings here, and Allah knows best, are the various crops that pr oduce straw, such as wheat and barley, and Rayhan are the leaves that grow on the stems.

Mankind is surrounded by Allah's Favors Allah said,

Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ НҪ (Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) meaning, "Omankind and Jinn,

which of the favors that Allah has given to you do you deny'' Muj ahid and others said this, and it is apparent when one reads what comes after it . Meaning the favors that are obvious to you while you are surrounded by them , you cannot deny or rej ect th e m . S o we s a y , ju s t a s th e believers among the Jinns said, "O Allah! None of Your favors do we deny. All praise is due to You.'' Ibn ' Abbas used to say, "Nay, our Lord!'' meaning, "None of Your favors do we deny.''

Л¶Оӯ ОҺ НүОЁ ЛҙП” ЛёПҹ ОҺ ЛҙПӣ Лі ПһЛ°Лҙ ОјЛё П Лҙ О» ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ЛҙПҰ Л° ЛҙОҙ П§ Л¶П№ ОҚ ЛҙП– ЛҙП  ЛҙО§ НҪ- ЛҙП– ЛҙП  ЛҙО§ ЛҙПӯ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ ЛіОӯ ОҺ НүП§ ПҰНӢ ПЈ ЛіОқ Л¶Оӯ ОҺ НүПЈ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ НүПҘ ОӮ ЛҙО  ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙОӯ Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ Л¶ПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘ Л¶О® ЛёПҗ ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ НҠОҸ ЛҙОӯ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП— Л¶О® ЛёОё ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ НҠОҸ Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ПҙЛ¶ ПҳЛҙ ОҳЛё П Лҙ Пі Л¶ПҰ ЛёПі ЛҙО® ЛёОӨ ЛҙО’ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙОқ ЛҙО® ЛҙПЈ-Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ПҙЛ¶ ПҗЛё О’Лҙ Пі НүП» ЛІОҘ ЛҙОҜ ЛёО® ЛҙО‘ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө П¬Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ О‘ Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘Л· О¬Лҙ ПңЛ¶ О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңЛ· О‘Лҙ Оӯ ЛҜПөОҚЛҜ Л·ПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“- ЛөОҶ ЛөПҹ ЛёОҶ П  НҠПҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө П¬Лё ПЁЛ¶ ПЈ ЛөОқ ЛөО® ЛёОЁ ЛҙПі

Лө ПҘОҺЛҙ ОҹЛё О®Лҙ ПӨПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ-Л·ПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘Л· О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңЛ· О‘Лҙ Оӯ ЛҜЛҙ П»ОҚЛҜ - ЛөПӘ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ ПўЛ°Лҙ П Лё ПӢЛё П»ОҺЛҙ Пӣ Л¶О® ЛёОӨ ЛҙО’ ЛёПҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ЛөО• ОҺ ЛҙОҢ ЛҙОё ПЁ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶Оӯ ОҚ ЛҙП® ЛҙО  ЛёПҹ ОҚ- Л·ПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘Л· О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңЛ· О‘Лҙ Оӯ ЛҜПөОҚЛҜ-Нј (14. He created man from sounding clay like the clay of pottery.) (15. And the Jinn He created from a smokeless flame of fire.) (16. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) (17. The Lord of the two easts and the Lo rd of the two wests.) (18. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) (19. He has Maraj a the two seas meeting together.) (20. Between them is a barrier which none of them can transgress.) (21. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) (22. Out of them both come out pearls and AlMarj an.) (23. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) (24. And His are AlJawar Al-Munsha'at, in the seas like A'lam.) (25. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will

you both deny)

The Creation of Humans and Jinns Allah mentions that He created mankind from clay, l ike that used in pottery, and created the Jinns from the tip of the flame of a smokeless fire . This was said by Ad-Dahhak from Ibn ' Abbas. It was also said by ' Ikrimah, Muj ahid, Al-Hasan and Ibn Zayd. Al-' Awfi reported from Ibn ' Abbas, "From the best part of the fire, fr om its smokeless flame.'' Imam Ahmad recorded that ' A'ishah said that Allah's Messenger said,

В» ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ НҠПҘ ОҺ ЛҙО  ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙП– Л¶П  ЛөО§ ЛҙПӯ Л¬Лі ОӯП®Лө П§ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛөО” ЛҙПң Л¶ОӢ ОҺ ЛҙП  ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶О– ЛҙПҳ Л¶П  ЛөО§ ПўЛө ПңЛҙ Пҹ ЛҙП’ Л¶О» ЛөПӯ ОҺНү ПӨЛ¶ ПЈ ЛөПЎ ЛҙО© ОҒ ЛҙП– Л¶П  ЛөО§ ЛҙПӯ Л¬Лі ОӯОҺЛҙ П§ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛіОқ Л¶Оӯ ОҺ ЛҙПЈВ« (The angels were created from light, the Jinns from a smokeless flame of fire, and ' Adam from what was described to you.) Muslim also collected this Hadith. Allah's statement:

Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ НҪ (Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) was explained above Allah is the Lord of the Two Easts and the Two Wests Allah said,

Нј Л¶ПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘ Л¶О® ЛёПҗ ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ НҠОҸ ЛҙОӯ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙП— Л¶О® ЛёОё ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ НҠОҸ ЛҙОӯ НҪ ((He is) the Lord of the two easts and the Lord of the two wests.) meaning the sunrise of summer and winter and the sunset of summer and wint er. Allah said in another Ayah,

НјЛ¶ ОҸЛ¶ О®Л°Лҙ ПҗЛҙ ПӨЛё ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ Л¶П• Л¶О® Л° ЛҙОё ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ НӢОҸ ЛҙО® Л¶О‘ ЛөПў Л¶Оҙ ЛёП— ЛөОғ ЛҙПј ЛҙП“ НҪ (S o, Iswear by the Lord of all the points of sunrise and sunset in the east and the west.)(70:40), referring to the different places from which the sun rises and then sets on people every day. Allah said in another Ayah,

ЛөП© ЛёО¬ Л¶ОЁ НүО— ОҺ ЛҙП“ ЛҙП® ЛөП« НүП» Л¶ОҮ ЛҙПӘ Л° ЛҙПҹ Л¶ОҮ ЛҙП» Л¶ОҸ Л¶О® ЛёПҗ ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Л¶П• Л¶О® ЛёОё ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ НҠОҸ НүОӯ НҪ Нј Лұ ПјПҙЛ¶ ПӣЛҙ Пӯ (The Lord of the east and the west; none has th e right to be worshipped but He. S o take Him alone as a trustee.)(73:9), referring to the differ ent places of sunrise and sunset and the benefits that this variation brings to the created, mankind and Jinns,

Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ НҪ (Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny)

Allah created Different Types of Water Allah said,

Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ПҙЛ¶ ПҳЛҙ ОҳЛё П Лҙ Пі Л¶ПҰ ЛёПі ЛҙО® ЛёОӨ ЛҙО’ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙОқ ЛҙО® ЛҙПЈ НҪ (He has Maraj a the two seas), or let them loose, according to Ibn ' Abbas. Allah's statement,

Нј Л¶ПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПҙ Л¶Пҳ ЛҙОҳ ЛёП  ЛҙПі НҪ (meeting together.) Ibn Zayd said, "He prevents them from meeting by the dividing barrier He placed between them to separate them.'' The two seas are the fresh and salty waters, the former coming from running rivers . We discussed this topic in Surat Al-Furqan when explaining Allah's statement;

ЛІОҸ ЛёО¬ ЛҙПӢ ОҚЛҙ О¬Л°Лҙ П« Л¶ПҰ ЛёПі ЛҙО® ЛёОӨ ЛҙО’ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙОқ ЛҙО® ЛҙПЈ ПҜЛ¶ О¬Нү ПҹОҚ ЛҙП® ЛөП« ЛҙПӯ НҪ ЛІО• ОҚ ЛҙО® ЛөП“ Лұ ОҚО®Лё О Л¶ ОЈЛҙ Пӯ ЛұОҺ О§ ЛҙОҜ ЛёО® ЛҙО‘ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө П¬Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ О‘ ЛҙПһ ЛҙПҢ ЛҙОҹ ЛҙПӯ ЛІОқ ОҺ ЛҙОҹ ЛөОғ ЛІОў ЛёП  Л¶ПЈ ОҚ ЛҙО¬ Л° ЛҙП« ЛҙПӯ Нј ЛұОҚ Оӯ П® ЛөО  ЛёОӨ НүПЈ (And it is He Who has let free the two seas: one palatable and sweet, and the other salty and bitter; and He has set a barrier and a comple te partition between them.)(25:53) Allah said,

Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ПҙЛ¶ ПҗЛё О’Лҙ Пі НүП» ЛІОҘ ЛҙОҜ ЛёО® ЛҙО‘ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө П¬Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ О‘НҪ (Between them is a barrier which none of th em can transgress.) meaning, He has placed a barrier of land between these two types of wate rs, so that they do not transgress upon each other, which would spoil the characterist ics they were created with. Allah said,

Нј Лө ПҘОҺЛҙ ОҹЛё О®Лҙ ПӨПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ ЛөОҶ ЛөПҹ ЛёОҶ П  НҠПҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө П¬Лё ПЁЛ¶ ПЈ ЛөОқ ЛөО® ЛёОЁ ЛҙПі НҪ (Out of them both come out pearls and Al-Marj a n. ) pearls are well-known. As for Marj an they say it means small pearls. Muj ahi d, Qatadah, Abu Ruzayn, Ad-Dahhak said it, and it h as also b e e n r e p o r t e d f r o m 'A l i . I t w a s a l s o s a i d t h a t i t me a n s l a r g e , p r e c i o u s p e a r l s , t h i s w a s mentioned by Ibn Jarir from some of the Salaf. Ibn Abi Hatim recorded from Ibn ' Abbas who said, "When it rains, the oysters in the sea open t heir mouths. What falls in them, the drops,

turns into pearls.'' Its chain of narrators is Sahih. Since this type of adornment is a favor from Allah to the people of earth, He reminded them of i t,

НӢО¬ ЛҙПң ЛөО— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ НҪНј Л¶ПҘ ОҺ ЛҙО‘ (Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) Allah said,

Нј ЛөО• ОҺ ЛҙОҢ ЛҙОё ПЁ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶Оӯ ОҚ ЛҙП® ЛҙО  ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөПӘ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (And His are Al-Jawar Al-Munsha'at), meaning the ships that float,

НјЛ¶ О®Лё ОӨЛҙ О’Лё ПҹОҚ П°Л¶ П“НҪ (in the seas), Muj ahid said, "Whatever ship hoists a sail, it is from Munsha'at, if it d oes not hoist a sail, it is not from the Muns ha'at.'' Qatadah said, "Al-Munsha'at means created.'' Others said that it is Al-Munshi'at meaning, "launched.''

НјЛ¶ ПўЛ°Лҙ П Лё ПӢНҮ П»ОҺЛҙ ПӣНҪ (like A'lam.) This means, they are like mountains with their great size, and it also refers to the trade and commercial services they make possible, t ransporting cargo from one area to another and from one province to another. Ships provide var ious benefits for people, including transporting different types of goods they need. Th erefore,

Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ НҪ (Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) ЛіПҘ ОҺ ЛҙП“ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лё ПҙЛҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛёПҰ ЛҙПЈ НҠПһ ЛөПӣ НҪ- ПӯЛө О« ЛҙПҡ НӢО‘ ЛҙОӯ ЛөПӘ ЛёОҹ ЛҙПӯ П°Лҙ ПҳЛё О’Лҙ ПіЛҙ Пӯ НӢО‘ ЛҙОӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ Л¶ПЎ ОҚ ЛҙО® ЛёПӣ Л¶П№ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ ПһЛ°Лё П Лҙ О Лё ПҹОҚНј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө Пң ЛҙП® ЛөП« ЛіПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі НүПһ ЛөПӣ Л¶ОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Л¶О• ЛҙП® Л° ЛҙПӨ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ПҰЛҙ ПЈ ЛөПӘ ЛөПҹ ЛҙО„ ЛёОҙ ЛҙПі НҪ Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ ЛіПҘ ЛёО„ ЛҙО· П°Л¶ П“ (26. Whatsoever is on it (the earth) will peri sh.) (27. And the Face of your Lord Dhul-Jalal walIkram will remain forever.) (28. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny)

(29. Whosoever is in the heavens and on the earth b egs of Him. Every day He is (engaged) in some affair!) (30. Then which of the bles sings of your Lord will you both deny)

Allah is the Ever Living, Free of all Need Allah states that all the residents of earth will p erish and die. The residents of the heavens will die, except whomever Allah wills . Only Allah's Honorable Face will remain, because our Lord,

the Exalted, the Blessed, is the Ever Living Who never dies. Qatadah said, "First, Allah mentioned His creatures and then He said that al l of this will perish.'' And in the reported supplication: ' O You the Ever Living Who sust ains all that exists! O You Who created the heavens and the earth without precedence. O You, Wh o Owns the glory and the honor, none has the right to be worshipped except You. We s eek refuge with Your mercy. Grant Us s ucces s in all of our matters. Please, do not abandon us to rely on ourselves even for an instant nor on any of Your creation.'' Ash-Sha'bi said, "When you have recited,

Нј ЛіПҘ ОҺ ЛҙП“ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лё ПҙЛҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛёПҰ ЛҙПЈ НҠПһ ЛөПӣ НҪ (Whatsoever is on it (the earth) will perish.) do not stop, continue reading,

Нј Л¶ПЎ ОҚ ЛҙО® ЛёПӣ Л¶П№ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ ПһЛ°Лё П Лҙ О Лё ПҹОҚ ПӯЛө О« ЛҙПҡ НӢО‘ ЛҙОӯ ЛөПӘ ЛёОҹ ЛҙПӯ П°Лҙ ПҳЛё О’Лҙ ПіЛҙ ПӯНҪ (And the Face of your Lord Dhul- Jalal wal-Ikram will remain foreve r.)'' This Ayah is similar to Allah's statement,

НјЛө ПӘЛҙ П¬Лё ОҹЛҙ Пӯ НүП» Л¶ОҮ ЛІПҡ Л¶Пҹ ОҺ ЛҙП« ЛіЛҜ ЛёП° ЛҙО· НҠПһ ЛөПӣ НҪ (Everything will perish save His Face.)(28:88) In this Ayah, Alla h describes His Noble Face as being Dhul-Jalal wal-Ikram, indicating that He is Worthy of being revered, and thus, never defied; and obeyed, and thus, never disobeyed,

Л¶ О“ОҚЛҙ ОӘЛҙ ПҗЛё ПҹОҺЛ¶ О‘ ПўЛө П¬Нү О‘Лҙ Оӯ ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөПӢ ЛёОӘ ЛҙПі ЛҙПҰ Пі Л¶О¬ НүПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҠ ЛҙПЈ ЛҙПҡ ЛҙОҙ ЛёП” ЛҙП§ ЛёО® Л¶О’ ЛёО» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ НҪ НјЛө ПӘЛҙ П¬Лё ОҹЛҙ Пӯ ЛҙПҘ Пӯ ЛөОӘ Пі Л¶О® ЛөПі НӢП° Л¶Оё ЛҙПҢ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ (And keep yourself patiently with those who call on their Lord morning and afternoon, seeking His Face.)(18:28), And as He said about those giving charity:

НјЛ¶ ПӘНү П ПҹОҚ Л¶ПӘ ЛёОҹ ЛҙП® Л¶Пҹ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛөПӨ Л¶ПҢ ЛёП„ ЛөП§ ОҺЛҙ ПӨНү П§Л¶ ОҮНҪ (We feed you seeking Allah's Face only.)(76:9) Ibn ' Abbas commented on the meaning of DhulJalal wal-Ikram, by saying, "Owner of greatness and pride.'' After Allah stated that all of the

inhabitants of the earth will die and end up in the Hereafter when He, Dhul-Jalal wal-Ikram,

will j udge them by His fair j udgement, He said,

П»ОҚЛҙ ЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ НҪНј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ (Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) Allah said,

ЛҙП® ЛөП« ЛіПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі НүПһ ЛөПӣ Л¶ОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Л¶О• ЛҙП® Л° ЛҙПӨ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ ПҰЛҙ ПЈ ЛөПӘ ЛөПҹ ЛҙО„ ЛёОҙ ЛҙПі НҪ Нј ЛіПҘ ЛёО„ ЛҙО· П°Л¶ П“ (Whosoever is in the heavens and on the earth begs of Him. Every day He is (engaged) in some affair.) In this Ayah, Allah affirms that He is Rich , Free of all wants for anyone else and that all creatures stand in need of Him, in all conditions and situations. They all seek His help willingly or unwillingly. Everyday, He is engaged in some affair. Al-A'mash reported from Muj ahid, from ' Ubayd bin ' Umayr,

НјЛі ПҘЛё О„Лҙ О· П°Л¶ П“ ЛҙП® ЛөП« ЛіПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі НүПһ ЛөПӣ НҪ (Every day He is (engaged) in some affair.) He said, "Of His affairs is that He answers the supplicant, or gives to the one requesting, or re moving adversity, or cures the one seeking to be cured.''

ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ Л¶ПҘ ЛҙПј ЛҙПҳ НүОң Пҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ П¬НҠ ПіЛҙ Оғ ЛёПў ЛөПң ЛҙПҹ ЛөПҚ ЛөО® ЛёП” ЛҙПЁ ЛҙОі НҪ ПҘЛҙ Оғ ЛёПў ЛөОҳ ЛёПҢ ЛҙП„ ЛҙОҳ ЛёОі ОҚ Л¶ПҘ Л¶ОҮ Л¶ОІ П§ Л¶П№ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ НӢПҰ Л¶О  ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙО® ЛҙОё ЛёПҢ ЛҙПӨ Пі Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ЛҙП» ЛёОҚ Пӯ ЛөО¬ ЛөП” П§ ОҺ ЛҙП“ Л¶ОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Л¶О• ЛҙП® Л° ЛҙПӨ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ Л¶О® Л° ЛҙП„ ЛёП— ЛҙОғ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛёОҚ Пӯ ЛөО¬ ЛөП” ПЁ ЛҙО— Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ ЛіПҰ Л° ЛҙП„ ЛёП  ЛөОҙ Л¶О‘ НүП» Л¶ОҮ ЛҙПҘ Пӯ ЛөО¬ ЛөП” ПЁ ЛҙО— ЛҙПј ЛҙП“ ЛІОұ ОҺ ЛҙОӨ ЛөП§ ЛҙПӯ ЛіОӯ ОҺ НүП§ ПҰНӢ ПЈ ЛІП… ОҚ ЛҙП® ЛөО· ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңЛё ПҙЛҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛөПһ ЛҙОі ЛёО® ЛөПі Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ Л¶ ПҘОҚЛҙ О®Л¶ ОјЛҙ ОҳПЁЛҙ О— (31. We shall attend to you, O Thaqalan!) (32. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) (33. Oassembly of Jinn and men! If you are able to pass beyond the zones of the heavens and the earth, then pass beyond (the m)! But you will never be able to pass them,

except with authority (from Allah)!) (34. Then wh ich of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) (35. There will be sent against you both, Shuwaz of fire and Nuhas, and you will not be able to defend yourselves.) (36. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) ur Lord will you both deny)

A Warning for Humans and Jinn Ibn Jurayj said that the Ayah,

НјЛё ПўЛө ПңЛҙ Пҹ ЛөПҚ ЛөО® ЛёП” ЛҙПЁ ЛҙОі НҪ (We shall attend to you,) means, ' We shall j udge you,' while Al-Bukhari said that it means, "We shall recompense you. Surely, nothing will busy Allah from attending to anything else.'' This type of s peech pattern is common in the Arabic lang uage. For example, one would s ay, "Iwill attend to you,'' even when one is not busy with any thing else. Allah's saying;

НјЛ¶ ПҘЛҙ ПјЛҙ ПҳНү ОңПҹОҚ ОҺЛҙ П¬НҠ ПіЛҙ ОғНҪ (Oyou Thaqalan!) refers to the humans and the Jinn s, as in the Hadith;

В»ПҰЛё ПҙЛҙ П Лҙ ПҳНү ОңПҹОҚ ОҺНү ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ ЛіЛҜ ЛёПІ ЛҙО· НҠПһ ЛөПӣ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лө ПҢЛҙ ПӨЛё ОҙЛҙ ПіВ« (Everyone will be able to hear it, except the Th aqalayn.) In another narration that explains it,

the Prophet said,

В»ПҰЛ¶ О Лё ПҹОҚЛҙ Пӯ ЛҙОІ ЛёП§ Л¶ОҲ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ОҺНү ПҹЛ¶ ОҮВ« (...except mankind and the Jinns.) Allah said,

Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ НҪ (Then which of the blessings of yo ur Lord will you both deny), then,

ЛёОҚ Пӯ ЛөО¬ ЛөП” ПЁ ЛҙО— ПҘЛҙ Оғ ЛёПў ЛөОҳ ЛёПҢ ЛҙП„ ЛҙОҳ ЛёОі ОҚ Л¶ПҘ Л¶ОҮ Л¶ОІ П§ Л¶П№ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ НӢПҰ Л¶О  ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙО® ЛҙОё ЛёПҢ ЛҙПӨ Пі НҪ ЛөО¬ ЛөП” П§ ОҺ ЛҙП“ Л¶ОҪ ЛёОӯ НҮП» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ Л¶О• ЛҙП® Л° ЛҙПӨ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ Л¶О® Л° ЛҙП„ ЛёП— ЛҙОғ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ ЛҙПҘ Пӯ ЛөО¬ ЛөП” ПЁ ЛҙО— ЛҙП» ЛёОҚ Пӯ Нј ЛіПҰ Л° ЛҙП„ ЛёП  ЛөОҙ Л¶О‘ НүП» Л¶ОҮ (Oassembly of Jinn and men! If you are able to pass beyond the zones of the heavens and the earth, then pass beyond (them)! But you will neve r be able to pass them, except with authority (from Allah)!) meaning, ' you will never be able to escape Allah's orders and decrees, because it encompasses you. You will never be able to avoi d or avert His rule and j udgement over you,

you are surrounded by it wherever you may be.' This is also about the Gathering when the angels, comprising seven lines in every direction, will surround the creatures. None of the creatures will be able to escape on that Day,

Нј ЛіПҰ Л° ЛҙП„ ЛёП  ЛөОҙ Л¶О‘ НүП» Л¶ОҮ НҪ (except with authority) meaning, exce pt with the comman dment from Allah,

ЛҙПЈ ЛёП® ЛҙПі ЛөПҰ Л° ЛҙОҙ П§ Л¶П№ ОҚ Лө ПқП®Лө ПҳЛҙ ПіНҪ НҠО® ЛҙП” ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҰ ЛёПі ЛҙОғ ЛіО¬ Л¶ОҢ- ЛҙОӯ ЛҙОҜ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙП» НүПј ЛҙПӣ Нј НҠО® ЛҙПҳ ЛҙОҳ ЛёОҙ ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛіО¬ Л¶ОҢ ЛҙПЈ ЛёП® ЛҙПі ЛҙПҡ НӢО‘ ЛҙОӯ П°Лҙ ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ (On that Day man will say: "Where (is the refuge ) to flee'' No! There is no refuge! Unto your Lord will be the place of rest that Day.)(75:10-12),

П®Лө О’Лҙ ОҙЛҙ Пӣ Лҙ ПҰПіЛ¶ О¬Нү ПҹОҚЛҙ ПӯНҪ ОҺЛҙ П¬Л¶ П Лё ОңЛ¶ ПӨЛ¶ О‘ ЛіО” ЛҙОҢ НӢПҙ ЛҙОі ЛөЛҜ ОҒ ЛҙО° ЛҙОҹ Л¶О• ОҺ ЛҙОҢ НӢПҙ НүОҙ Пҹ ОҚ ЛёОҚ ОҺЛҙ ПӨНү П§Лҙ О„Лҙ Пӣ ЛіПў Л¶О» ОҺ ЛҙПӢ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ Л¶ПӘ НүП  Пҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҰ НӢПЈ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛҙПҹ ОҺНү ПЈ ЛІО” НүПҹ Л¶О« ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛөПҳ ЛҙП« ЛёО® ЛҙО— ЛҙПӯ ЛҙПҡ Л¶ОҢ Л° ЛҙПҹ ЛёПӯ ЛөОғ Лұ ОҺПӨЛ¶ П Лё ПҲЛө ПЈ Л¶Пһ ЛёПҙ НүПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҰ НӢПЈ ОҺЛұ ПҢЛҙ П„Л¶ П— ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛөП« П® ЛөОҹ ЛөПӯ ЛёО– ЛҙПҙ Л¶Оё ЛёПҸ ЛөОғ Нј ЛҙПҘ Пӯ ЛөОӘ Л¶П  Л° ЛҙО§ ОҺЛҙ П¬ПҙЛ¶ П“ ЛёПў ЛөП« Л¶Оӯ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ ЛөОҗ Л° ЛҙОӨ ЛёО» ЛҙОғ (And those who earned evil deeds, the recompense of an evil deed is the like thereof, and humiliating disgrace will cover them . No defender will they have fr om Allah. Their faces will be covered as it were with pieces from the darknes s of night. They are the dwellers of Fire, they will abide therein forever.)( 10:27) Allah's statement,

ЛҙПј ЛҙП“ ЛІОұ ОҺ ЛҙОӨ ЛөП§ ЛҙПӯ ЛіОӯ ОҺ НүП§ ПҰНӢ ПЈ ЛІП… ОҚ ЛҙП® ЛөО· ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңЛё ПҙЛҙ П Лҙ ПӢ ЛөПһ ЛҙОі ЛёО® ЛөПі НҪ Нј Л¶ ПҘОҚЛҙ О®Л¶ ОјЛҙ ОҳПЁЛҙ О— (There will be sent against you both, Shuwaz of fire and Nuhas, and you will not be able to defend yourselves.) ' Ali bin Abi Talhah reported from Ibn ' Abbas that Shuwaz is the f lame of fire. Abu Salih said, "It is the flame above the fir e below the smoke.'' Ad-Dahhak said,

НјЛі ОӯОҺНү П§ ПҰНӢ ПЈ ЛІП… ОҚ ЛҙП® ЛөО· НҪ (Shuwaz of fire) "Aflood of fire.'' Allah said;

Нј ЛІОұ ОҺ ЛҙОӨ ЛөП§ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (and Nuhas) ' Ali bin Abi Talhah reported from Ibn ' Abbas; "The smoke of the fire.'' Similar was reported from Abu Salih, S a'id bin Jubayr and Abu Sinan. Ibn Jarir said that the Arabs used to call the smoke of the fire, Nuhas and Nihas. But he said that the scholars of Qur'anic recitation said that in this Ayah, the word recited is Nuha s. Muj ahid said, "Molten brass poured over their heads.'' Qatadah held the same view. Ad-Dahhak said,"Nuhas is liquid copper.'' The Ayah means,

' if you, mankind and the Jinns, try to escape on the Day of Resurrection, then the angels,

including those among them who guard Hellfire, wi ll bring you back by directing smoke of fire and molten brass on you.' Allah's statement,

Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП” Л¶П§ ОҚ ЛҙО® Л¶Ој ЛҙОҳ ПЁ ЛҙО— ЛҙПј ЛҙП“ НҪ (and you will not be able to de fend yourselves. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ П«НӢ ОӘПҹОҺЛҙ Пӣ ЛұО“ ЛҙО© ЛёОӯ ЛҙПӯ Лё О–Лҙ П§ОҺЛҙ ПңЛҙ П“ Лө ЛҜОӮЛҙ ПӨНү ОҙПҹОҚ Л¶О– НүПҳ ЛҙОё П§ ОҚ ОҚЛҙ О«Л¶ ОҲЛҙ П“НҪ Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ ЛІОІ П§ Л¶ОҮ Л¶ПӘ Л¶О’ П§ ЛҙО« ПҰЛҙ ПӢ ЛөПһ ЛҙОҢ Л° ЛёОҙ ЛөПі НүП» ЛіО¬ Л¶ОҢ ЛҙПЈ ЛёП® ЛҙПҙ ЛҙП“НҮПҘ ОӮ ЛҙОҹ - Л·ПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘Л· О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңЛ· О‘Лҙ Оӯ ЛҜПөОҚЛҜ- ЛёПў ЛөП¬ Л° ЛҙПӨ Пҙ Л¶Оҙ Л¶О‘ ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөПЈ Л¶О® ЛёО  ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөП‘ ЛҙО® ЛёПҢ ЛөПі Л¶ПЎ ОҚ ЛҙОӘ ЛёП— ЛёП» ОҚ ЛҙПӯ П° Л¶О» ОҚ ЛҙП® НүПЁ Пҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ЛөО¬ ЛҙО§ ЛёОҶ ЛөПҙ ЛҙП“- ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңЛ· О‘Лҙ Оӯ ЛҜПөОҚЛҜ Л·ПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘Л· О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О—-ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөПЈ Л¶О® ЛёО  ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ОҺЛҙ П¬Л¶ О‘ ЛөОҸ Л·О¬ ЛҙПң ЛөПі П°Л¶ ОҳНү ПҹОҚ ЛөПў НүПЁ ЛҙП¬ ЛҙОҹ Л¶П© Л¶О¬ Л° ЛҙП« ПҙЛ¶ ПӨЛҙ ОЈ ЛҙПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘ ЛҙПӯ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ О‘ ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөП“ П® ЛөП„ ЛҙПіЛіПҘ ОҚ ЛҜ ЛіПў- ЛҜПөОҚЛҜ Л·ПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“

Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘Л· О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңЛ· О‘Лҙ Оӯ-Нј (37. Then when the heaven is rent asunder, and it b ecomes Wardah like Dihan.) (38. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you bo th deny) (39. S o, on that Day he will not be questioned about his sin, (neither) human nor Ji nn.) (40. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) (41. The criminals wi ll be known by their marks, and they will be seized by their foreheads and feet.) (42. Then wh ich of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) (43. This is the Hell which the criminals denied.) (44. They will go between it and Hamim An!) (45. Then which of the blessing s of your Lord will you both deny)

The Horrors of the Day of Resurrection Allah said,

НјЛө ЛҜОӮЛҙ ПӨНү ОҙПҹОҚ Л¶О– НүПҳ ЛҙОё П§ ОҚ ОҚЛҙ О«Л¶ ОҲЛҙ П“НҪ (Then when the heaven is rent asunder,) on the Day of Resurrection; this meaning is clear in this and similar Ayat, such as,

Нј ЛІО” ЛҙПҙ Л¶П« ОҚ ЛҙПӯ ЛіО¬ Л¶ОҢ ЛҙПЈ ЛёП® ЛҙПі ЛҙП° Л¶П¬ ЛҙП“ Лө ЛҜОӮЛҙ ПӨНү ОҙПҹОҚ Л¶О– НүПҳ ЛҙОё П§ ОҚ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (And the heaven will be rent asunder, for that Day it (the heaven) will be frail and torn up.)(69:16),

ЛөО” ЛҙПң Л¶ОҢ Л° ЛҙП  ЛҙПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПқ НӢО° ЛөП§ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ ПўЛ°Лҙ ПӨЛҙ ПҗЛё ПҹОҺЛ¶ О‘ Лө ЛҜОӮЛҙ ПӨНү ОҙПҹОҚ ЛөП– НүПҳ ЛҙОё ЛҙО— ЛҙПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі ЛҙПӯ НҪ Нј ЛұПј Пі Л¶О° ПЁ ЛҙО— (And (remember) the Day when the heaven shall be re nt asunder with clouds, and the angels will be sent down, with a grand descending.)(25:25) and,

НҪ ЛёО– НүПҳ ЛҙОё П§ ОҚ Лө ЛҜОӮЛҙ ПӨНү ОҙПҹОҚ ОҚЛҙ О«Л¶ ОҮ-Нј ЛёО– НүПҳ ЛөОЈ ЛҙПӯ ОҺЛҙ П¬НӢ О‘Лҙ О®Л¶ Пҹ ЛёО– ЛҙП§ Л¶О« ЛҙОғ ЛҙПӯ (When the heaven is split asunder, and listens to and obeys its Lord -- and it must do so.)(84:12) Allah's statement,

Нј Л¶ПҘ ОҺ ЛҙП« НӢОӘ Пҹ ОҺ ЛҙПӣ ЛұО“ ЛҙО© ЛёОӯ ЛҙПӯ ЛёО– ЛҙП§ ОҺ ЛҙПң ЛҙП“ НҪ (and it becomes Wardah like Dihan.) This means they will melt j ust as sediment and silver are melted when heated. And they will be colored, j ust as dies stain something, sometimes red,

sometimes yellow, or blue, or green. This demonstra tes the extent of the horrors of the Mighty Day of Resurrection. As-Suddi said, "It will be as rosy color and as filth oil.'' Muj ahid said Нј Л¶ПҘ ОҺ ЛҙП« НӢОӘ Пҹ ОҺ ЛҙПӣ НҪ (like Dihan), "Like the colo rs of dyes.'' Allah said;

Нј НҮПҘ ОӮ ЛҙОҹ ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ ЛІОІ П§ Л¶ОҮ Л¶ПӘ Л¶О’ П§ ЛҙО« ПҰЛҙ ПӢ ЛөПһ ЛҙЛ° ЛёОҙ ЛөПі НүП» ЛіО¬ Л¶ОҢ ЛҙПЈ ЛёП® ЛҙПҙ ЛҙП“ НҪ (S o on that Day he will not be questioned abou t his sins, (neither) hum an nor Jinn.) this is similar to His saying;

ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөПҳ Л¶П„ ПЁ ЛҙПі ЛҙП» ЛөПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПі ОҚЛҙ О¬Л°Лҙ П«НҪ- Лҙ ПҘПӯЛө ОӯЛ¶ О¬Лҙ ОҳЛё ПҢЛҙ ПҙЛҙ П“ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ ЛҙПҹ ЛөПҘ ЛҙО« ЛёОҶ ЛөПі ЛҙП» ЛҙПӯ Нј (That will be a Day when they shall not speak, and they will not be permitted to put forth any excuse.)(77:35-36) This is the case at the time, then all the creatures will be questioned about their deeds. Allah said;

Лҙ ПҰПҙЛ¶ ПҢЛҙ ПӨЛё ОҹЛҙ Оғ ЛёПў ЛөП¬ НүПЁ ЛҙП  ЛҙЛ° ЛёОҙ ЛҙПЁ ЛҙПҹ ЛҙПҡ НӢО‘ ЛҙОӯ ЛҙП® ЛҙП“ НҪ-Нј Лҙ ПҘП®Лө П Лҙ ПӨЛё ПҢЛҙ Пі Лё ОҚП®Лө П§ОҺЛҙ Пӣ ОҺНү ПӨЛҙ ПӢ (S o, by your Lord, We shall certainly call all of t hem to account. For all that they used to do.)(15:92-93) Qatadah said, "On that they will be questioned and then their mouths will be sealed and their hands and feet will reveal what they used to do.'' Allah the Exalted said,

НјЛё ПўЛө П¬Л°Лҙ ПӨПҙЛ¶ ОҙЛ¶ О‘ ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөПЈ Л¶О® ЛёО  ЛөПӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛөП‘ ЛҙО® ЛёПҢ ЛөПі НҪ (The criminals will be known by their marks,) i. e., by special marks that distinguish them. AlHasan and Qatadah said, "They will be known by their dark faces and their blue eyes.'' Isay that

this contrasts to the marks that will distinguish t he believers, such as the light that will appear on the parts of the body that they used to wash whi le performing ablution. Allah said,

НјЛ¶ ПЎОҚЛҙ ОӘЛё П—НҮ П»ОҚЛҙ Пӯ П° Л¶О» ОҚ ЛҙП® НүПЁ Пҹ ОҺ Л¶О‘ ЛөО¬ ЛҙО§ ЛёОҶ ЛөПҙ ЛҙП“ НҪ (and they will be s eized by their foreheads an d feet.) meaning, the angels of punishment will bend their heads down to their feet and throw th em like this into the Hellfire. Al-A'mash said that Ibn ' Abbas said, "He will be taken by his foreh ead and his feet and be broken j ust as a stick is broken to be thrown into an oven.'' Allah said,

ЛҙОҹ Л¶П© Л¶О¬ Л° ЛҙП« НҪНј Лҙ ПҘП®Лө ПЈЛ¶ О®Лё О Лө ПӨЛё ПҹОҚ ОҺЛҙ П¬Л¶ О‘ ЛөОҸ НӢО¬ ЛҙПң ЛөПі П°Л¶ ОҳНү ПҹОҚ ЛөПў НүПЁ ЛҙП¬ (This is the Hell which the criminals denied.) mean ing, ' this is the Fire that you used to deny existed that it; now you see it before your eyes !' While being chastised, criticized, disgraced and belittled, this will be said to the disbeliever s.

Нј ЛіПҘ ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ Лі ПўПҙЛ¶ ПӨЛҙ ОЈ ЛҙПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘ ЛҙПӯ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ О‘ ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөП“ П® ЛөП„ ЛҙПі НҪ (They will go between it and Hamim An!) meaning, th ey will sometimes be punished with fire and they will sometimes be given Hamim which is a d rink like molten copper tearing their intestines and internal organs,

Лҙ ПҘП®Лө О’Лҙ ОӨЛё ОҙЛө Пі Лө ПһЛ¶ ОҙЛ°Лҙ П Нү ОҙПҹОҚПӯ ЛёПў Л¶П¬ Л¶Пҳ Л° ЛҙПЁ ЛёПӢ ЛҙОғ П°Л¶ П“ Лө ПһЛ°Лҙ П Лё ПҸНҮ П»ОҚ Л¶О« Л¶ОҮ НҪ- П°Л¶ П“ Нј ЛҙПҘ Пӯ ЛөО® ЛҙО  ЛёОҙ ЛөПі Л¶Оӯ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ НүПў ЛөОӣ Л¶Пў Пҙ Л¶ПӨ ЛҙОӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ (When the iron collars will be rounded over thei r necks, and the chains, they shall be dragged along, in the Hamim, then they will be burned in the Fire.)(40:71-72) Allah said НјЛі ПҘОҚЛҙ ЛҜНҪ (An) meaning hot, due to the fierce and intens e heat that is impossible to bear. Ibn ' Abbas said;

Нј ЛіПҘ ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ Лі ПўПҙЛ¶ ПӨЛҙ ОЈ ЛҙПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘ ЛҙПӯ ОҺЛҙ П¬Лҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ О‘ ЛҙПҘ П® ЛөП“ П® ЛөП„ ЛҙПі НҪ (They will go between it and Hamim An!) "Tha t has reached the ultimate temperature and boiling fiercely.'' Similar was said by Muj ahi d, S a'id bin Jubayr, Ad-Dahhak, Al-Hasan, AthThawri and As-Suddi. Qatadah also commented, "Its boiling started when Allah created the

heavens and the earth!'' Muhammad bin Ka'b Al-Qurazi said, "The (disobedient) servan t will be seized by the forehead and stirred in that boil ing water until his flesh melts and only the bones and the eyes in his head remain. This is the meaning of Allah's statement,

Нј Лҙ ПҘПӯЛө О®Лҙ О Лё ОҙЛө Пі Л¶Оӯ ОҺ НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“ НүПў ЛөОӣ Л¶Пў Пҙ Л¶ПӨ ЛҙОӨ ЛёПҹ ОҚ П°Л¶ П“НҪ (In the Hamim, then they will be burned in th e Fire.)(40:72) And Al-Hamim Al-An means hot.''

There is another report from Al-Qurazi;

НјЛі ПҘОҚЛҙ ЛҜ ЛіПў Пҙ Л¶ПӨ ЛҙОЈ НҪ (Hamim An) that it means "prepared. '' This is a ls o th e v ie w o f I b n Z a y d . A n d s a y in g th a t it means "prepared'' does not contradict the first rep ort from Al-Qurazi which says that it means hot, for Allah said:

Нј ЛіО” ЛҙПҙ Л¶П§ ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ ЛіПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙПӢ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ П°Лҙ ПҳЛё ОҙЛө О—НҪ (They will be given to drink from a spring, Aniyah.) (88:5) which means severe unbearable heat, and His saying;

Нј ЛөПӘ Л° ЛҙП§ Л¶ОҮ ЛҙПҰ Пі Л¶О® Л¶ПҲ Л° ЛҙП§ ЛҙО® ЛёПҙ ЛҙПҸ НҪ (not to wait for it to be prepared)(33:53) which me ans properly cooking and preparing it. S o His saying;

НјЛі ПҘОҚЛҙ ЛҜ ЛіПў Пҙ Л¶ПӨ ЛҙОЈ НҪ (Hamim An.) Hamim, that is very hot. Surely, punishing the disobedient criminals as well as favoring those who had Taqwa, is from Allah's gr ace, mercy, j ustice, compassion and kindness for His creatures. His warnings against His torment and punishment , such as mentioned in these Ayat, should encourage all creatures to abando n the Shirk and disobedience they engage in,

and this is why Allah reminded them of this favor;

Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ НҪ (Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) Л¶ПӘ НӢО‘ ЛҙОӯ ЛҙПЎ ОҺ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПЈ ЛҙП‘ ОҺ ЛҙО§ ЛёПҰ ЛҙПӨ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙПӯ НҪ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ОҳНү ПЁЛҙ Оҹ Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ ЛіПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПЁ ЛёП“ ЛҙОғ ОӮ ЛҙО— ОҚ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙО« Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ Л¶ПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПі Л¶О® ЛёО  ЛҙО— Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ ПӢ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ П¬ПҙЛ¶ П“ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ Л¶ПҘ ОҺ ЛҙОҹ ЛёПӯ ЛҙОҜ ЛіО” ЛҙП¬ Л¶Пң Л° ЛҙП“ НӢПһ ЛөПӣ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ П¬ПҙЛ¶ П“ ЛөО—Нј Л¶ПҘ ОҺ ЛҙО‘ НӢО¬ ЛҙПң (46. But for him who fears the standing before his Lord, there will be two Gardens.) (47. Then which of the blessings of your Lo rd will you both deny) (48. With Afnan.) (49. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) (50. In them (both) will be two springs flowing (free).) (51. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) (52. In them (both) will be every kind of fruit in pairs.) (53. Then wh ich of the blessings of yo ur Lord will you both deny)

The Delight of Those Who have Taqwa in Paradise Allah the Exalted said,

НјЛ¶ ПӘНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ ЛҙПЎ ОҺ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПЈ ЛҙП‘ ОҺ ЛҙО§ ЛёПҰ ЛҙПӨ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙПӯ НҪ (But for him who fears the standing before his Lord ,) on the Day of Resurrection,

Нј ПҜ ЛҙП® ЛҙП¬ ЛёПҹ ОҚ Л¶ПҰ ЛҙПӢ ЛҙОІ ЛёП” НүПЁ Пҹ ОҚ П°Лҙ П¬Лҙ П§Лҙ ПӯНҪ (And restrained himself from th e desires.) (79:40), and does not indulge nor prefe r this worldly life. He who knows that the Hereafter is better an d more lasting, so he fulfills what his Lord ordered him and stays away from His prohibitions , then he will earn two gardens from his Lord on the Day of Resurrection. Al-Bukhari reco rded that ' Abdullah bin Qays said that the Messenger of Allah said,

В» Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ОҳНү ПЁЛҙ ОҹЛҙ Пӯ Л¬ ОҺ ЛҙПӨ Л¶П¬ Пҙ Л¶П“ ОҺЛҙ ПЈЛҙ Пӯ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө П¬Лө ОҳЛҙ ПҙЛ¶ П§ОҒ ЛіО” НүПҖ Л¶П“ ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ОҳНү ПЁЛҙ Оҹ ЛҙПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘ ЛҙПӯ Л¶ПЎ ЛёП® ЛҙПҳ ЛёПҹ ОҚ ЛҙПҰ ЛёПҙ ЛҙО‘ ОҺЛҙ ПЈЛҙ Пӯ Л¬ ОҺ ЛҙПӨ Л¶П¬ Пҙ Л¶П“ ОҺЛҙ ПЈЛҙ Пӯ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө П¬Лө ОҳЛҙ ПҙЛ¶ П§ОҒ ЛіОҗ ЛҙП« ЛҙО« ЛёПҰ Л¶ПЈ Л¶ОҮ ОҚ Пӯ ЛөО® ЛөПҲ ЛёПЁ ЛҙПі ЛёПҘ ЛҙОғ Л¶ ЛҜОҺЛҙ ПіЛ¶ О®Лё О’Л¶ ПңЛё ПҹОҚ ЛөЛҜ ОҚ ЛҙО© Л¶Оӯ ОҺНү ПҹЛ¶ ОҮ НүПһ ЛҙОҹ ЛҙПӯ НүО° ЛҙПӢ ЛёПў Л¶П¬ НӢО‘ ЛҙОӯ П°Лҙ Пҹ ПҘЛё ОӘЛҙ ПӢ Л¶О” НүПЁ ЛҙОҹ ПІЛ¶ П“ Л¶ПӘ Л¶П¬ ЛёОҹ ЛҙПӯ П°Лҙ П Лҙ ПӢВ« (There are two gardens made of silver -- their ve ssels and all that they contain. And there are two gardens made of gold -- their vessels and all that they contain. And nothing stands between the people in the ' Adn Garden and looking a t their Lord, the Exalted and Most Honored, but the covering of pride before Hi s Face.) The Group, with the exception of Abu Dawud, collected this via the Hadi th of ' Abdul-' Aziz. This Ayah is general and applies to both humans and Jinns, providing proof that those among the Jinns who believe and have Taqwa will enter Paradise, for Allah is reminding the At h-Thaqalayn of this favor, as He says;

Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ОҳНү ПЁЛҙ Оҹ Л¶ПӘ НӢО‘ ЛҙОӯ ЛҙПЎ ОҺ ЛҙПҳ ЛҙПЈ ЛҙП‘ ОҺ ЛҙО§ ЛёПҰ ЛҙПӨ Л¶Пҹ ЛҙПӯ НҪ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— (But for him who fears the standing before his Lord, there will be two Gardens. Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) Th en He describes these two gardens, by saying,

ЛіПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПЁ ЛёП“ ЛҙОғ ОӮ ЛҙО— ОҚ ЛҙПӯ ЛҙО« НҪНј (With Afnan.) their trees have be autiful young branches that ho ld and produce every type of ripened beautiful fruit,

Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ НҪ (Then which of the blessings of your Lord wi ll you both deny) ' Ata' Al-Khurasani and several others said that Afnan means spreading branches of trees that reach the branches of other trees,

Нј Л¶ПҘ ОҺ ЛҙПі Л¶О® ЛёО  ЛҙО— Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ ПЁЛё ПҙЛҙ ПӢ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ П¬ПҙЛ¶ П“НҪ (In them (both) will be two springs flowing.) free t o water these trees and branches that produce all kinds of fruits,

Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ НҪ (Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) Al-Hasan Al-Basri said that one of these springs is called Tasnim, and the other calle d As-Salsabil. ' Atiyah said that the water of one of these springs is from non-standing wate r and the other from wine that gives delight to those who drink it. Allah's statement,

Нј Л¶ПҘ ОҺ ЛҙОҹ ЛёПӯ ЛҙОҜ ЛіО” ЛҙП¬ Л¶Пң Л° ЛҙП“ НӢПһ ЛөПӣ ПҰЛ¶ ПЈ ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛ¶ П¬ПҙЛ¶ П“НҪ (In them (both) will be every kind of fruit in pairs.), of every type and kind of frui t, that which they knew before, and better, and that which they did not know before. Therein, there are delights that no eye has ever seen, no ear ha s ever heard and no heart has ever imagined,

Нј Л¶ ПҘОҺЛҙ О‘НӢ О¬Лҙ ПңЛө О— ОҺЛҙ ПӨЛө ПңНӢ О‘Лҙ Оӯ Л¶ЛҜ П» ОҚ ЛҙЛҜ НӢПҜ ЛҙО„ Л¶О’ ЛҙП“ НҪ (Then which of the blessings of your Lord will you both deny) Ibrahim bin Al-Hakam bin Aban said that his father narrated from ' Ikrimah that Ibn ' Abbas said, "There is not a fruit that exists in this life, sweet or bitter, but it exis ts in Paradise, even the colo- cynth.'' Ibn ' Abbas also said, "There is nothing in the world that is in the Hereafter except in name.'' Meaning there is such an enormous difference and contrast between the two in enj oyment and value.
